title: Development of an Automatic Rule-Based Algorithm for the Detection of Ovarian Cancer Recurrence From Electronic Health Records
summary: Researchers developed Auto-Recur, an automatic rule-based algorithm that leverages electronic health records to detect ovarian cancer recurrence by integrating multiple data sources including imaging findings from PET-CT, CT, and MRI scans, biomarker levels (CA125), and treatment history (surgery, chemotherapy, radiotherapy). A hybrid algorithmic approach combining image analysis, biomarker data, and treatment information proved most effective for recurrence detection. The Auto-Recur system substantially reduced the manual burden of clinical review, saving approximately 1,340 days of human resources when implemented at scale. This artificial intelligence application demonstrates how structured extraction and rule-based analysis of diverse clinical data types from medical records can streamline cancer surveillance and improve the efficiency of recurrence monitoring in ovarian cancer patients.

title: Indications affect neonatal outcomes following early rescue ICSI: a retrospective study
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary: This retrospective study evaluated neonatal outcomes following early rescue intracytoplasmic sperm injection (rICSI), an assisted reproductive technique applied when fertilization fails during standard in vitro fertilization. While early rICSI proved safe for patients with total fertilization failure (TFF), the study revealed that expanding rICSI use to partial fertilization failure (PFF) cycles was associated with increased small for gestational age (SGA) outcomes, with relative risks of 1.56. A positive correlation was identified between fertilization rate achieved via IVF and improved birth weight z-scores in PFF patients, suggesting that baseline fertilization capacity influences neonatal outcomes. The differential impact of rICSI depending on the underlying indication—TFF versus PFF—highlights the importance of patient selection criteria when expanding fertility treatment protocols. These findings indicate that while rICSI may be appropriate for total fertilization failure, extending this technique to partial fertilization failure cases requires careful consideration of potential adverse neonatal consequences.

title: Improved Utilization of Serial Testing Without Increased Admissions after Implementation of High-Sensitivity Troponin I: a Controlled Retrospective Cohort Study
summary: This article examines the clinical impact of implementing high-sensitivity cardiac troponin I (hs-cTn) testing protocols in emergency department settings for myocardial infarction diagnosis. The hs-cTn intervention bundle was associated with a substantial 48 percentage point increase in serial troponin testing utilization, demonstrating improved diagnostic methodology adoption. Notably, while the intervention significantly reduced emergency department length of stay by approximately 50 minutes, it did not increase hospital admission rates, suggesting more efficient risk stratification. The findings also showed no significant increase in 72-hour ED revisits resulting in admission, indicating that the improved testing protocol did not compromise patient safety or lead to missed diagnoses. This represents an important clinical finding: implementing high-sensitivity troponin testing allows for faster diagnostic evaluation and patient disposition without inadvertently increasing unnecessary admissions or adverse outcomes. The results support the adoption of hs-cTn protocols as an effective strategy to optimize emergency care efficiency while maintaining diagnostic accuracy for acute myocardial infarction.

title: Autologous platelet‐rich plasma for assisted reproduction
journal: The Cochrane Database of Systematic Reviews
summary: Platelet-rich plasma (PRP), an autologous preparation extracted from whole blood containing concentrated growth factors, has been investigated as a potential treatment to enhance outcomes in assisted reproductive technology for infertile women. The growth factors present in PRP theoretically stimulate tissue repair and may improve endometrial receptivity and ovarian response to hormonal stimulation. However, this Cochrane systematic review found only very low-certainty evidence regarding PRP's effects on clinical pregnancy, live birth, and miscarriage rates when administered via intrauterine or intraovarian injection compared with no intervention or alternative treatments. A notable concern emerged regarding intrauterine PRP infusion, which showed a potentially increased risk of preterm delivery with medium certainty evidence. Overall, the current evidence base is insufficient to support routine clinical use of PRP in assisted reproduction, and additional high-quality randomized controlled trials are needed to establish both efficacy and safety profiles.

title: Measuring uterine cavity volume with sonohysterography: A new objective method
journal: Ultrasound: Journal of the British Medical Ultrasound Society
summary: This study introduces sonohysterography as an objective method for measuring uterine cavity volume with improved diagnostic accuracy compared to traditional approaches. The researchers established significant correlations between uterine cavity volume and key demographic factors, specifically patient age and parity, indicating these variables influence cavity dimensions. Sonohysterography demonstrated strong validity when compared against true volumetric measurements, while measurements using only a Foley catheter balloon also showed meaningful associations with actual cavity volumes. The technique appears particularly valuable for characterizing women with distinct uterine cavity morphologies, offering enhanced precision over conventional diagnostic methods. By providing standardized, quantitative assessment of uterine cavity dimensions, sonohysterography could facilitate more accurate diagnosis and individualized management planning in reproductive conditions where cavity morphology is clinically relevant.

title: Comparison of Simple Ultrasound Rules by International Ovarian Tumor Analysis (IOTA) with RMI-1 and RMI-4 (Risk of Malignancy Index) in Preoperative Differentiation of Benign and Malignant Adnexal Masses
journal: Journal of Obstetrics and Gynaecology of India
summary: This study compared the diagnostic accuracy of the International Ovarian Tumor Analysis (IOTA) Simple Ultrasound Rules with two Risk of Malignancy Index (RMI) scoring systems for preoperative differentiation of benign and malignant adnexal masses. The IOTA Simple Rules, which rely on ultrasound imaging alone without requiring additional biomarkers, demonstrated substantially superior performance compared to RMI-1 and RMI-4, which require CA125 serum level measurements for calculation. The IOTA Rules achieved sensitivity and specificity of 88.9% and 94.1% respectively for distinguishing malignant masses, significantly outperforming both RMI scoring systems in sensitivity. These findings support the clinical adoption of IOTA Simple Rules as a more accurate and practical approach for preoperative adnexal mass characterization, as they eliminate the need for biomarker testing while providing improved diagnostic discrimination between benign and malignant lesions.

title: MRI classification of uterosacral ligament involvement in endometriosis: the Hôtel-Dieu classification
summary: This study introduces the Hôtel-Dieu classification, the first MRI-based staging system for uterosacral ligament (USL) involvement in deep infiltrating endometriosis. The classification categorizes USL pathology into two main groups: linear types (L-category) with regular or irregular margins and hemorrhagic or nodular types (N-category), each associated with distinct presentations of deep infiltrating endometriosis. MRI imaging successfully identified these USL involvement categories in women with endometriosis symptoms, enabling non-invasive disease staging. However, the study revealed that MRI tends to underestimate USL involvement, particularly for type 1 lesions, suggesting diagnostic limitations despite the classification's utility. This imaging classification provides a structured approach to characterizing the morphological heterogeneity of endometrial disease at the USL, potentially improving clinical assessment and treatment planning in endometriosis patients.

title: Endometriosis and Disability: Analysis of Federal Court Appeals of Social Security Disability Insurance and Supplemental Security Income Claims by Individuals Suffering From Endometriosis
journal: Women's health issues : official publication of the Jacobs Institute of Women's Health
summary:
# Summary

Endometriosis causes disruptive physical and psychosocial symptoms and is associated with disability, yet individuals seeking Social Security Disability Insurance face substantial evidentiary obstacles in legal appeals. Courts require extensive medical evidence including diagnostic tests, surgeries, and physician visits linked directly to functional limitations, finding subjective symptom reports alone inadequate and treating physician testimony insufficient without supporting documentation. A critical barrier emerges from courts' treatment expectations: positive responses to medication or surgery are interpreted as cures that undercut disability claims, and hysterectomy is frequently viewed as definitive treatment despite evidence that symptoms often persist post-operatively. Courts also discount claimant credibility based on perceived inconsistencies such as normal physical exams, lack of acute distress, or conservative treatment use, while simultaneously penalizing gaps or delays in treatment and requiring continuous 12-month symptom duration—a threshold often interrupted by any documented treatment response. Additionally, the cyclic nature of endometriosis symptoms and variation in pain severity across functional abilities weaken claims, creating a paradox where claimants must demonstrate both continuous severe disability and appropriate medical engagement while avoiding any evidence of treatment benefit.

title: Acceptability of Gynecologic e-Health Modalities by Hispanic/Latina Women of Reproductive Age
summary:
# Summary

The COVID-19 pandemic significantly disrupted gynecological healthcare access among Hispanic/Latina women of reproductive age, with approximately 21% reporting difficulties obtaining medical attention due to office hour limitations, contact barriers, work conflicts, and infection fears. Endometriosis was the most common gynecological condition reported (43.4%), often managed through e-health modalities. Electronic health interventions, particularly telemedicine consultations and menstrual cycle tracking mobile apps, emerged as highly acceptable alternatives, with nearly 64% of participants already using period tracking apps primarily to monitor menstruation and ovulation. Acceptance of e-health modalities was significantly higher among younger women (under 29), students, those with lower incomes, and individuals without an established gynecologist, while higher education and advanced technological expertise correlated with telemedicine acceptance. Key features driving app adoption included calendar functions, symptom tracking capabilities, shareable reports, and integration with telemedicine consultations and wellness support. These findings suggest that strategically designed e-health platforms represent a promising approach to improve gynecological care equity and continuity of care for Hispanic/Latina women, particularly during public health emergencies and natural disasters.

title: Embryo development and live birth in women with one previously failed full IVF/ICSI cycle
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary: Women undergoing a second IVF/ICSI cycle following an initial failed attempt demonstrated substantially improved oocyte retrieval and embryo development outcomes compared to their first cycle, with median oocyte numbers increasing from 9 to 11, fertilized oocytes from 5 to 7, usable embryos from 4 to 6, and blastocysts from 1 to 3. Blastocyst formation rates notably improved from 33% to 50% in the second cycle. However, despite these enhanced embryological parameters, live birth rates paradoxically decreased from 51.82% in the initial cycle to 40.82% in the second cycle, suggesting that superior embryo quality does not guarantee improved reproductive outcomes. Maternal age emerged as a critical determinant of success in repeat cycles, with women under 35 and 35–37 years achieving comparable live birth rates around 42%, while women aged 38–40 years achieved 25.49% and those over 40 years only 16.22%. These findings indicate that while repeated IVF cycles improve gamete and embryo metrics, other factors beyond embryo morphology—potentially including endometrial receptivity or age-related physiological changes—may compromise implantation and clinical success in subsequent attempts.

title: Therapeutic Approaches for Urologic Chronic Pelvic Pain Syndrome; Management: Research Advances, Experimental Targets, and Future Directions
journal: The Journal of Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics
summary: Urologic chronic pelvic pain syndrome (UCPPS) involves multiple pathogenic mechanisms including abnormal pelvic nociception mediated by nerve growth factor and vascular endothelial growth factor signaling. Emerging therapeutic approaches targeting these growth factors, along with stem cell treatments and amniotic bladder therapy, show promise for managing UCPPS symptoms. Beyond biological interventions, cognitive behavioral therapy represents a potentially valuable psychological approach for improving patient outcomes. The field is advancing through development of novel urinary biomarkers for improved diagnostic accuracy and investigation of immune and autoimmune mechanisms, as well as the role of chronic psychologic stress in pain persistence. These multifaceted research directions highlight that effective UCPPS management will likely require integrated approaches addressing neurobiological, immunological, and psychological dimensions of the disease.

title: A prospective study on risk factors associated with the development of isthmocele after caesarean section
summary: This prospective study identified multiple risk factors for isthmocele (uterine niche) development following caesarean delivery, including the number of previous caesarean sections, maternal BMI, surgical duration, and characteristics of the previous caesarean scar. Women with multiple prior caesarean deliveries, elevated body mass index, and longer operative times demonstrated significantly higher isthmocele incidence. The research demonstrated that vaginal birth after caesarean delivery, management of maternal obesity, and adequate surgical training can effectively prevent isthmocele development. For diagnostic purposes, both transvaginal ultrasonography (TVS) and saline infusion sonography (SIS) proved effective for detecting isthmoceles, with comparable diagnostic accuracy and similar limits of agreement for clinically important measurements. These findings emphasize that isthmocele prevention requires a multifaceted approach addressing modifiable risk factors during and after caesarean delivery procedures.

title: Live birth after vitrification of oocytes from capacitation in vitro maturation
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary: This study demonstrates that capacitation in vitro maturation (CAPA-IVM), a refined protocol for oocyte development, significantly improves oocyte competence and enables successful fertility preservation through vitrification. The research establishes that vitrified-warmed oocytes derived from CAPA-IVM can be effectively used in assisted reproductive techniques, culminating in live birth outcomes. These findings underscore the clinical potential of combining advanced in vitro maturation protocols with oocyte cryopreservation technology to expand fertility preservation options for women. The integration of CAPA-IVM with oocyte vitrification represents a meaningful advancement in reproductive medicine, offering improved accessibility and efficacy for female fertility preservation. This approach holds particular promise for expanding the application of fertility preservation to broader patient populations requiring reproductive options.

title: Characteristics and predictors of pain among women who underwent cesarean section in Fiji
summary: This study examined pain outcomes in 312 Fijian women following cesarean delivery, identifying several key predictors and clinical consequences of post-operative pain. Lower parity and first-time motherhood emerged as significant risk factors for increased post-cesarean pain, suggesting that maternal experience plays an important role in pain perception following this procedure. The research evaluated pain levels across three anesthesia types—spinal, epidural, and general anesthesia—though specific comparative findings were not detailed in the extracted data. The clinical impact of inadequate pain management was substantial, with over 70% of women experiencing difficulties performing daily activities and approximately 41% expressing dissatisfaction with their pain management protocols. These findings highlight the need for improved pain management strategies tailored to nulliparous and primiparous women undergoing cesarean delivery. The study underscores the importance of addressing post-operative pain control to enhance functional recovery and patient satisfaction in the cesarean delivery population.

title: Radiomics and quantitative multi-parametric MRI for predicting uterine fibroid growth
summary: Uterine fibroids are benign smooth muscle tumors that cause significant clinical morbidity through pelvic pain, abnormal bleeding, and reproductive dysfunction. This study demonstrates that multiparametric MRI combined with radiomics analysis can effectively predict fibroid growth rates by identifying specific imaging biomarkers associated with faster-growing lesions, including heterogeneous contrast-agent uptake on dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI, elevated SumAverage and reduced IMC2 features on T2 and ADC maps respectively, and specific morphological characteristics like perimeter length and fibroid extent. Machine learning approaches utilizing principal component analysis with linear discriminant analysis successfully integrated quantitative MRI features—particularly T2 mapping and apparent diffusion coefficient mapping—to generate a radiomics risk score with demonstrated clinical utility for stratifying growth risk. Qualitative imaging features such as an enhancing rim on ADC maps further distinguished slower-growing fibroids, providing complementary diagnostic value. These quantitative radiomics models show promise for enhancing prognostic assessment, guiding treatment selection, predicting ablation efficacy, and identifying patients at risk for postoperative reintervention. The integration of specialized quantitative MR sequences with advanced analytical methods represents a significant advance in personalizing fibroid management based on imaging-derived biological signatures.

title: Pain as a disease in the new International Classification of Diseases (ICD-11): Latin American expert consensus
summary: The International Classification of Diseases 11th revision (ICD-11) introduces a significant paradigm shift in chronic pain management by recognizing pain as a disease entity with biopsychosocial dimensions rather than solely a symptom of underlying pathology. Chronic pain represents one of the most frequent reasons for medical consultation throughout Latin America, underscoring the clinical urgency of improved classification and management strategies. The new ICD-11 framework is expected to enhance chronic pain management outcomes for both pain specialists and primary care providers by providing a standardized, comprehensive definition that acknowledges the multifactorial nature of chronic pain conditions. This revised classification system provides tangible benefits for medical care delivery, clinical research, and health policy implementation across Latin American healthcare systems. By establishing a unified conceptual framework, ICD-11 aims to reduce diagnostic variability and improve clinical consistency in managing chronic pain conditions in resource-constrained settings. The recognition of chronic pain as a distinct disease entity rather than a secondary symptom represents a foundational shift that should facilitate more targeted and effective therapeutic interventions.

title: Immunohistochemistry Markers in Ovarian and Fallopian Tube Neoplasms: a Comprehensive Review
summary: Immunohistochemistry (IHC) has become an essential diagnostic and prognostic tool in ovarian and fallopian tube neoplasm management, enabling accurate tumor characterization and subtype classification. By targeting specific biomarkers through antibody panels, IHC identifies diverse histological features and molecular alterations that would otherwise remain undetected, thereby enhancing diagnostic accuracy in routine clinical workflows. These immunological markers provide critical insights into tumor biology while simultaneously guiding prognostication and predicting individual treatment responses. The integration of IHC into standard diagnostic protocols facilitates precise tumor subclassification and supports the development of personalized treatment strategies tailored to each patient's specific molecular profile. Collectively, IHC-based biomarker analysis represents a complementary approach that bridges morphological assessment and molecular characterization, improving both the accuracy of ovarian cancer diagnosis and the precision of therapeutic decision-making.

title: Maternal Placental Growth Factor (PlGF) levels, sonographic placental parameters, and outcomes of IVF pregnancies with and without embryo trophectoderm biopsy
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary:
# Summary

In vitro fertilization is associated with abnormal trophoblast invasion, which leads to decreased circulating placental growth factor (PlGF) levels in maternal serum. This study found that frozen embryo transfer procedures involving trophectoderm biopsy were associated with reduced maternal PlGF levels compared to non-biopsied cycles, with median PlGF levels of 614.5 pg/mL versus 717.0 pg/mL respectively. The trophectoderm biopsy procedure itself appears to contribute to lower maternal serum PlGF, suggesting that embryo sampling may further compromise placental development and function in IVF pregnancies. These findings indicate that the mechanical disruption of the trophectoderm during genetic testing may exacerbate the already impaired placentation associated with assisted reproductive technologies, potentially affecting downstream pregnancy outcomes through reduced angiogenic factor signaling.

title: Personalized embryo transfer guided by rsERT with hourly precision improves pregnancy outcomes in patients with a receptive window of implantation: a pilot study
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary: This pilot study demonstrates that personalized embryo transfer (pET) guided by a modified RNA-sequencing-based endometrial receptivity (rsERT) model significantly improves assisted reproduction outcomes in patients with identifiable receptive windows of implantation. The rsERT model predicts optimal transfer timing with hourly precision, enabling synchronized embryo-endometrium interaction during the receptive phase. Patients receiving receptivity-timed pET achieved substantially higher intrauterine pregnancy rates (57.38% versus 44.81%) and implantation rates (46.81% versus 33.10%) compared to conventional embryo transfer, even after adjustment for confounding factors. The pET approach also reduced implantation failure rates by 13 percentage points and increased multiple-pregnancy rates, reflecting enhanced embryo viability and uterine receptivity synchronization. These findings suggest that transcriptomic profiling-guided personalized timing represents a promising AI-assisted strategy to optimize pregnancy outcomes in assisted reproductive technology, particularly for patients with defined receptivity windows during the implantation window.

title: Potential damage to ovarian reserve from laparoscopic electrocoagulation in endometriomas and benign ovarian cysts: a systematic review and meta-analysis
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary: This systematic review and meta-analysis compared the effects of different hemostatic techniques during laparoscopic cystectomy for treating ovarian endometriomas and benign ovarian cysts on patient ovarian reserve. Bipolar electrocoagulation, a commonly used thermal hemostatic method, was found to significantly reduce markers of ovarian reserve, including anti-Müllerian hormone levels and antral follicle counts, compared with nonthermal hemostatic approaches. In contrast, nonthermal hemostatic methods demonstrated superior preservation of ovarian reserve and were associated with significantly higher postoperative anti-Müllerian hormone levels. These findings suggest that while laparoscopic cystectomy effectively treats both endometriomas and benign ovarian cysts, the choice of hemostatic technique substantially impacts long-term ovarian function. The data indicate that nonthermal hemostatic methods should be preferred during these procedures to minimize iatrogenic damage to ovarian reserve, particularly in patients for whom fertility preservation is a clinical priority.

title: Adamts9 is required for the development of primary ovarian follicles and maintenance of female sex in zebrafish†
summary: This study demonstrates that adamts9, an extracellular matrix metalloprotease, is essential for female fertility and sexual differentiation in zebrafish. Loss of adamts9 causes delayed primordial germ cell proliferation and arrest of ovarian follicles at the primary (Stage IB) stage, resulting in female infertility and progressive female-to-male sex conversion in late juveniles and adults. The adamts9 knockout phenotype involves dysregulation of extracellular matrix organization genes during ovarian sexual maturation and inflammatory transcriptomic signatures in adult ovaries. While growth defects in female adamts9 knockout fish could be partially rescued through overfeeding and reduced rearing density, ovarian developmental defects persisted, indicating a specific requirement for adamts9 in follicle development independent of general nutritional status. These findings establish the extracellular matrix and its proteolytic remodeling as critical regulators of ovarian follicle development and sex maintenance, with potential implications for understanding reproductive dysfunction mechanisms.

title: Modeling delay of age at natural menopause with planned tissue cryopreservation and autologous transplantation
journal: American journal of obstetrics and gynecology
summary: This mathematical modeling study demonstrates that planned ovarian cortex cryopreservation and autologous transplantation can substantially delay natural menopause by preserving primordial follicles that would otherwise be lost through atresia. The menopause delay achieved depends on multiple interdependent factors: the age at tissue harvest, the amount of cortex removed, post-transplant follicle survival rates, and the number of staged transplantation procedures. Fractionating cryopreserved tissue into multiple transplants maximizes menopause delay—for example, tissue harvested at age 25 with 80% follicle survival delivered in six separate procedures could delay menopause by up to 47 years, though more conservative scenarios predict 12-15 years of delay. The strategy should be individualized based on ovarian reserve status (measured by anti-Müllerian hormone levels), with women possessing larger primordial follicle reserves benefiting most from preserving greater tissue amounts. Beyond fertility preservation for cancer patients, this approach offers potential cardiovascular and bone health benefits through extended estrogen exposure, though balancing risks such as increased breast cancer incidence with these benefits requires careful patient counseling and timing optimization.

title: Effect of follicle-stimulating hormone dose on the risk of being classified as suboptimal responders according to the POSEIDON criteria
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary: This study examined factors influencing suboptimal ovarian response during assisted reproduction, classified according to POSEIDON criteria. Contrary to expectations, FSH dose alone was not independently associated with suboptimal response risk in multivariable analysis; however, FSH doses exceeding 300 IU daily showed a protective effect compared to lower doses. Female age, anti-Müllerian hormone (AMH) levels, antral follicle count (AFC), and gonadotropin type emerged as the primary determinants of suboptimal response classification. The findings suggest that individualized gonadotropin protocols incorporating LH activity supplementation alongside FSH may help mitigate suboptimal response risk in vulnerable patient populations. These results support a personalized approach to ovarian stimulation that considers baseline ovarian reserve markers rather than relying solely on FSH dosing adjustments.

title: Human oocyte zona pellucida abnormalities: evaluation of clinical impact for different zona pellucida abnormalities and role of using assisted hatching
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary:
# Summary

The zona pellucida is critical for oocyte development, fertilization, and early embryo progression, but abnormalities in this structure significantly compromise reproductive outcomes. Patients with zona pellucida abnormalities (ZPA) demonstrate altered hormone levels and substantially reduced laboratory outcomes in in vitro fertilization, including decreased metaphase II oocyte rates, cleavage rates, high-quality embryo rates, and blastocyst formation rates. ZPA type influences both sperm binding capacity and oocyte membrane elasticity, with different ZPA phenotypes correlating with varying degrees of clinical impact. Laser-assisted zona pellucida hatching improves embryonic development in ZPA patients, with timing-dependent efficacy: hatching on day one benefits patients with mild abnormalities (types A/B), while day four hatching is more effective for severe abnormalities (types C/D). These findings demonstrate that ZPA classification is essential for predicting clinical outcomes and optimizing assisted reproductive technology interventions through appropriately timed assisted hatching procedures.

title: Artificial intelligence in gynecologic and obstetric emergencies
summary: Artificial intelligence demonstrates significant clinical utility in gynecologic and obstetric emergencies, particularly for endometriosis diagnosis and acute abdominal pain management. AI-powered algorithms, including fuzzy C-means and hard C-means machine learning approaches, substantially improve diagnostic accuracy for ovarian endometriosis on MRI imaging, achieving diagnostic rates of 94.3% compared to 63.2% with conventional imaging alone. Beyond diagnosis, AI reduces unnecessary surgical interventions by resolving diagnostic uncertainties in pelvic pain cases and decreases intraoperative morbidity through improved preoperative planning, resulting in reduced surgical duration and blood loss. The technology enhances acute abdominal pain triage and postoperative complication prediction while overcoming human cognitive biases through objective decision-making and identification of complex, nonlinear interactions among risk factors. AI-based tools also support multiple clinical applications including cesarean delivery detection, fetal monitoring, and endometrial cancer screening, with robotic surgical assistance further reducing postoperative morbidity and recovery time. Overall, AI integration into emergency gynecologic and obstetric care improves diagnostic accuracy, optimizes surgical outcomes, and enhances clinical decision-making across perioperative periods.

title: The effect of coenzyme Q10 pretreatment on ovarian reserve in women undergoing hysterectomy with bilateral salpingectomy: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
summary:
# Summary

Hysterectomy with bilateral salpingectomy, commonly performed for conditions such as leiomyoma and adenomyosis, significantly diminishes ovarian reserve by disrupting ovarian blood supply and increasing oxidative stress, resulting in decreased anti-Müllerian hormone (AMH) levels and elevated follicle-stimulating hormone postoperatively. Coenzyme Q10, a lipid-soluble antioxidant localized in inner mitochondria, improves oocyte quality and quantity through enhanced mitochondrial energy production and can rescue ovarian follicles from apoptosis by reversing oxidative stress and increasing developing follicles in preclinical studies. Despite the theoretical benefit of CoQ10 pretreatment in improving ovarian response and embryo quality in fertility contexts, this randomized controlled trial found that two weeks of preoperative CoQ10 supplementation failed to prevent the significant decline in AMH levels observed six weeks after surgery compared to placebo. The study confirmed that hysterectomy with bilateral salpingectomy causes substantial ovarian reserve decline, though CoQ10 pretreatment did not significantly protect against these changes, possibly due to insufficient treatment duration or the need for longer preoperative preparation. CoQ10 demonstrated excellent safety with no adverse effects or complications in users, suggesting that while it shows promise in other reproductive contexts, optimal dosing and duration for protecting ovarian reserves during gynecological surgery remains undetermined.

title: Effects of Bacillus amyloliquefaciens FD777 and Macleaya cordata Extract on Performance, Immunity, Gastrointestinal System Microbiome, and Profitability in Holstein Calves
journal: Animals : an Open Access Journal from MDPI
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluated the effects of Bacillus amyloliquefaciens FD777 (BA) and Macleaya cordata extract (MCE) supplementation on Holstein calf health and production outcomes. Both the probiotic and plant extract significantly improved overall calf health, reducing morbidity and mortality rates while increasing body weight gain and various growth metrics including body length, depth, and rump width. The probiotic BA treatment was particularly effective, producing zero mortality compared to 11.8% in controls, while also increasing serum glutathione levels and enhancing antioxidant capacity. Economically, both supplements improved profitability, with BA supplementation reducing treatment costs and total rearing expenses while generating USD 444.2 more profit per calf than controls. MCE provided antimicrobial, antioxidative, and anti-inflammatory benefits partly through alkaloid compounds and by reducing rumen ammonia concentration while increasing microbial protein production. These results demonstrate that strategic use of probiotics and plant-derived supplements can substantially enhance calf health, growth performance, and farm profitability.

title: The Effect of Wearing Elastic Compression Stockings on Leg Edema in Pregnant Women in Late Pregnancy as Determined by Measuring the Deep Venous Velocity and Flow
summary:
# Summary

Late pregnancy causes leg edema in approximately 80% of pregnancies through multiple mechanisms: hormonal changes increase vascular permeability, the enlarged uterus compresses the inferior vena cava leading to venous hypertension and increased plasma volume, and lymphatic obstruction combined with reduced plasma colloid oncotic pressure impairs fluid reabsorption. These hemodynamic changes result in significantly reduced popliteal venous velocity and blood flow in pregnant women at 36 gestational weeks, with further reductions in edematous legs compared to non-edematous legs. Pregnancy also induces a prothrombotic state characterized by elevated fibrin generation, increased coagulation factors II, VII, VIII, and X, and decreased fibrinolytic activity, creating additional thrombotic risk. Importantly, leg edema itself is associated with higher deep vein thrombosis (DVT) risk, as edematous legs show slower venous flow and increased subcutaneous thickness. Elastic compression stockings effectively mitigate these complications by increasing popliteal venous velocity, blood flow, and flow volume, thereby improving edema, reducing leg pain, and preventing venous reflux and hypertension. This intervention represents a clinically significant approach to reducing DVT development in pregnant women with lower limb edema during late pregnancy.

title: Visual-Numeric Endometriosis Scoring System (VNESS) for mapping surgical findings: A validation study
summary: The Visual-Numeric Endometriosis Scoring System (VNESS) is a novel classification tool designed to standardize the recording and communication of surgical findings across twelve pelvic anatomical compartments, from the adnexa and pelvic sidewalls to the rectum and pouch of Douglas. The system uses a numeric scale (0-4) where VNESS 1 represents superficial disease and VNESS 4 represents full-thickness invasion, with provision for documenting adenomyosis and extrapelvic findings. Validation studies demonstrate that VNESS achieves excellent inter-observer reproducibility with particularly high agreement for severe disease (97% for VNESS 4) compared to superficial disease (53% for VNESS 1), reflecting the challenge of distinguishing minimal lesions without tactile feedback during video-based assessment. The system shows stronger correlation with disease severity and surgical complexity than some existing classification methods, though superficial endometriosis remains more difficult to classify consistently. VNESS offers advantages over traditional systems like rASRM and ENZIAN by providing a simplified, visually intuitive representation of disease distribution and severity that is readily understood by both patients and clinicians, thereby improving communication regarding surgical complexity and treatment planning.

title: Diagnostic value of multimodal hysterosalpingo-contrast sonography combined with negative intrauterine contrast-enhanced ultrasound in female infertility
summary:
# Summary

Multimodal hysterosalpingo-contrast sonography (HyCoSy) combined with negative hysterography represents a highly effective non-invasive diagnostic approach for assessing female infertility by evaluating tubal patency and detecting uterine lesions. The integration of 4D, 3D, and 2D ultrasound modes with harmonic imaging significantly improved diagnostic success rates, with 4D mode achieving 86.7% success and the combined approach reaching over 91% accuracy. Negative hysterography demonstrated exceptional diagnostic performance for uterine malformations (100% sensitivity and specificity), cesarean scar diverticulum (98.39% accuracy), endometrial polyps (92.59% sensitivity), and uterine adhesions (88.89% sensitivity), showing excellent agreement with hysteroscopy findings. The multimodal technique proved operator-independent, enabling both experienced and less-experienced sonographers to achieve equivalent diagnostic efficiency in evaluating fallopian tube patency and uterine cavity lesions. These findings establish multimodal HyCoSy combined with negative hysterography as a simple, rapid, and accurate examination method suitable for comprehensive infertility evaluation, potentially reducing the need for more invasive procedures like hysteroscopy or laparoscopy.

title: Could digital twins be the next revolution in healthcare?
journal: The European Journal of Public Health
summary: Digital twins represent an emerging healthcare technology that integrates artificial intelligence, machine learning, Internet of Things, and big data analysis to create patient-specific virtual models incorporating human physiology, immunology, and real-time patient data. In women's health, digital twins are specifically applied to monitor and manage endometriosis alongside other gynecological conditions like fibroids, enabling personalized treatment strategies and remote monitoring. The technology shows high potential across multiple clinical domains, including predictive disease analysis, surgical simulation, treatment optimization, and improved diagnostic assertiveness, while simultaneously reducing patient waiting times and optimizing resource allocation in healthcare systems. Medical education, trauma management, and chronic disease monitoring all benefit from digital twin applications, which accelerate clinical trials and drug development through detailed molecular and pharmacological simulations. However, significant implementation challenges persist, particularly regarding data integration, privacy protection, and interoperability across healthcare systems, requiring safeguards such as blockchain encryption and biometric authentication. Overall, digital twins promise to transform precision medicine by enabling early disease detection, enhancing patient empowerment, and shifting healthcare delivery from standardized protocols toward individualized, data-driven interventions.

title: Radiomics model building from multiparametric MRI to predict Ki-67 expression in patients with primary central nervous system lymphomas: a multicenter study
summary:
# Summary

This multicenter study developed a radiomics model using multiparametric MRI to noninvasively predict Ki-67 expression and proliferative activity in primary central nervous system lymphomas (PCNSL). Quantitative parameters from apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC), diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI), and T1 contrast-enhanced (T1-CE) sequences each showed significant correlations with Ki-67 expression, with rT1-CEmean demonstrating the strongest association. A combined radiomics model integrating all three MRI sequences achieved an area under the curve of 0.869 in distinguishing high from low proliferation tumors, outperforming models using only ADC and DWI. Since high Ki-67 expression (≥90%) is associated with poorer overall and progression-free survival in PCNSL patients, the model's ability to predict proliferation status noninvasively could enable better treatment stratification and prognostic assessment, potentially avoiding invasive stereotactic biopsy in some cases.

title: The PRECISION study protocol: Can cervical stiffness in the second trimester predict preterm birth in high-risk singleton pregnancies? A feasibility, cohort study
summary:
# Summary

Preterm birth remains a leading cause of neonatal morbidity and mortality, yet current screening tools like cervical length ultrasound have limited predictive capacity for spontaneous preterm birth. The PRECISION study evaluates cervical stiffness as a novel biomarker for preterm birth risk, using the Pregnolia System to objectively measure cervical biomechanical properties in high-risk pregnancies during the second trimester. Research indicates that cervical softening precedes cervical shortening, and the Pregnolia System can detect this softening earlier than traditional cervical length measurements, potentially enabling earlier intervention in at-risk populations. Lower cervical stiffness is associated with spontaneous preterm birth, suggesting that direct assessment of cervical consistency may improve upon existing screening approaches. By comparing cervical stiffness measurement against the current gold standard of serial cervical length surveillance, this feasibility study aims to identify a more effective screening tool that could allow for individualized, timely prevention strategies in women at highest risk of preterm delivery.

title: Mucosal washes are useful for sampling intestinal mucus-associated microbiota despite low biomass
summary:
# Summary

The intestinal mucus-associated microbiota plays a critical role in host health and disease, with mucus produced by goblet cells forming a protective barrier whose composition directly influences the establishment and maintenance of these microbial communities. This study demonstrates that mucosal washes and brushes represent superior sampling methods for studying intestinal microbiota compared to traditional biopsies or scrapings, as they substantially reduce host DNA contamination while maintaining higher bacterial content and comparable microbial diversity metrics. Despite lower overall DNA concentrations, mucosal washes and brushes yield bacterial compositions with less host bias, making them particularly valuable for analyzing low-biomass samples where contamination has historically posed technical challenges. The sampling approach itself significantly affects microbiota composition measurements in humans, with mucosal washes producing distinct bacterial profiles compared to biopsies, yet the method consistently captures ecologically relevant mucus-associated communities with greater fidelity to host-microbiome interactions. These findings suggest that standardizing mucosal wash and brush sampling protocols could improve the transferability and comparability of microbiome research findings across studies and host species, ultimately providing better insights into how intestinal dysbiosis contributes to inflammatory and chronic diseases.

title: Recent Advances in SAW Sensors for Detection of Cancer Biomarkers
summary: Surface acoustic wave (SAW) sensors represent a promising technology for detecting cancer biomarkers through their ability to identify volatile organic compounds, proteins, DNA, microRNAs, and circulating tumor cells in body fluids. The most widely used biomarkers in clinical practice include PSA, CEA, and CA-125/MUC16, while emerging biomarkers such as exosomes, microRNAs, and circulating tumor cells are gaining recognition for their diagnostic potential. Cancer-associated volatile organic compounds—including alkanes, aldehydes, ketones, and aromatic hydrocarbons—are linked to oxidative stress, lipid peroxidation, and disrupted cellular metabolism, making them valuable indicators of tumor development. Love-mode SAW devices are the most common configuration for biomarker cancer detection, with design optimization achieved through piezoelectric materials like lithium niobate and quartz, along with interdigital transducer configurations that enhance sensitivity. Sensor performance is further improved through functionalization with antibodies, aptamers, DNA probes, and advanced materials such as graphene and carbon nanotubes, which enhance both specificity and sensitivity. These technological advances position SAW sensors as a versatile platform for non-invasive cancer diagnosis across multiple cancer types including lung, colorectal, prostate, breast, and ovarian cancers.

title: Assessing the Relationships of Expenditure and Health Outcomes in Healthcare Systems: A System Design Approach
summary: This healthcare systems analysis demonstrates that increased health expenditure significantly improves population health outcomes across multiple metrics. Higher healthcare spending—whether at national, household, or government levels—consistently associates with increased life expectancy, reduced mortality rates (including infant mortality), and improved healthy life years, with strategic allocation customized to specific population needs being essential for maximizing these benefits. Robust funding mechanisms and publicly funded healthcare systems reduce access inequalities and provide more consistent support to vulnerable populations, while preventive programs and enhanced access to vaccinations and prenatal care yield substantial additional health gains. Conversely, austerity policies and underfunding of essential services exacerbate existing care disparities and health inequalities. The research underscores that balancing fiscal discipline with sufficient healthcare funding, combined with well-managed strategic investments in healthcare infrastructure and personnel, optimizes population health outcomes while reducing the catastrophic out-of-pocket financial burdens that can drive households into poverty.

title: Obstetrics and Gynecology Medicine: Go from Bench to Bedside
summary:
# Summary

This comprehensive review examines how bench-to-bedside translational research and artificial intelligence are advancing obstetrics and gynecology, with particular emphasis on endometriosis and related reproductive disorders. Key molecular findings include the identification of endothelial dysfunction-related genes—particularly NOS3 and SELP—as significant predictors of venous thromboembolism risk and pregnancy outcomes, while genetic variants like SELP rs6136 T allele are associated with reduced progression-free survival. Multiple risk factors for pregnancy loss were identified, including diabetes mellitus, advanced maternal age, gynecological disorders, and inflammatory conditions, underscoring the complex interplay of genetic, epigenetic, and environmental factors in disease progression. The review highlights emerging AI applications transforming clinical practice, including machine learning models for predicting IVF treatment success, AI-powered detection of fetal anomalies with up to 90% accuracy, and personalized medicine approaches based on genetic profiling that improve treatment efficacy for gynecological cancers. Additionally, the evidence supporting COVID-19 vaccination during pregnancy to enhance placental antioxidant defenses and reduce adverse pregnancy outcomes demonstrates how translational research informs clinical recommendations, establishing a framework for implementing technological advances to improve early detection, diagnosis, and individualized treatment strategies across obstetrics and gynecology.

title: The Medical Basis for the Photoluminescence of Indocyanine Green
summary:
# Summary

Indocyanine green (ICG) is a fluorescent dye that absorbs near-infrared light at approximately 780 nm and emits fluorescence in the 800–850 nm range, with its optical properties enhanced by conjugated double-bond systems within its polymethine chain structure. The compound is rapidly absorbed into the bloodstream following intravenous injection, processed and primarily excreted by the liver with minor renal elimination, and exhibits a low toxicity profile with rare adverse effects. ICG's clinical applications span diagnostic and therapeutic domains, including fluorescence-guided surgery for precise tumor excision, blood flow visualization, sentinel lymph node mapping, and emerging photothermal and photodynamic therapies, with NIR penetration enabling deeper tissue imaging while reducing background autofluorescence. Recent advances involve encapsulating ICG within nanoparticles to enhance fluorescence stability, protect against photobleaching, enable targeted delivery to specific cells or tumors, and improve visualization of tumor vasculature and tissue perfusion. Nanoparticle-based ICG formulations represent a significant innovation, allowing for more precise localization to tumor sites in cancer imaging and treatment while reducing collateral damage to healthy tissues and enabling photothermal therapy-induced release of reactive oxygen species for cell killing.

title: Inadequate Reporting of Harm From Randomized Clinical Trials in Top Medical Publications
summary:
# Summary

This article reveals critical deficiencies in how randomized controlled trials report drug-related harms despite their methodological advantages in distinguishing treatment effects from natural disease progression. Randomized controlled trials generate robust harm-related data through controlled designs and placebo comparisons that establish causal relationships between drugs and specific adverse events, yet the review found that nearly half of studies employed frequency thresholds that excluded minor harms and 87% relied solely on descriptive statistics rather than appropriate inferential methods. The standardization of adverse event coding through MedDRA and CTCAE classifications has improved data homogeneity, enabling pooling across studies for meta-analyses; however, selective reporting practices, dichotomization of continuous outcomes, and inadequate statistical power substantially reduce the ability to synthesize and correctly interpret safety signals. The authors highlight that implementation of the updated CONSORT Harms 2022 guidelines—which provides standardized guidance on minimum reporting items and statistical approaches—is essential to ensure that benefit-risk analyses capture clinically meaningful harms across diverse patient populations.

title: Prognosis of extracolonic findings on clinical computed tomographic colonography: A single-center experience
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluates the prognostic significance of extracolonic findings detected on computed tomographic colonography (CTC) using a revised classification system. CTC proved valuable not only for colorectal cancer screening but also for detecting extracolonic lesions including gastric cancer, gynecological conditions, renal carcinoma, and abdominal aortic aneurysms, with findings classified into E1-E4 categories based on clinical significance. E4 findings, associated with malignant lesions, lymph node metastases, and peritoneal dissemination, carried substantially worse prognosis (mean survival 6.2 years) compared to E1-E3 combined findings (10.0 years), and were crucial for treatment planning and guiding clinical decision-making. E2 and E3 findings showed limited impact on treatment modifications, while the revised E classification system demonstrated high concordance (0.83) with initial clinical assessment and more intuitively reflected survival differences. The study demonstrates that CTC-detected extracolonic findings, particularly E4 category results, provide essential prognostic information that significantly influences treatment strategies and patient outcomes in a substantial proportion of patients undergoing CTC examination.

title: Long-acting parenteral formulations of hydrophilic drugs, proteins, and peptide therapeutics: mechanisms, challenges, and therapeutic benefits with a focus on technologies
summary: Long-acting injectable formulations represent a significant advancement in drug delivery technology, particularly for hydrophilic drugs, proteins, and peptides that are otherwise susceptible to enzymatic degradation in the gastrointestinal tract. These formulations improve patient adherence and compliance by reducing dosing frequency while minimizing toxicity and avoiding hepatic first-pass metabolism, making them suitable for treating chronic diseases including cancer, neurological, metabolic, cardiovascular, and mental disorders. Key delivery systems such as polymeric microspheres, multivesicular liposomes, and in-situ forming implants achieve sustained drug release through strategic modifications of formulation components—including fatty acyl chain length in prodrugs, oil viscosity in depot systems, and cholesterol content in lipid bilayers—each of which can be optimized to control pharmacokinetic parameters like peak concentration and time to peak. Clinical examples demonstrate the therapeutic efficacy of these technologies across diverse applications, including buserelin acetate for endometriosis, leuprolide acetate for cancer, and exenatide for diabetes mellitus, while maintaining structural stability of protein therapeutics such as interferon α-2b. The choice between vegetable and synthetic oils, along with careful engineering of microsphere composition and liposome structure, addresses critical challenges of physical and chemical instability, enabling extended therapeutic benefits and prolonged drug release periods ranging from weeks to months.

title: Ovarian function after COVID-19: long-term effects and vaccine safety in ART patients
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary: COVID-19 infection produces transient but measurable effects on ovarian function in assisted reproductive technology (ART) patients, particularly reducing the number of retrieved and mature oocytes in women over 36 years of age. The mechanism appears to involve alterations in follicular fluid composition, including reduced interleukin-1β levels and temporarily depressed vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF), which collectively impair endothelial cell migration and angiogenic capacity. However, these effects are reversible, with VEGF levels and DNA integrity normalizing between 9 and 18 months post-infection. In contrast, COVID-19 vaccination demonstrates excellent safety for reproductive outcomes, with no significant differences in retrieved or mature oocytes or overall ovarian response observed between vaccinated and unvaccinated ART patients. These findings suggest that while acute COVID-19 may transiently compromise oocyte yield, vaccination poses no reproductive risk and supports its continued use in patients planning assisted reproduction.

title: Intra‐ and interobserver agreement of proposed objective transvaginal ultrasound image‐quality scoring system for use in artificial intelligence algorithm development
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluated an objective transvaginal ultrasound image-quality scoring system designed to standardize data preparation for artificial intelligence algorithm development in gynecological imaging. The researchers found that interobserver agreement varied significantly by image type and rater expertise, with poor agreement between sonographers and sonologists (ICC 0.336) but moderate agreement among radiologists (ICC 0.600), while uterine images showed lower interobserver but slightly higher intraobserver agreement than ovarian images. The subjective nature of ultrasound interpretation and inadequate system definition contributed to inconsistent ratings, though objective scoring systems demonstrated better agreement than purely subjective assessment. These findings highlight a critical bottleneck: AI algorithms require high-quality, consistently labeled training data, yet human labeling of ultrasound images inherently introduces noise and inconsistency, particularly across different professional groups. The authors conclude that reliable, objective image-quality scoring methods are essential for advancing AI in gynecological ultrasound, and recommend developing AI approaches capable of accommodating the noisy labeling that will inevitably result from human expert review.

title: Application of artificial intelligence to ultrasound imaging for benign gynecological disorders: systematic review
summary:
# Summary

This systematic review examines the application of artificial intelligence, particularly deep learning and machine learning models, to ultrasound imaging for diagnosing and managing benign gynecological disorders. Ultrasound serves as the first-line imaging modality for gynecological diseases, and AI techniques including convolutional neural networks, radiomics, and hybrid approaches enhance its diagnostic capability through automated detection, classification, and segmentation of pathological structures. Across 59 studies spanning multiple conditions, AI models demonstrated high performance in distinguishing endometriosis and endometriomas from other adnexal lesions, classifying polycystic ovary syndrome, discriminating ovarian cyst types, and identifying adnexal torsion, with some models achieving area under the curve values exceeding 0.99. Deep learning models were employed in 55.9% of studies and machine learning models in 37.3%, with applications extending to segmentation of ovarian follicles, measurement of ovarian volume, and prediction of hyper-response during assisted reproductive technology cycles. These findings indicate that AI-integrated ultrasound imaging has substantial potential to improve diagnostic accuracy and clinical decision-making in gynecological practice, though systematic validation and standardization of these models remain important future directions.

title: Performance of algorithms using wrist temperature for retrospective ovulation day estimate and next menses start day prediction: a prospective cohort study
summary:
# Summary

This prospective cohort study demonstrates that wrist skin temperature measured by wearable devices can effectively track menstrual cycle events through algorithms that leverage progesterone-induced luteal phase temperature elevations following ovulation. Three algorithms developed from wrist temperature data achieved high performance rates: Algorithm 1 and 2 provided retrospective ovulation estimates in approximately 80% of cycles, while Algorithm 3 predicted the next menses start day with 89.4% accuracy at the time of ovulation detection. All three algorithms significantly outperformed traditional calendar-based methods, with mean absolute errors of 1.66–1.91 days compared to substantially higher calendar method errors. Continuous wrist temperature monitoring overcomes limitations of traditional basal body temperature tracking and provides a non-invasive, objective approach for menstrual cycle monitoring that could have important clinical applications in reproductive health management. The superior performance of wearable temperature sensors suggests they may be particularly valuable for patients with irregular cycles or those seeking more accurate ovulation and menstruation prediction.

title: The Utility of Total Thrombus‐Formation Analysis System (T‐TAS) in the Thrombosis and Hemostasis Field: A Scoping Review
summary:
# Summary

The Total Thrombus-Formation Analysis System (T-TAS) is a functional hemostasis assay that measures platelet adhesion, activation, and aggregation under physiological shear conditions to assess primary hemostasis and thrombus formation. T-TAS effectively detects von Willebrand disease (Types 1, 2A, 2B, and 3), severe platelet function disorders, and monitors the effects of anticoagulant and antiplatelet therapies including warfarin, direct oral anticoagulants, and dual antiplatelet therapy. The system demonstrates clinical utility in predicting bleeding events, assessing hemostatic recovery after platelet transfusion, and monitoring treatment efficacy in various thrombotic and hemostatic conditions. Notably, T-TAS identified a prothrombotic state in endometriosis patients, suggesting potential application in characterizing the hemostatic dysfunction associated with this condition. T-TAS offers particular advantages in evaluating platelet function in thrombocytopenic patients where traditional testing may be limited, with different chip configurations (PL-chip, AR-chip, HD-chip) optimized for specific clinical applications.

title: Influence of Internet-Based Health Management on Control of Blood Glucose in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes: A Four-Year Longitudinal Study
summary:
# Summary

This four-year longitudinal study demonstrates that internet-based health management significantly improves glycemic control and health outcomes in Type 2 diabetes patients through facilitation of sustainable behavioral changes. The intervention reduced fasting blood glucose from 14.16 to 11.78 mmol/L and improved lipid profiles by decreasing total cholesterol, triglycerides, and low-density lipoprotein cholesterol. Key contributors to improved blood glucose control included smoking cessation, increased intake of fish, eggs, poultry, soybeans, nuts, fruits, and vegetables, along with enhanced physical activity and reduced sedentary time. Over the study period, the diabetes control rate among participants increased dramatically from 7.22% to 26.91%, with female participants demonstrating significantly better glycemic control than males. These findings underscore the effectiveness of internet-based health management platforms in promoting lifestyle modifications that reduce the burden of Type 2 diabetes complications and enhance patient quality of life.

title: Low-air-pressure clean room system: A flexible, high-quality model for assisted reproduction laboratories
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates that a customizable low-air-pressure clean room system substantially improves in vitro fertilization (IVF) laboratory conditions and reproductive outcomes. The system maintains critical environmental parameters—including positive pressure differential (13 Pa) to prevent dust contamination, precise temperature control at 37±0.2°C, and humidity regulation—while employing HEPA H14 filters and activated charcoal filtration to reduce airborne particles and volatile organic compounds to near-zero levels. Clinical results from the optimized clean room model showed significantly superior embryo development, with blastocyst formation rates of 63.8% compared to 58.6% in conventional laboratories, and clinical pregnancy rates of 49.8% versus 44.9%, reflecting the critical sensitivity of embryos to environmental contaminants including particulates ≥0.5 µm and VOCs. The system achieved a pregnancy rate of 58.5% following vitrified blastocyst transfer with an implantation rate of 36.2%, demonstrating that meticulous environmental control through advanced HVAC design directly enhances ART success rates. These findings underscore that embryo culture success depends not only on laboratory protocols but fundamentally on maintaining an ISO class 5 air quality standard and preventing environmental perturbations such as pressure fluctuations caused by door openings.

title: Non-Invasive Biomarkers in the Era of Big Data and Machine Learning
summary:
# Summary

This article reviews non-invasive biomarkers and their integration with artificial intelligence and machine learning for disease diagnosis across multiple clinical domains. Non-invasive biomarkers—encompassing molecular approaches like circulating tumor DNA and microRNAs, as well as proteomic and metabolomic analyses—reveal genetic, epigenetic, and metabolic alterations while providing enhanced diagnostic precision through blood samples and other accessible biological materials. Computational advancements in AI and machine learning significantly enhance diagnostic accuracy by uncovering complex patterns in biomarker data that would be difficult to detect through traditional analysis. The article demonstrates the broad clinical applicability of this approach through numerous case studies, including deep learning models for ultrasound-based diagnosis of vascular diseases, CNN-based systems for cancer and dermatologic screening, thermal imaging for infectious diseases, and MRI-derived biomarkers for organ-specific conditions. These combined technological advances represent a paradigm shift toward non-invasive, accessible, and highly accurate disease diagnosis, reducing the need for invasive procedures like biopsies while improving early detection and clinical decision-making.

title: A novel flexible near-infrared endoscopic device that enables real-time artificial intelligence fluorescence tissue characterization
summary: This study presents a novel flexible near-infrared (NIR) endoscopic system enhanced with artificial intelligence for real-time tissue characterization during endoscopy. The device utilizes indocyanine green (ICG), a clinically approved fluorophore that operates at 780-805 nm wavelengths, avoiding autofluorescent background noise while enabling visualization of tissue perfusion. The flexible NIR imaging system achieves 133 micrometers minimum object resolution with a 52 mW laser excitation power and demonstrates effective visualization of ICG at clinically relevant concentrations. A machine learning algorithm was developed for automated fluorescence quantification and flow chamber identification, achieving 79% accuracy compared to manual delineation using Jaccard Index validation. This AI-assisted approach addresses current clinical limitations, including the approximately 20% error rate in standard biopsy categorization for cancer detection, with potential applications in colorectal cancer surveillance, perioperative perfusion assessment, and tissue characterization across multiple endoscopic procedures.

title: Artificial intelligence-based risk assessment tools for sexual, reproductive and mental health: a systematic review
journal: BMC Medical Informatics and Decision Making
summary:
# Summary

This systematic review examines artificial intelligence-based tools for assessing risks in sexual, reproductive, and mental health, demonstrating that AI systems substantially outperform traditional clinical methods when properly implemented. The review identifies multiple high-impact applications of machine learning and natural language processing, including prediction models for cervical cancer, preterm birth, polycystic ovary syndrome, stillbirth, and preeclampsia, as well as AI-assisted screening and triage systems that enhance resource allocation and preliminary diagnosis. A critical finding is the bidirectional relationship between sexual and reproductive health and mental health outcomes: adverse reproductive events like unintended pregnancies, sexually transmitted infections, and unsafe abortions are linked to anxiety and depression, while poor mental health simultaneously impacts contraceptive use and healthcare-seeking behaviors. AI-based risk prediction models leverage data from multiple sources to identify new risk factors and guide clinical decision-making, particularly benefiting low and middle-income countries where access to mental health services remains severely limited. These integrated AI approaches offer promise for democratizing healthcare delivery and addressing the interconnected challenges of reproductive and mental health outcomes across diverse populations.

title: The effects of electromagnetic fields on the number of ovarian primordial follicles: An experimental study
journal: The Kaohsiung Journal of Medical Sciences
summary: This experimental study investigated how electromagnetic field (EMF) exposure affects ovarian reserve by measuring primordial follicle numbers in rats. Exposure to EMF resulted in a substantial reduction in total ovarian follicle count, with the EMF-exposed group showing an average of 70.00 ± 19.03 follicles compared to 150.25 ± 49.53 in controls—representing approximately a 53% decrease in ovarian follicle numbers. The reduction was observed bilaterally, with both right and left ovarian follicle counts significantly decreased in the exposure group relative to baseline. These findings suggest that EMF exposure has a detrimental effect on ovarian primordial follicles, potentially compromising ovarian function and reproductive capacity. While not directly focused on endometriosis pathogenesis, this work raises important considerations about environmental factors that may influence ovarian health in conditions affecting reproductive function.

title: Systematic review on the use of artificial intelligence to identify anatomical structures during laparoscopic cholecystectomy: a tool towards the future
summary:
# Summary

This systematic review examines artificial intelligence applications for identifying anatomical structures during laparoscopic cholecystectomy, aiming to reduce bile duct injuries caused by misunderstanding biliary anatomy. Multiple deep learning architectures—including YOLOv3/7/8, Deep Convolutional Neural Networks, ResNet50, U-Net, and SegFormer—have been successfully applied to detect critical structures like the cystic artery, cystic duct, and common bile duct, as well as to assess the Critical View of Safety (CVS), a key surgical milestone. AI systems demonstrated high performance with mean average precision reaching 98% for biliary structure detection, and several specialized models (EndoDigist, DeepCVS, SmartCoach) achieved inter-rater agreement with surgeons exceeding 82-93%. Importantly, AI-assisted annotation changed surgical assessments in 27% of cases, with 70% of these shifts deemed safer, indicating the technology's potential to enhance operative safety. The evidence suggests that AI-enhanced surgical guidance could reduce complications in challenging cases such as obesity or acute cholecystitis, though continued validation in clinical practice remains essential for establishing this technology as a standard intraoperative decision support tool.

title: Hyperthermic intraperitoneal chemotherapy plus SOX chemotherapy versus SOX chemotherapy alone in patients with gastric cancer and peritoneal metastasis: a phase II randomized clinical trial
summary: Gastric cancer with peritoneal metastasis carries an exceptionally poor prognosis, with peritoneal dissemination representing the most common metastatic pattern in gastric cancer patients. This phase II randomized trial compared hyperthermic intraperitoneal chemotherapy (HIPEC) with paclitaxel combined with SOX chemotherapy against SOX chemotherapy alone, finding that SOX monotherapy achieved superior progression-free survival (median 8.5 months) and overall survival (median 13.0 months) compared to the HIPEC combination group (median OS 10.0 months). However, subgroup analysis revealed that HIPEC provided potential survival benefits in patients with high peritoneal cancer indices (PCI >6) or substantial ascites (≥1,000 mL), suggesting treatment efficacy depends on disease burden. HIPEC's mechanism involves delivering high chemotherapeutic drug concentrations directly to the peritoneal cavity while hyperthermia at 47°C selectively kills malignant cells, and mechanical flushing removes free cancer cells with reduced systemic toxicity. Conversion to surgical resection emerged as a critical prognostic factor, with converted patients achieving median overall survival of 23 months versus significantly lower survival in non-converted patients. Common adverse events across both groups included leukopenia, anemia, hyponatremia, anorexia, and fatigue, though the toxicity profiles were comparable between treatment arms.

title: A meta-analysis of the impact of initial hemodialysis access type on mortality in elderly incident hemodialysis population
summary: This meta-analysis examines how the type of vascular access used at hemodialysis initiation affects mortality in elderly patients with end-stage renal disease. The study demonstrates that patients who initiate hemodialysis with central venous catheters (CVCs) experience significantly higher all-cause mortality compared to those with pre-established arteriovenous fistulas (AVFs), with a 53% increased mortality risk. CVCs are associated with elevated rates of both cardiovascular-related and infection-related mortality, mechanisms that involve catheter-induced bacterial colonization, biofilm formation, and elevated inflammatory markers like C-reactive protein. The data reveal that delayed referral to nephrologists and emergent dialysis initiation via CVC are linked to particularly high mortality and hospitalization rates across all elderly age groups. Routine preoperative vascular imaging and assessment substantially improve AVF placement success and patency rates, offering a preventive strategy. These findings underscore the critical importance of timely nephrology referral and pre-emptive vascular access creation in elderly hemodialysis patients to reduce infection, cardiovascular complications, and mortality risk.

title: MicrobeRX: a tool for enzymatic-reaction-based metabolite prediction in the gut microbiome
summary:
# Summary

MicrobeRX is a computational tool that predicts drug metabolites by integrating enzymatic reactions from both human and gut microbial metabolism. The platform incorporates 5,487 human reactions from the Recon3D genome-scale model and 4,030 unique microbial reactions derived from 6,286 strains representing 1,396 species of the human gut microbiome, enabling comprehensive analysis of how drugs are metabolized through both host and microbial pathways. MicrobeRX substantially outperforms existing tools like BioTransformer 3.0, correctly predicting 66% of drugs and 63.7% of metabolites compared to BioTransformer's 16.7% drug prediction accuracy, while generating over 53,000 high-confidence predictions across 1,074 drugs. The tool uniquely bridges genomic and chemical spaces through multiple integrated analysis modules that assess physicochemical properties, enzyme annotations, and metabolic pathways, while also providing drug bioavailability predictions using Lipinski's rule of five and BOILED-Egg analysis. By revealing both unique and overlapping metabolic capabilities between human and microbial systems, MicrobeRX provides valuable insights into how complex host-microbiome interactions shape drug metabolism and individual drug response, with potential applications in understanding how the gut microbiome influences therapeutic efficacy and metabolite levels in clinical contexts.

title: Inter‐observer reproducibility of the 2021 AAGL Endometriosis Classification
journal: The Australian & New Zealand Journal of Obstetrics & Gynaecology
summary: The 2021 AAGL Endometriosis Classification system represents a potential improvement over the revised ASRM staging system for evaluating endometriosis, particularly in correlating with surgical complexity and clinical outcomes related to pain and fertility. However, this study demonstrates that the AAGL system exhibits poor inter-observer reproducibility in its current format, with significant disagreement among observers in initial staging assessments and systematic bias toward higher scores by certain evaluators. Critical sources of variability include ambiguity in lesion size estimation, unclear criteria for adhesions caused by endometriosis, and inaccuracies inherent to visual estimation by clinicians. Importantly, inter-observer agreement improved substantially when consensus rules were applied to clarify and simplify areas of ambiguity in interpretation, reducing inter-observer variability and maintaining consistency across evaluations. While the AAGL system showed high concordance with surgical complexity in initial prospective studies, external validation efforts failed to reproduce these results, highlighting the need for refinement. The authors conclude that before widespread clinical implementation, the AAGL classification requires further clarification of ambiguous criteria and potentially standardized guidelines analogous to the consensus rules that improved reproducibility in this study.

title: Artificial intelligence in abdominal and pelvic ultrasound imaging: current applications
summary: Artificial intelligence applications in abdominal and pelvic ultrasound imaging demonstrate substantial promise for improving disease diagnosis and clinical monitoring across multiple organ systems. Deep learning models, particularly convolutional neural networks and their variants, have been successfully deployed for detecting and classifying diverse pathologies including liver lesions, acute cholecystitis, appendicitis, renal cysts, and pediatric conditions like intussusception. In the pelvic imaging domain relevant to endometriosis, deep learning models have achieved high diagnostic accuracy for Pouch of Douglas assessment, pelvic mass detection, and ovarian pathology evaluation, with reported performance metrics exceeding 95% accuracy in several applications.
