summary: This retrospective cohort study of 572 IVF cycles demonstrates that preimplantation genetic testing for monogenic conditions (PGT-M) significantly improves clinical pregnancy and live birth rates in individuals at risk of inheriting single-gene disorders such as cystic fibrosis, fragile X syndrome, and myotonic dystrophy type 1. The study employed trophectoderm cell biopsy combined with karyomapping protocol to enable haplotype phasing and simultaneous 24-chromosome aneuploidy screening, allowing selective transfer of unaffected embryos. Notably, cycles involving autosomal recessive indications achieved the highest success rates, while X-linked dominant conditions showed comparatively lower cycle-based pregnancy rates. However, multiple factors independently reduced success outcomes: patients with concurrent subfertility factors were 42% less likely to achieve live birth, and advanced maternal age, elevated FSH dosage, and abnormal BMI all showed negative associations with pregnancy and live birth rates. These findings underscore that while PGT-M substantially improves reproductive outcomes for monogenic disease prevention, patient-specific variables substantially modulate treatment success and should inform clinical counseling and cycle planning.

title: Single- vs double-layer uterine closure of the cesarean scar in niche development: the Nicest Study
summary:
# Summary

This comparative study demonstrates that double-layer uterine closure at cesarean delivery produces superior anatomical outcomes for scar healing compared to single-layer closure. At six months postpartum, the double-layer technique resulted in greater residual myometrial thickness (4.3 versus 4.0 mm) and a better healing ratio (69% versus 60%), while the single-layer approach was associated with larger niche volumes and greater niche depth and length. Cesarean scar defects (niches) develop in approximately one-third of women after primary cesarean delivery and are clinically significant because larger niches correlate with postmenstrual spotting, subfertility, and adverse outcomes in subsequent pregnancies, particularly when residual myometrial thickness falls below 3 mm. Double-layer closure substantially reduces the proportion of large niches with severely reduced myometrial thickness and may therefore mitigate these complications. The study validates transvaginal ultrasound and three-dimensional imaging as diagnostic tools for niche detection and characterization, establishing that a niche volume exceeding 0.15 cm³ predicts postmenstrual spotting with high sensitivity and specificity.

title: Hydrogels in Contraceptive Applications and Their
summary:
# Summary

Hydrogels represent a promising alternative to traditional contraceptive methods, offering distinct advantages over conventional approaches like condoms (which have a 13% failure rate) and hormonal contraceptives (which commonly cause negative side effects). These water-absorbing polymer networks mimic natural tissue properties and can be engineered as either natural formulations (nontoxic, biocompatible, and biodegradable) or synthetic versions (with enhanced water absorption and durability). Hydrogel contraceptives function through multiple mechanisms including physical barriers that occlude the reproductive tract, spermicidal effects that disrupt sperm function, and controlled drug release systems. Notable hydrogel-based technologies include RISUG and Vasalgel, which are reversible male contraceptives that prevent sperm passage for extended periods (10-15 years) and can be reversed with specific solutions, and intrauterine devices like the P-block that prevents tubal and intrauterine pregnancies. While Ushercell demonstrated dual contraceptive and antimicrobial activity against HIV and other sexually transmitted infections, Phase 3 clinical trials were halted due to increased HIV infection rates, highlighting the importance of rigorous safety evaluation in contraceptive development.

title: IVF versus ICSI in patients without severe male factor infertility: a randomized clinical trial
summary:
# Summary

This randomized clinical trial compared conventional in vitro fertilization (c-IVF) and intracytoplasmic sperm injection (ICSI) in patients without severe male factor infertility. While ICSI was originally developed to overcome barriers posed by low sperm count and motility, the study found that ICSI did not improve cumulative live birth rates compared to c-IVF (43.2% versus 47.3%, respectively). Additionally, ICSI resulted in lower fertilization rates per oocyte retrieved, lower embryo utilization rates, and reduced live birth rates after the first embryo transfer, with no significant differences in obstetric and perinatal outcomes between groups. These findings establish c-IVF as the preferred first-line treatment for patients with normal or mildly reduced sperm parameters, and support reserving ICSI exclusively for cases of severe male factor infertility. The results challenge the historical expansion of ICSI use beyond its original indication and align with recent Asian randomized controlled trials demonstrating no benefit of ICSI in non-severe male factor scenarios.

title: FIGO position statement: Gamete donations
journal: International Journal of Gynaecology and Obstetrics
summary:
# Summary

This FIGO position statement addresses the critical role of gamete donation in assisted reproductive technologies and the comprehensive ethical and medical standards required to protect donor and recipient welfare. Gamete donation, involving both sperm donors (recommended age 21-45 years) and oocyte donors (age 21-35 years), treats infertility but necessitates rigorous screening protocols including genetic testing for conditions such as cystic fibrosis, spinal muscular atrophy, thalassemia, and hemoglobinopathy, as well as infectious disease screening for HIV, hepatitis B and C, syphilis, chlamydia, gonorrhea, cytomegalovirus, and rubella. Informed consent and mental health assessment are mandatory, and gametes must be handled according to Good Laboratory Practices with strict cryopreservation protocols and comprehensive record-keeping for traceability. A major challenge identified is the conflict between intended donor anonymity and modern DNA databases, which increasingly compromise long-term confidentiality, with different countries adopting divergent regulatory approaches ranging from complete anonymity preference in China and Iran to mandatory non-anonymous registration in Sweden and Australia. Recipients must be informed of potential risks including miscarriage, preterm birth, and genetic abnormalities, while stem cell research represents a promising alternative to address ethical concerns surrounding gamete donation.

title: Factors associated with diagnostic and treatment intervals in colorectal cancer: A linked data study
summary: This study examined factors influencing diagnostic and treatment delays in colorectal cancer using linked administrative data. The research identified that patients presenting with alarm symptoms experienced significantly shorter diagnostic intervals compared to those with non-alarm symptoms, while paradoxically, frequent general practice attendance was associated with longer diagnostic delays, potentially reflecting diagnostic uncertainty in primary care. Treatment intervals varied substantially by modality, with surgery achieving the shortest timeframe (18 days median) compared to chemotherapy (27 days), radiotherapy (32 days), and chemoradiotherapy (32 days), and by cancer location, with colon cancer requiring faster intervention than rectal cancer. The study emphasized that diagnostic and treatment delays correlate with poorer outcomes and identified systemic barriers including the gatekeeper role of general practitioners and lack of unified medical records as contributors to prolonged intervals. Key recommendations included implementing electronic decision support systems, clinical practice guidelines, direct access colonoscopy pathways, and nurse-led endoscopy services to reduce delays and improve colorectal cancer outcomes.

title: Advancements in artificial intelligence for ultrasound diagnosis of ovarian cancer: a comprehensive review
summary:
# Summary

This comprehensive review examines artificial intelligence applications for ultrasound-based diagnosis of ovarian cancer, particularly in patients with endometriosis where diagnostic specificity is challenging. TP53 gene mutations, present in 96% of high-grade plasmacytoid ovarian cancer cases, cause loss of protein function that impairs DNA repair capacity and promotes aggressive cancer cell growth associated with poor prognosis. AI-assisted ultrasound systems have substantially improved diagnostic performance, with ensemble convolutional neural network models achieving 91% sensitivity and 92% specificity, significantly outperforming traditional machine learning approaches and reducing diagnostic variability across sonographers of varying experience levels. Multimodal integration approaches that combine ultrasound imaging features with clinical data and tumor markers such as CA-125 demonstrate enhanced predictive performance, with fusion models achieving area under the curve values of 0.83–0.85 and models like OvcaFinder effectively reducing false-positive rates. Beyond classification, advanced radiomics models can predict patient outcomes including overall survival, recurrence-free survival, and lymph node status, enabling comprehensive prognostic assessment. However, the field faces critical challenges including overfitting from insufficient training data and the need to integrate genetic and molecular signatures with imaging features to further advance diagnostic accuracy and clinical utility.

title: Testicular extracellular matrix/gelatin-based scaffold using gas foaming to support spermatogonial stem cells
journal: Iranian Journal of Basic Medical Sciences
summary:
# Summary

This study developed hybrid scaffolds combining decellularized testicular extracellular matrix (ECM) and gelatin using gas foaming to support spermatogonial stem cell growth, addressing infertility caused by cancer treatments. Male ram testes were decellularized using NaCl buffer and Triton X-100 to remove cellular content while preserving structural ECM components including collagen, glycosaminoglycans, proteoglycans, and elastin. The resulting gel-ECM 5% hybrid scaffolds exhibited uniform porous microstructures (298-330 μm pore size) that significantly enhanced cell penetration, attachment, and viability compared to other formulations, while glutaraldehyde crosslinking at 0.5% concentration optimized cell survival. The appropriate pore diameter and scaffold composition together facilitated oxygen diffusion, nutrient transport, and metabolic waste removal, promoting testicular cell proliferation and differentiation. These findings suggest that testicular ECM-gelatin hybrid scaffolds represent a promising tissue engineering approach for constructing bioartificial testes to treat infertility in cancer survivors.

title: AI-based pelvic floor surface electromyography reference ranges and high-precision pelvic floor dysfunction diagnosis
summary: This study develops AI-based diagnostic tools to improve pelvic floor dysfunction (PFD) detection by leveraging surface electromyography (sEMG) data. Pelvic floor dysfunctions—characterized by urinary incontinence, fecal incontinence, pelvic organ prolapse, and pelvic pain—significantly impair quality of life through physical discomfort and emotional distress. The researchers constructed a comprehensive multidimensional sEMG database and employed machine learning techniques, including genetic algorithms for parameter optimization and ensemble learning, to establish AI-derived reference ranges and develop the AI-Diagnostician-PFD model, which surpasses the limitations of the classical 20-year-old Glazer protocol. The AI-Diagnostician-PFD model demonstrated superior diagnostic performance compared to conventional machine learning and deep learning approaches, achieving an AUC of approximately 0.81 on validation datasets. Normal pelvic floor function is associated with pelvic floor muscle strength ratings M3–M5, whereas conditions like urinary incontinence, pelvic organ prolapse, and chronic pelvic pain correlate with abnormal neuromuscular activation patterns detectable by sEMG. This AI-enabled diagnostic framework promises to be implemented as free software on clinical machines, providing clinicians with a more precise and accessible tool for PFD diagnosis.

title: Analysis of a women’s health information website established by the Japanese Ministry of Health, Labour, and Welfare
summary:
# Summary

This analysis examines HealthCareLabo, a women's health information website established by Japan's Ministry of Health, Labour, and Welfare, focusing on its role in promoting health literacy and disease awareness. The study reveals that women of reproductive age show particularly high engagement with health-related concerns, including premenstrual syndrome and endometriosis, conditions that remain significantly underfunded and under-researched despite their prevalence. The Self-check feature—particularly self-assessment tools for PMS/PMDD and endometriosis—emerged as a central component of the platform, with 66% of engaged users aged 25-44 utilizing these screening tools. The research demonstrates that these screening tools improve diagnostic efficiency and help guide patients toward appropriate medical attention, while simultaneously enhancing overall health literacy. The findings underscore how digital health platforms can address gaps in women's health education, particularly for reproductive-age populations, and help counteract care-avoidant behaviors associated with low health literacy. Overall, the study positions HealthCareLabo as an effective intervention for improving health outcomes in women by facilitating early detection and promoting informed health-seeking behaviors.

title: Transforming women's health, empowerment, and gender equality with digital health: evidence-based policy and practice
summary: Digital health technologies substantially improve women's healthcare access, self-care, and self-monitoring capabilities while simultaneously advancing women's empowerment and gender equality through enhanced health education and skills acquisition. These technologies contribute to better overall health outcomes for women by supporting self-management of vital signs, medication adherence, and lifestyle modifications, while also promoting social connectivity and interpersonal communication. Mobile health applications represent the dominant technological approach, featured in 62.5% of reviewed studies, with telehealth and web-based platforms comprising an additional 31.3%. Artificial intelligence has been specifically tested for endometriosis detection, and digital health interventions successfully treat postpartum depression and monitor pregnancy, demonstrating broad clinical applicability. However, significant barriers limit equitable implementation, including restricted access, cultural factors, socioeconomic disparities, and caregiving responsibilities that disproportionately affect certain populations of women. Overall, digital health technologies have documented potential to advance the UN Sustainable Development Goals, though realizing this potential requires addressing underlying inequities in technology access and adoption.

title: Accuracy of multimodal vaginal ultrasound in the detection and assessment of scar healing after caesarean section: a correlational meta-analysis
summary:
# Summary

Multimodal transvaginal ultrasound, which integrates two-dimensional grey-scale imaging, three-dimensional ultrasonography, and color Doppler blood flow assessment, demonstrates high accuracy in detecting and evaluating post-caesarean scar healing. Poor scar healing is characterized by ultrasonographic findings including hypoechoic or anechoic areas, myometrial thinning (≤3.73 mm lower uterine segment thickness), myometrial discontinuity, blurred incision contours, and absent blood flow signals around the scar. At the molecular level, poor scar healing involves excessive fibroblast proliferation in the lower uterine segment, chronic endometritis, and elevated inflammatory cytokines (IL-1α, IL-1β, IL-6, IL-8, TNF-α, and SDF-1α) within cesarean scar diverticula. Inadequate scar healing significantly increases risks of serious obstetric complications in subsequent pregnancies, including uterine rupture, uterine bleeding, and placenta praevia, which compromise both maternal fertility and overall health. The multimodal ultrasound approach overcomes limitations of conventional imaging by avoiding interference from intestinal gas and obesity while providing superior resolution for detailed assessment of scar architecture and vascularization.

title: Proteomic risk scores for predicting common diseases using linear and neural network models in the UK biobank
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates that plasma proteomic risk scores substantially enhance disease prediction across multiple common conditions when integrated with clinical models. Neural network-based proteomic scores outperformed traditional linear models for 11 tested diseases by capturing nonlinear relationships and complex protein interactions, with improvements ranging from 0.02 to 0.08 in C-index discrimination metrics. Key disease-associated proteins were identified, including GDF15 as a shared predictor across 12 diseases, PAEP as a unique endometriosis biomarker, and NTRK2 as the largest contributor to overall risk scores, while proteins like HPGDS and IL13RA1 showed protective associations with Parkinson's disease. The proteomic approach achieved particularly strong predictive performance for end-stage renal disease (C-index 0.91) and Parkinson's disease (C-index 0.80), highlighting clinical potential for early disease detection in conditions with limited screening options. These findings establish plasma proteomics combined with machine learning as a powerful strategy for personalized disease risk stratification, especially in diseases such as Parkinson's and multiple sclerosis where early detection remains challenging.

title: Development of a single-center predictive model for conventional in vitro fertilization outcomes excluding total fertilization failure: implications for protocol selection
summary:
# Summary

This study developed predictive models for conventional in vitro fertilization (c-IVF) outcomes to identify patients at risk of fertilization failure. Multiple clinical and semen parameters were identified as significant predictors, including protective factors such as male age and total progressive motile sperm count (TPMC), and risk factors including elevated female BMI and sperm DNA fragmentation index (DFI). A logistic regression model demonstrated superior predictive performance with a mean area under the curve of 0.734, outperforming random forest and XGBoost approaches, while a derived nomogram showed moderate-to-high discriminative power (C-index = 0.722) with confirmed clinical utility. Notably, rescue intracytoplasmic sperm injection (ICSI) dramatically improved outcomes in patients with low baseline fertilization, increasing normal fertilization rates from 17.16% to 61.78% and reducing unfertilized oocytes from 79.86% to 28.26%. These findings indicate that comprehensive assessment of both female and male factors enables accurate prediction of fertilization failure risk and supports informed protocol selection for optimizing IVF success.

title: Supplementation with oviductal EVs from the estrus, metestrus, and diestrus stages improved developmental competence of IVF mouse embryos
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates that oviductal extracellular vesicles (EVs) harvested from estrus, metestrus, and diestrus stages significantly enhance mouse embryo development in vitro through maternal-embryo crosstalk mechanisms. Supplementation with these stage-specific EVs improved blastocyst yield, hatching rates, and blastocyst cell numbers in a dose-dependent manner, with optimal effects observed at 3×10⁹ to 1.2×10¹⁰ particles/ml. The beneficial effects were optimized through repeated EV addition (every 2 days) and by adding EVs at the 3-4 cell stage onward, suggesting that EVs mediate critical communication between the oviductal environment and developing embryos. Mechanistically, oviductal EVs penetrate the zona pellucida and transfer bioactive cargos including proteins and RNAs to embryo membranes, with diestrus-derived EVs particularly enriched in estrogen receptor content that supports embryonic progression. These findings highlight how EV-based supplementation could improve in vitro fertilization outcomes by compensating for the absent embryo-oviduct crosstalk in conventional culture systems.

title: Perspectives in transvaginal sonography for the diagnosis of adenomyosis
summary: Adenomyosis, characterized by infiltration of endometrial tissue into the myometrium, causes severe pain, excessive menstrual bleeding, and significantly impacts quality of life in women of reproductive age. Transvaginal sonography (TVS) has emerged as a reliable non-invasive diagnostic tool that provides superior image clarity for assessing adenomyosis-associated lesions and detecting key sonographic features including subendometrial echogenicity, myometrial heterogeneity, junctional zone abnormalities, and the "question mark sign." Advanced sonographic techniques including three-dimensional TVS, color Doppler ultrasound, contrast-enhanced ultrasound, and elastography enhance diagnostic accuracy by improving blood flow assessment and tissue characterization, with elastography detecting the increased tissue stiffness resulting from fibrosis and myometrial hyperplasia. The Morphological Uterus Sonographic Assessment (MUSA) guidelines have standardized TVS interpretation and improved diagnostic accuracy, while combining TVS with magnetic resonance imaging further optimizes diagnostic performance, particularly in complex cases with concurrent fibroids. Artificial intelligence and deep learning integration with conventional imaging represents a transformative advancement, enabling automated measurement of junctional zone thickness, identification of echogenic features, and differentiation of adenomyosis from other uterine pathologies with greater speed and accuracy than conventional analysis alone.

title: Leveraging machine learning models to evaluate immune infiltration in the ovarian cancer microenvironment: a single-cell analysis approach
summary: This study applies machine learning to characterize immune infiltration in ovarian cancer using single-cell transcriptomic analysis and computational deconvolution algorithms. The researchers employed multiple approaches including TIMER 2.0, CIBERSORT, and xCell to quantify immune and stromal cell populations, then constructed predictive models using RandomForest and Support Vector Machine algorithms after removing batch effects through standardized correction methods. A RandomForest model achieved superior performance (AUC 0.88) compared to traditional immune scoring indices, demonstrating the advantage of machine learning for predicting immune infiltration status at cellular resolution. The study identified distinct immune cell subpopulations—including myeloid cells, tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes, T cells, NK cells, and dendritic cells—along with their interaction networks and expression of immunomodulatory molecules such as checkpoint inhibitors, cytokines, and chemokines. Critically, increased immune cell infiltration was associated with improved patient survival and better treatment response, establishing immune composition as a key prognostic factor in ovarian cancer. This machine learning-based approach provides a robust strategy for identifying biomarkers and therapeutic targets to enable individualized immunotherapy and improve clinical outcomes in this heterogeneous disease.

title: Enhancing oocyte activation in women with ovarian failure: clinical outcomes of the Stem Cell Regenera study using G-CSF mobilization of peripheral blood stem cells and intraovarian injection of stem cell factor-enriched platelet rich plasma in real-world-practice
summary:
# Summary

The Stem Cell Regenera treatment, which combines granulocyte colony-stimulating factor (G-CSF) mobilization of peripheral blood hematopoietic stem cells with intraovarian injection of stem cell factor-enriched platelet rich plasma (SCFE-PRP), successfully induced oocyte activation in 68.28% of women with ovarian failure, poor ovarian response, diminished ovarian reserve, or premature ovarian insufficiency. This activation, defined by increases in antral follicle count and anti-Müllerian hormone levels, led to spontaneous gestation in 7.07% of activated patients and pregnancy rates of 14.14% following IVF, with no pregnancies achieved in women without oocyte activation. CD34+ hematopoietic stem cells mobilized through G-CSF treatment demonstrated strong association with successful oocyte activation and promoted angiogenesis and tissue regeneration through paracrine signaling mechanisms. Younger age (under 38 years), higher leukocyte counts, and elevated CD34+ cell numbers significantly influenced activation outcomes. The treatment was well-tolerated with safe stem cell mobilization in all participants, though approximately 30% of women required G-CSF dosage adjustments during the four-day protocol.

title: Optimizing Esophageal Cancer Diagnosis with Computer-Aided Detection by YOLO Models Combined with Hyperspectral Imaging
summary: This article presents a computer-aided detection system for esophageal cancer that combines hyperspectral imaging with YOLO deep learning models to improve diagnostic accuracy. The study evaluated multiple YOLO variants (YOLOv3-v8) trained on both standard white-light imaging (WLI) and SAVE (a hyperspectral imaging technique), with SAVE consistently outperforming traditional WLI across all models, particularly for early-stage cancer detection. YOLOv8 demonstrated superior sensitivity specifically for squamous cell carcinomas, while narrow-band imaging enhanced lesion detection by identifying lesions missed on white-light examination. The research establishes that achalasia increases esophageal cancer risk through prolonged mucosal stagnation and dysplastic changes, with adenocarcinoma showing better survival outcomes than squamous cell carcinoma when diagnosed early. Technical optimization involved addressing sensor artifacts including dark current noise and non-linear color responses that distort hyperspectral data accuracy. Overall, the integration of hyperspectral imaging with advanced YOLO object detection models significantly enhances esophageal cancer identification and characterization capabilities.

title: Current Unveiling Key Research Trends in Endometrial Cancer: A Comprehensive Topic Modeling Analysis
summary:
# Endometrial Cancer: Risk Factors, Molecular Classification, and Emerging Therapeutic Strategies

This comprehensive analysis identifies obesity, advanced age, and chronic low-grade inflammation as primary risk factors for endometrial cancer, with incidence and mortality rising substantially with increased body mass index. The disease shows distinct molecular subtypes—POLE ultramutated, microsatellite instability, copy-number low, and TP53-mutated—which have implications for treatment selection, including emerging strategies with PARP inhibitors and immunotherapy combinations. Clinical staging and histological grade remain critical prognostic indicators, while depth of myometrial invasion and lymph node metastasis status are key determinants of recurrence risk and overall survival. Diagnostic approaches have evolved to include transvaginal ultrasound, endometrial biopsy, and hysteroscopy, with sentinel lymph node biopsy improving detection accuracy while reducing false negatives. Minimally invasive laparoscopic surgical approaches have demonstrated superior recovery profiles and reduced complication rates compared to traditional laparotomy. Recent therapeutic advances, particularly the combination of pembrolizumab and lenvatinib, alongside progestin therapy for conservative management in select patient populations, represent important developments in endometrial cancer treatment.

title: Applications of Spatial Transcriptomics in Veterinary Medicine: A Scoping Review of Research, Diagnostics, and Treatment Strategies
summary:
# Summary

Spatial transcriptomics (ST) is an advanced molecular profiling technology that enables comprehensive mapping of gene expression patterns while preserving tissue architecture, with applications across diagnostic, mechanistic, and therapeutic research domains. The technology facilitates biomarker discovery, tissue heterogeneity analysis, and investigation of host-immune responses and tumor microenvironments through platforms such as 10× Visium, Slide-seq, and MERFISH, which offer varying resolutions and tissue compatibility. While human oncology applications dominate ST research—particularly in breast, liver, colorectal, brain, and lung cancers—emerging studies demonstrate applications in veterinary medicine for companion animal and livestock health, including mucosal immunity, parasitology, and nervous system degeneration research. Analytical tools including Seurat, Giotto, BayesSpace, and SpaGCN enable spatial data integration, clustering, and domain detection, with batch correction methods addressing cross-platform data alignment challenges. Animal models including mice, macaques, cattle, and canines serve critical roles in platform validation and mechanistic research, supporting translational insights between comparative animal models and human disease. Overall, ST technology is driving precision medicine and targeted therapeutic development by revealing disease mechanisms and identifying diagnostic biomarkers across diverse clinical applications.

title: Bacterial extracellular vesicles as a tunable platform for vaginal drug delivery
summary: This study evaluates bacterial extracellular vesicles (bEVs) derived from *Escherichia coli* Nissle 1917 as a tunable platform for vaginal drug delivery, demonstrating successful loading of model proteins such as moxNeonGreen into these nanoparticles. The bEVs, which are naturally produced by Gram-negative bacteria and can encapsulate various molecular cargoes, showed no negative impact on vaginal epithelial or endocervical cells, establishing their safety for mucosal application. After vaginal administration in mice, bEVs were retained in the female reproductive tract for over six hours, supporting their potential for targeted tissue delivery. The research leverages genetic engineering and growth parameter optimization to enhance protein loading efficiency into bEVs, making them versatile carriers for therapeutic applications. This work positions bEVs as an attractive alternative to synthetic nanoparticles for treating reproductive diseases, building on the established benefits of vaginal drug delivery for conditions including preterm birth, vaginal atrophy, and sexually transmitted infections. The combination of bacterial origin, inherent safety profile, and tunable loading capacity suggests bEVs could overcome manufacturing challenges associated with conventional drug delivery systems.

title: Intrauterine adhesion after missed miscarriage evacuation: efficacy of different estrogen doses on incidence and risk factors
summary: This study evaluated estrogen dosing strategies for preventing intrauterine adhesions (IUA) following missed miscarriage evacuation. Preoperative administration of estradiol valerate at 5 mg/day significantly reduced IUA incidence to 4.84% compared to 7.14% at lower doses and 21.43% in the control group, while also promoting superior endometrial recovery and faster menstrual restoration. The 5 mg/day estradiol valerate group demonstrated substantially greater endometrial thickness at 14 days postoperatively, suggesting a direct protective effect on the endometrium. Pre-existing risk factors for adhesion formation were identified, including preoperative endometrial thickness below 4 mm and a history of prior IUA, both serving as independent predictors of postoperative adhesion risk. These findings establish optimal estrogen dosing for adhesion prevention and highlight the importance of identifying high-risk patients with thin endometrium or previous adhesion history for enhanced prophylactic strategies.

title: Fertility Awareness-Based Methods for Family Planning: A Systematic Review
summary:
# Summary

Fertility awareness-based methods (FABMs) are family planning approaches that track multiple physiological indicators including basal body temperature, cervical mucus changes, urinary hormone levels, and menstrual cycle patterns to identify fertile windows and manage conception or contraception. The primary methods include calendar-based approaches, cervical mucus observation, symptom-thermal methods combining mucus and temperature data, and symptom-hormonal methods incorporating technology to detect urinary metabolites. When used correctly, FABMs demonstrate efficacy rates exceeding 90% for both contraception and conception, with intercourse timed during peak fertility phases associated with 87% pregnancy rates compared to only 5% when limited to low-fertility days. Digital technologies substantially enhance FABM success by reducing user error through automated tracking and personalized insights, though effectiveness depends critically on adequate user education, consistent application, and proper interpretation of fertility signals. FABMs also facilitate diagnosis of ovulation-related disorders such as polycystic ovary syndrome and endometriosis while promoting partner communication and body literacy, though healthcare provider knowledge remains essential for optimal outcomes.

title: A descriptive analysis of a fertility services waitlist in Eastern Canada
summary: This descriptive analysis of a fertility services waitlist examines the multifactorial nature of infertility and its clinical management in Eastern Canada. Maternal age emerges as a critical determinant of fertility status, with advancing female age reducing both unassisted pregnancy success and assisted reproductive technology outcomes through mechanisms including oocyte depletion and anovulation. The study identifies multiple causes of infertility including tubal damage, ovulatory dysfunction, and sperm abnormalities, all of which impose significant psychological burden on affected couples. Beyond pathological factors, modifiable lifestyle variables—including obesity, poor nutrition, smoking, and lack of exercise—substantially influence fertility outcomes, suggesting that comprehensive reproductive care should integrate behavioral interventions. The analysis documents several clinical approaches to infertility management available through specialized fertility services, ranging from pharmacological treatments like clomiphene citrate to assisted reproductive technologies and gamete cryopreservation. Notably, the study recognizes that failure to conceive may reflect diverse underlying conditions including endometriosis, highlighting the importance of thorough diagnostic evaluation in fertility assessment.

title: Comprehensive application of artificial intelligence in colorectal cancer: A review
summary:
# Summary

Artificial intelligence, particularly deep learning and machine learning algorithms, is increasingly integrated into comprehensive colorectal cancer management across screening, diagnosis, treatment planning, and prognosis assessment. Machine learning systems can leverage patient demographic data and complete blood count information to assess cancer risk, while computer-aided detection and diagnosis systems significantly improve polyp detection rates and enable endoscopists to differentiate between neoplastic and non-neoplastic lesions during endoscopy. Advanced radiomics approaches, including CT, T2-weighted imaging, and 18F-FDG PET/CT radiomics, facilitate prediction of molecular features such as microsatellite instability and KRAS mutations, as well as patient prognosis. Deep learning models have demonstrated capability in predicting cancer recurrence using digital pathology images and identifying clinical risk factors including tumor size, perineural invasion, and positive lymph node ratio. These AI applications represent a paradigm shift in colorectal cancer management, transforming early detection, molecular characterization, and outcome prediction to improve clinical outcomes for patients with this second most lethal malignancy worldwide.

title: Exploring the Nutrition‐Related Healthcare Experiences of Individuals With Endometriosis: Qualitative Interviews With Consumers and Dietitians
journal: Journal of Human Nutrition and Dietetics
summary: Endometriosis is an estrogen-driven inflammatory disease characterized by ectopic endometrial tissue that causes chronic pain, gastrointestinal disturbances, and substantially reduced quality of life, with individuals experiencing an average diagnostic delay of seven years. Nutritional interventions, particularly dietitian-led approaches and low FODMAP diets, have demonstrated effectiveness in improving endometriosis symptoms and quality of life by modulating inflammation, estrogen levels, and menstrual cyclicity. However, individuals frequently resort to self-directed restrictive dietary modifications, which paradoxically increases the prevalence of disordered eating and may exacerbate symptoms or create new health complications, with those having advanced disease being particularly vulnerable to restrictive practices. Critical barriers to accessing professional nutrition support include diagnostic delays, financial constraints, geographical limitations, and lack of recognized referral pathways, compounded by insufficient healthcare provider recognition of dietitians' expertise and widespread reliance on non-credible information sources. Both consumers and dietitians emphasize the necessity for evidence-based nutritional guidance, clear referral systems, early dietary intervention, and multi-disciplinary holistic care that respects individual values and motivations. System-level changes including transparent information sources, established referral pathways, clinical support networks, and alignment between practitioner recommendations and patient values are essential to optimize endometriosis management and quality of life outcomes.

title: Application of enhanced recovery after surgery in perioperative management of patients undergoing laparoscopic surgery for benign gynecological conditions
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates that Enhanced Recovery After Surgery (ERAS) protocols significantly optimize perioperative management for patients undergoing laparoscopic surgery for benign gynecological conditions. The ERAS protocol employs a multidisciplinary approach incorporating preoperative nutritional optimization, precise anesthesia, early mobilization, multimodal analgesia, and early catheter removal to mitigate the physiological stress response triggered by surgery. Compared to conventional management, ERAS patients achieved faster gastrointestinal recovery (first flatus in 15.5 versus 21.2 hours), shorter hospital stays (4.9 versus 6.3 days), reduced postoperative cortisol levels indicating decreased physiological stress, and better preservation of nutritional markers including prealbumin and albumin. These improvements translated to substantial clinical benefits: reduced postoperative complications, lower healthcare costs (13,960 versus 15,270 yuan), decreased risk of urinary tract infections and nosocomial infections through early catheter removal and hospital discharge, and higher patient satisfaction rates. The ERAS protocol's effectiveness stems from preventing extended preoperative fasting and associated metabolic dysfunction while simultaneously reducing surgical-induced inflammatory responses and stress hormone elevation.

title: Dual-Emitting Molecularly Imprinted Nanopolymers for the Detection of CA19-9
summary:
# Summary

This study describes dual-emitting molecularly imprinted nanopolymers (dual@nanoMIPs) that combine blue-emitting carbon dots and yellow-emitting quantum dots for sensitive detection of CA19-9, a clinically important biomarker for pancreatic and gastrointestinal cancers. The dual@nanoMIPs exploit ratiometric fluorescence, where CA19-9 binding causes differential quenching of the two fluorophores, reducing environmental interference and improving detection reliability. The sensor achieves high selectivity with minimal cross-reactivity across a wide dynamic range (4.98 × 10⁻³ to 8.39 × 10² U mL⁻¹) in serum samples, with rapid target recognition within one hour. The underlying mechanism involves electrostatic interactions between the negatively charged CA19-9 and the positively charged imprinted polymer matrix, with fluorescence recovery upon template removal confirming the reversibility and specificity of binding. This nanotechnology-based point-of-care diagnostic tool represents a significant advancement for early pancreatic cancer screening and longitudinal monitoring of cancer patients with improved quantification accuracy through the observable color shift from yellow to green.

title: Intraoperative Ultrasound as a Decision-Making Tool in Modern Gynecologic Oncology
summary: Intraoperative ultrasound (IOUS) has emerged as a transformative decision-making tool in gynecologic oncology, substantially improving surgical outcomes across multiple dimensions. IOUS increases complete resection rates from 68% to 87% and demonstrates exceptional sensitivity (88-93%) for detecting subcentimeter metastases, while simultaneously reducing unnecessary lymphadenectomies by 38% and lowering procedure-related morbidity including a 62% reduction in symptomatic lymphoceles and 30% decreased postoperative lymphedema rates. The technology proves particularly valuable in endometriosis and fertility-preserving surgery, guiding complete resection of deep infiltrating disease, localizing occult tumors, and identifying metastatic lymph nodes missed by preoperative imaging, with IOUS-guided resection achieving 100% complete excision in cases of recurrent borderline serous tumors. IOUS demonstrates superior sensitivity compared to conventional CT and MRI staging, particularly for detecting para-aortic nodal metastases and interval nodes smaller than 1 cm, and can alter intraoperative decision-making in 25-40% of cases. Modern ultrasound systems with real-time navigation capabilities enable nephron-sparing precision in renal tumor resection and optimize cytoreduction in advanced ovarian cancer, addressing the critical clinical imperative that complete gross resection correlates with nearly doubled median survival compared to suboptimal cytoreduction.

title: Effects of different embryo culture media on birthweight following assisted reproductive technology
summary:
# Summary

This study examined how different embryo culture media used during in vitro fertilization affect newborn birthweight and associated health outcomes. The researchers found that the composition of culture media significantly influences birthweight z-scores, with G5 and HTF media producing higher birthweight z-scores and increased risks of large-for-gestational-age (LGA) infants and macrosomia compared to ready-to-use media like Cook and G5-PLUS. While female BMI emerged as the most important factor determining birthweight, embryo culture medium had moderate significance as an independent predictor. The increased birth sizes associated with G5 and HTF media are clinically relevant because LGA and macrosomia in infancy are associated with elevated risks of obesity, cardiovascular disease, and diabetes in adulthood. These findings suggest that ready-to-use culture media may be preferable for reducing adverse birthweight outcomes, though the underlying mechanisms—such as the role of human serum albumin and its bioactive components—require further investigation to optimize embryo culture protocols.

title: Nasal Residence Depending on the Administered Dosage Form: Impact of Formulation Type on the In Vivo Nasal Retention Time of Drugs in Rats
summary:
# Summary

This study investigates how different intranasal dosage forms affect drug residence time in the nasal cavity, a critical factor for optimizing absorption and brain delivery. Solution formulations retain drugs longer in the nasal cavity than particulate forms, while non-adhesive powders and microparticles are rapidly cleared by mucociliary mechanisms into the nasopharynx and gastrointestinal tract. Incorporation of mucoadhesive polymers—particularly sodium alginate and CMC-Na—significantly extends nasal residence time, with gel formulations providing superior retention compared to powder formulations, increasing retention time by over three-fold in some cases. The site of nasal deposition notably affects residence time, though solution formulations maintain consistent clearance profiles regardless of deposition location. These findings demonstrate that formulation type critically influences drug residence behavior through effects on dissolution, diffusion, and mucociliary clearance, enabling precise control of drug absorption for nasal delivery applications including potential treatment of conditions like endometriosis, migraine, and diabetes insipidus.

title: Innovations in Stress Urinary Incontinence: A Narrative Review
summary:
# Summary

Stress urinary incontinence is a multifactorial condition primarily affecting middle-aged women, with pelvic floor muscle weakness, intrinsic sphincter deficiency, urethral hypermobility, and vaginal atrophy as major pathophysiological contributors. Key risk factors include age, parity, obesity, diabetes, vaginal delivery, and obstetric injury, which alter anatomical integrity and pelvic floor function through effects on the levator ani muscle and external urethral sphincter. Diagnostic innovations including transperineal ultrasound, high-resolution 3D/4D imaging, shear-wave elastography, and standardized questionnaires (PFDI-20, PISQ-12) have improved detection accuracy and assessment of pelvic floor biomechanical properties. First-line conservative treatments comprise pelvic floor exercises and weight loss, with pharmacological options including duloxetine, anticholinergics like oxybutynin, and topical estrogen, while surgical interventions such as tension-free vaginal tapes, Burch colposuspension, sling procedures, and bulking agent injections are reserved for non-responsive cases. Emerging therapeutic approaches including stem cell therapy and laser therapy are being investigated, and recent research has identified biomarkers related to oxidative stress, inflammation, and altered extracellular matrix metabolism as potential contributors to disease pathogenesis.

title: Beyond the Inner Cell Mass: Evaluating the Impact of Inner Cell Mass Visibility on Implantation Prediction in Static Blastocyst Images – A Retrospective Cohort Study
summary:
# Summary

This retrospective cohort study challenges the conventional belief that superior inner cell mass (ICM) visibility enhances implantation prediction accuracy in blastocyst assessment. While ICM morphology and the ICM-to-blastocyst diameter ratio are established markers of implantation potential, the study found that embryologists achieved comparable or slightly higher prediction accuracy (44.14%) when ICM visibility was poor compared to good visibility (39.68%), suggesting that ICM visualization status alone is not a reliable predictor of clinical pregnancy. The researchers identified multiple factors compromising ICM visibility in static images, including blastocyst sphericity, asymmetric expansion, differential fluid distribution, gravitational settling of ICM cells, and variations in ICM-TE development. Beyond ICM assessment, trophectoderm (TE) morphology—including cell junction integrity, cytoplasmic projections, and zona pellucida density—emerged as potentially stronger indicators of embryo viability and implantation potential. The study advocates for artificial intelligence tools capable of analyzing enhanced ICM and TE images across multiple focal depths to overcome imaging limitations and improve morphological feature detection for more accurate embryo selection without requiring repeated embryo manipulation that risks mechanical damage.

title: Patient-level cost analysis of subfertility pathways in the Dutch healthcare system
journal: The European Journal of Health Economics
summary: This cost analysis of subfertility treatment pathways in the Dutch healthcare system reveals substantial variation in fertility treatment expenses driven by patient-level characteristics and technical factors. The study demonstrates that IVF procedures, particularly when combined with intracytoplasmic sperm injection (ICSI), generate significant cost variation due to personalized treatment requirements and differing laboratory resource demands. Key cost drivers include the number of embryos cultured, clinical consultations required, oocytes retrieved, and the specific fertilization techniques employed, with IVF-ICSI cycles requiring notably longer laboratory staff time (445 minutes) compared to standard IVF (313 minutes). The adoption of vitrification and cryopreservation techniques for embryo freezing substantially reduces per-cycle costs, saving up to €1,998 per treatment trajectory through improved outcomes in frozen embryo transfer cycles. In the Dutch healthcare context where basic insurance covers unlimited cycles of ovulation induction and intrauterine insemination, understanding these cost drivers is essential for optimizing resource allocation and reimbursement structures in subfertility management.

title: Integrating O-RADS US v2022, CEUS, and CA125 to enhance the diagnostic differentiation of ovarian masses: development of the OCC-US model
summary:
# Summary

This study developed the OCC-US model, an integrated diagnostic system combining O-RADS US v2022, contrast-enhanced ultrasound (CEUS), and serum CA125 levels to improve differentiation of benign versus malignant ovarian masses. The OCC-US model achieved superior diagnostic performance with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.916, significantly outperforming individual modalities, while maintaining high sensitivity (84.9%) and substantially improving specificity (85.4%) compared to O-RADS US v2022 alone (71.5%). Notably, OCC-US reduced the false-positive rate from 23.1% to 6.2% and decreased unnecessary surgical recommendations from 300 to 243 cases, with an optimal threshold score of ≥3 indicating malignancy risk. Independent predictors of malignancy identified through multivariate analysis included O-RADS ≥4, CEUS score ≥4, and CA125 ≥37.815 U/mL, which collectively optimized risk stratification and surgical decision-making. The model demonstrated high inter-observer agreement (κ=0.840), establishing its clinical reliability for safer, more targeted management of adnexal masses.

title: Time-Lapse Evaluation of Embryos in Non-Obstructive Azoospermia (NOA): High Rate of 1PN Fertilization and Rapid Embryo Development in TESE Compared to Ejaculated Sperm
summary:
# Summary

This study examined embryo development patterns in non-obstructive azoospermia (NOA) patients using time-lapse microscopy, comparing testicular sperm obtained via micro-TESE with ejaculated sperm. Testicular sperm, which originate from impaired spermatogenesis associated with testicular dysfunction or hypothalamic-pituitary axis disorders, showed significantly faster embryo developmental kinetics and higher rates of 1-pronuclei (1PN) fertilization compared to ejaculated sperm, despite lower 2-pronuclei (2PN) rates. The elevated 1PN rate in testicular sperm embryos may result from parthenogenetic activation, abnormal nuclear envelope formation, or delayed pronuclear fusion. Despite these morphokinetic differences and the immature chromatin status of testicular sperm, micro-TESE combined with ICSI achieved pregnancy rates and live birth outcomes comparable to those using ejaculated sperm after hormonal treatment. Male and female age, along with recurrent implantation failure, significantly influenced aneuploidy rates and preimplantation genetic testing outcomes, suggesting that PGT-A screening may particularly benefit older patients or those with repeated implantation failures regardless of sperm source.

title: Immunomodulation and targeted drug delivery with high intensity focused ultrasound (HIFU): Principles and mechanisms
summary:
# Summary

High-intensity focused ultrasound (HIFU) is a non-invasive therapeutic technology that operates through both thermal and non-thermal mechanisms to treat cancer, infectious diseases, and neurological conditions. Thermally, HIFU induces coagulative necrosis and cellular death by rapidly heating tissue above 55°C, while mechanical effects—including stable and inertial cavitation—enhance drug delivery by increasing cell membrane permeability and disrupting vascular barriers through sonoporation. HIFU improves chemotherapy and radiotherapy efficacy by promoting vasodilation, increasing vascular permeability, and augmenting blood flow and oxygen delivery to tumors, with nitric oxide release playing a key role in these vascular effects. The technology enables spatially confined drug and gene delivery across the blood-brain barrier by disrupting tight junction proteins such as claudin-1, claudin-5, occludin, and ZO-1, thereby unlocking applications in glioblastoma and other CNS disorders. Additionally, histotripsy—a mechanical variant of HIFU—creates tissue fractionation for cancer treatment and has demonstrated the capacity to induce anti-tumor immunity, representing a promising immunomodulatory approach in oncology.

title: Intracytoplasmic sperm injection hampers fertilization rate and pregnancy per initiated cycle in patients with extremely poor ovarian response
summary: In patients with extremely poor ovarian response undergoing assisted reproduction, intracytoplasmic sperm injection (ICSI) paradoxically increases total fertilization failure rates and reduces cumulative pregnancy rates compared to conventional IVF, contrary to its intended purpose of preventing fertilization failure in this population. The elevated risk appears driven by ICSI's requirement for earlier denudation of cumulus cells, which shortens supportive in vitro incubation of the cumulus-oocyte complex and disrupts critical cytoplasmic maturation processes. Poor responders are particularly vulnerable to nucleus-cytoplasmic maturation asynchrony—a leading cause of total fertilization failure—due to accelerated oocyte development, and premature denudation exacerbates this by reducing cumulus cell-mediated support necessary for calcium store accumulation and proper oocyte competence. While ICSI remains the gold standard for severe male factor infertility, the study demonstrates that when fertilization failure originates from oocyte quality rather than sperm factors, ICSI does not improve pregnancy or live birth outcomes and may be counterproductive. These findings suggest that patient selection is critical, and conventional IVF should be considered preferentially for extremely poor responders without documented male factor infertility to preserve oocyte developmental potential.

title: Microscaled Cell Surface Proteomics for Cryo-preserved Cells and Tissue Samples
summary:
# Summary

This study presents two complementary microscaled cell surface proteomics methods—N-glycopeptide enrichment (NGE) and wheat-germ agglutinin-horseradish peroxidase (WGA-HRP)—optimized for low-input applications using fresh or cryopreserved cells and tissues. Together, these workflows identified over 700 cell surface proteins, with NGE showing superior specificity and capturing adhesion proteins with lower transcript abundance, while WGA-HRP preferentially detected ion transporters with minimal glycosylation. The study validated both methods by tracking EGFR internalization and lysosomal degradation following EGF stimulation, revealing that chloroquine-mediated autophagy inhibition impairs recycling while maintaining surface depletion. Critically, cryopreservation maintained near-complete overlap with fresh samples and enzymatic dissociation with TrypLE preserved cell surface epitopes, enabling accurate profiling from fewer than one to two million endometrial cells. These optimized protocols establish a robust platform for biomarker discovery and drug target identification in translational settings where sample quantity and quality are limiting, particularly relevant for endometriosis diagnosis and characterization.

title: Evaluation of GPT-4 Accuracy in the Interpretation of Medical Imaging: Potential Benefits, Limitations, and the Future
summary:
# Summary

This retrospective study evaluated GPT-4o's diagnostic accuracy in interpreting radiological images across various anatomical regions and pathological conditions. The findings revealed significant performance variability depending on imaging modality and anatomy type: X-ray images demonstrated 2.21 times higher diagnostic accuracy compared to CT scans, while pelvic imaging showed 6.25 times lower accuracy than other anatomical regions, and neoplastic conditions were interpreted correctly 2.7 times less frequently than bleeding conditions. GPT-4o exhibited particular difficulty with pelvic anatomy identification and demonstrated a bimodal distribution of response quality that suggested heavy reliance on prior similar image encounters rather than genuine image analysis. Critical limitations included AI hallucinations—fabricated image details that negatively impacted diagnostic accuracy—and susceptibility to text-based suggestions that could mislead interpretations. The inclusion of clinical history significantly improved GPT-4o's diagnostic performance, suggesting that while AI shows potential to streamline radiological workflows, current capabilities remain insufficient for reliable independent pelvic or neoplastic condition assessment.

title: Editorial: Reviews in obstetrics and gynecology 2024
summary: This editorial reviews significant developments in obstetrics and gynecology, covering diverse clinical topics from preeclampsia prevention to endometriosis management. Calcium supplementation emerges as an effective intervention for reducing preeclampsia incidence through its role in vascular function and blood pressure regulation, particularly in calcium-deficient pregnancies. Risk stratification for late-onset preeclampsia incorporates elevated BMI, advanced maternal age, and chronic hypertension, with low-dose aspirin showing promise as preventive therapy. The review also highlights misoprostol's advantages for labor induction with reduced need for oxytocin augmentation, romosozumab's effectiveness in fracture prevention through improved bone mineral density, and the association between HIV infection and high-risk HPV complications. Furthermore, the editorial emphasizes integrating mental health support into infertility care, given the significant associations between depression, psychological distress from lack of partner support, and infertility outcomes, while noting emerging evidence for traditional Chinese medicine and natural products in endometriosis treatment.

title: Dosimetric and Radiobiological Evaluation of Inhomogeneity-Corrected Dose Distribution in Prophylactic Radiotherapy for Heterotopic Ossification
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluates radiotherapy approaches for preventing heterotopic ossification, an abnormal bone formation commonly occurring after trauma, surgery, spinal cord injuries, and burn injuries. Heterotopic ossification develops through activation of bone morphogenetic proteins, transforming growth factor-beta, Wnt signaling, and mesenchymal stem cell differentiation into osteoblasts. Low-dose radiotherapy (7-8 Gy single fraction) delivered early post-operatively using advanced techniques like 3D conformal or intensity-modulated radiotherapy effectively inhibits osteogenesis and prevents heterotopic ossification, though long-term risks include secondary malignancies. The dosimetric analysis reveals that inhomogeneity-corrected dose calculations (IC-on plans) more accurately represent biological effectiveness compared to homogeneous calculations (IC-off plans), which overestimate dose conformity and deliver higher doses to target volumes (D50%, D90%, D95%, D98% all higher in IC-off), leading to suboptimal clinical outcomes. While IC-off plans produced higher generalized equivalent uniform dose and equivalent doses, IC-on plans achieved steeper dose gradients for better sparing of adjacent tissues. This work demonstrates that inhomogeneity correction is essential for accurate treatment planning and improved radiobiological effectiveness in heterotopic ossification prevention.

title: Infertility screening in unmarried women: A scoping review protocol
summary:
# Summary

Infertility, defined as the inability to achieve pregnancy after 12 months of unprotected sexual intercourse, encompasses both male and female factors and disproportionately impacts women's psychological and emotional well-being. Clinical infertility screening in females primarily focuses on assessing ovarian reserve and uterine health through integrated diagnostic approaches including clinical assessments, hormonal assays (FSH, LH, AMH), pelvic ultrasonography, and advanced procedures such as hysteroscopy and laparoscopy when indicated. These screening methods collectively identify infertility etiologies and guide therapeutic interventions, though societal stigma surrounding unmarried women's infertility may delay screening and lead to poorer outcomes including missed identification of treatable conditions such as tubal factor infertility and ovulatory dysfunction. This scoping review protocol systematically maps evidence on infertility screening practices in unmarried women, identifies relevant clinical guidelines, and synthesizes screening methods and their advantages and disadvantages. By addressing this knowledge gap, the review aims to develop targeted interventions that improve reproductive health outcomes in this underserved population.

title: A Missed Opportunity: Prioritizing the Development of a Healthy Market Ecosystem for Equitable Menstrual Health Within the International Conference on Population and Development Programme of Action
summary: This article argues that menstrual health represents a critical but underutilized component of the International Conference on Population and Development agenda, with far-reaching implications for reproductive health, gender equity, and economic development. Comprehensive menstrual health access—encompassing information, products, services, and facilities—directly improves educational participation, workplace engagement, and protection from gender-based violence, while lack of access perpetuates poor reproductive outcomes including increased vulnerability to infections and delayed identification of conditions such as endometriosis. Strategic interventions including menstrual health conversations, awareness campaigns, integrated clinical services, and product accessibility improve health-seeking behaviors and enable early symptomatic identification of reproductive tract pathology. The article emphasizes that stigmatization surrounding menstruation undermines psychosocial agency and body literacy, further increasing vulnerability to gender-based violence and limiting participation in social and economic life. Addressing menstrual health through integrated approaches—combining family planning counseling, maternal health support, appropriate water and sanitation facilities, and product choice—represents a necessary foundation for achieving broader reproductive health outcomes and gender equity. The authors call for prioritizing menstrual health within policy and practice frameworks to unlock significant gains in educational advancement, economic empowerment, and elimination of gender-based violence.

title: Preference, adherence and acceptability of three non-medicated intravaginal rings of differing external diameters: a randomized, crossover trial
journal: American journal of obstetrics and gynecology
summary: This randomized crossover trial evaluated how intravaginal ring external diameter affects user preferences, adherence, and acceptability by comparing 46mm, 56mm, and 66mm non-medicated rings. The 56mm ring emerged as optimal, demonstrating the lowest expulsion rates (11% versus 18-45%) and highest adherence while maintaining acceptability scores comparable to the smaller 46mm ring. Although the 46mm ring was preferred by 59% of participants, it experienced significantly higher expulsion and removal frequencies, which could compromise contraceptive efficacy and method continuation. The 66mm ring showed the poorest performance, with the highest expulsion rates (45%), lowest acceptability scores, and reduced ease of use and comfort. These findings validate the 54-58mm external diameter range currently used in marketed and developmental vaginal rings as the optimal balance between user preference, device retention, and acceptability. Ring external diameter substantially influences acceptability through multiple pathways including ease of insertion, comfort, and size perception, suggesting that optimizing device dimensions is critical for improving user acceptance and contraceptive efficacy with intravaginal delivery systems.

title: Visualisation and quantification of the effects of surgical humidification on intestinal perfusion and viability in a porcine model
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluated surgical humidification using warm, humidified carbon dioxide insufflation as an intervention to prevent tissue damage during open abdominal surgery in a porcine model. The research demonstrated that surgical humidification significantly improved core and local intestinal temperatures, enhanced tissue oxygenation, and reduced lactate accumulation in both jejunal and duodenal tissues—markers indicating improved tissue perfusion and metabolic function. The cold, dry operating theatre environment normally causes evaporative cooling and tissue desiccation, leading to reduced oxygenation, hypothermia, and systemic dehydration that collectively impair wound healing and increase risks of surgical site infection and anastomotic leak. Histological analysis revealed that surgical humidification provided significant protective effects against peritoneal and intestinal tissue damage compared to controls. These findings support the clinical application of warm humidified CO2 insufflation as a simple intervention to optimize intraoperative tissue conditions and potentially reduce common postoperative complications in gastrointestinal surgery.

title: Transvaginal ultrasound approach for diagnosing pelvic venous disorders
journal: Journal of Vascular Surgery: Venous and Lymphatic Disorders
summary:
# Summary

Transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) with color Doppler is a highly sensitive and specific imaging modality for diagnosing pelvic venous disorders, including pelvic varicose veins, venous reflux, and post-thrombotic changes. The technique enables detailed assessment of periuterine, perivaginal, gonadal, and iliac veins with superior visualization compared to transabdominal ultrasound, MR venography, and CT venography, achieving 96% sensitivity and 100% specificity. TVUS can identify venous reflux in vessels smaller than 5 mm and detect reflux times exceeding one second, while also characterizing anatomical escape points in the perineal, gluteal, and inguinal regions that contribute to varicose vein formation. The higher frequency transvaginal transducer effectively visualizes internal iliac vein tributaries and valve incompetence, and is useful for diagnosing pelvic pain and investigating unexplained pulmonary thromboembolism. Beyond initial diagnosis, TVUS serves as an important follow-up imaging tool after therapeutic interventions such as gonadal and iliac vein embolization, establishing it as the first-line imaging approach for comprehensive evaluation of pelvic venous pathologies.

title: Integration of Bioengineered Tools in Assisted Reproductive Technologies
summary:
# Summary: Bioengineered Tools for Improving Assisted Reproductive Technology Outcomes

Infertility, a condition associated with endometriosis, polycystic ovarian syndrome, and low sperm quality, significantly impacts reproductive success through impaired sperm motility and oocyte quality. Traditional sperm selection methods based on density gradient centrifugation inadvertently generate reactive oxygen species that cause DNA fragmentation, compromising fertilization potential. Microfluidic platforms represent a major advancement, leveraging natural sperm behaviors including rheotaxis, thigmotaxis, chemotaxis, and thermotaxis to select high-quality sperm while reducing DNA damage and preserving motility and genetic integrity. These bioengineered devices, including the Fertile Chip and ZyMōt Multi system, have demonstrated improved clinical pregnancy rates by minimizing gamete stress during selection and denudation processes. Additionally, microfluidic approaches using surface acoustic waves overcome limitations of traditional cumulus cell removal methods, reducing denudation time without compromising oocyte integrity or fertilization rates. Overall, integration of microfluidic technologies into assisted reproductive procedures offers substantial promise for enhancing embryo quality, developmental outcomes, and ultimately increasing successful pregnancy rates in infertile patients.

title: Longitudinal measurements of NO-mediated vasodilation and physical activity over one year following endometriosis excision surgery: a passive experiment
summary:
# Summary

This longitudinal study demonstrates that endometriosis is associated with both macrovascular and microvascular endothelial dysfunction, characterized by attenuated nitric oxide (NO)-mediated vasodilation, which substantially elevates cardiovascular disease risk including myocardial infarction and stroke. While surgical excision effectively resolves endometriosis symptoms and improves flow-mediated dilation measures, the procedure alone does not restore underlying vascular endothelial function as determined by NO bioavailability. However, resistance exercise combined with physical activity significantly improves microvascular endothelial function over the one-year postoperative period, suggesting that structured exercise programming—rather than surgery alone—is essential for restoring vascular health in endometriosis patients. These findings highlight that endometriosis represents a systemic vascular disorder extending beyond pelvic pathology, and that targeted physical rehabilitation should be considered a critical component of comprehensive treatment to mitigate long-term cardiovascular complications.

title: Is integrative therapy of traditional Chinese medicine and progesterone capsule more effective than monotherapies in oligomenorrhea and hypomenorrhea? Evidence based on a multi-center randomized controlled trial and metabolomic profile
summary: This multi-center randomized controlled trial investigated the efficacy of traditional Chinese medicine (TCM), progesterone capsules, and their combination in treating oligomenorrhea (extended menstrual cycles >35 days) and hypomenorrhea (decreased menstrual flow <30 mL per cycle), conditions often associated with anovulation and compromised ovarian function. The combination therapy (COM) group demonstrated the highest total effective rate at 61.54% after three months, with metabolomic analysis revealing significant alterations in glycerophospholipid and tryptophan metabolism across all treatment groups. TCM monotherapy particularly improved ovarian reserve function in patients with low ovarian reserves, increasing AMH levels and decreasing elevated FSH levels while promoting endometrial thickening and elevating reproductive hormones (FSH, E2, LH). The combination approach provided rapid menstrual regulation with fewer adverse effects compared to progesterone monotherapy, which caused notable side effects including elevated prolactin levels and lacked improvement in menstrual blood flow. Both TCM-containing regimens (TCM alone and COM) effectively improved menstrual flow and clinical symptoms by addressing qi and blood deficiencies, suggesting that integrative therapy combines the progesterone-mediated rapid regulation of hormonal cycles with TCM's systemic metabolic optimization and superior ovarian function preservation.

title: Label-free distinction of implant infection–associated bacterial biofilms by Mueller matrix polarimetry
summary:
# Summary

Bacterial biofilm agglomerates on biomedical implants cause persistent infections by developing antibiotic resistance through complex community structures, with Staphylococcus species predominating on orthopedic devices and Streptococcus and Porphyromonas species colonizing dental implants in temporal succession. Mueller matrix polarimetry emerged as a label-free optical diagnostic tool capable of distinguishing different bacterial biofilm species based on their unique polarization signatures, exploiting differences in biofilm architecture, extracellular matrix composition, and bacterial morphology. The study demonstrated that distinct bacterial species exhibit characteristic Mueller matrix element patterns—particularly elevated m22 values in biofilms compared to uncolonized titanium controls—correlating with species-specific biofilm topographies ranging from loosened structures in S. oralis to compact formations in S. epidermidis and protruding clusters in S. aureus. These polarization differences arise from the physical properties of biofilms including thickness, density, extracellular matrix composition (polysaccharide-rich versus proteinaceous), and bacterial cell size and shape, which collectively introduce birefringent behavior and volumetric scattering. The successful differentiation of bacterial biofilm species by Mueller matrix analysis suggests potential clinical applications for early detection of implant-associated infections while avoiding culture-dependent delays, with the technique's previous effectiveness in endometriosis diagnosis indicating broader applicability for optical biomedical diagnostics.

title: ChatGPT’s role in alleviating anxiety in total knee arthroplasty consent process: a randomized controlled trial pilot study
summary: This randomized controlled trial pilot study demonstrates that ChatGPT-assisted informed consent significantly reduces perioperative anxiety in total knee arthroplasty patients compared to standard consent procedures. Patients in the ChatGPT group showed substantially lower anxiety scores across multiple validated measures—including HADS-A, PAS-7, and VAS-A—both immediately after the consent process and on postoperative day five, alongside reduced postoperative pain scores. Beyond anxiety reduction, ChatGPT-assisted education substantially improved patient satisfaction with preoperative education and overall hospitalization experience. The improvement in these outcomes likely reflects ChatGPT 4.0's ability to provide standardized yet personalized responses to patient questions during the consent process, addressing individual concerns while maintaining consistent, accurate medical information. These findings suggest that AI-driven patient education tools may enhance the informed consent process by balancing standardization with personalization, potentially leading to better treatment compliance and clinical outcomes. While this study focuses on orthopedic surgery, the demonstrated benefits of AI-assisted consent processes could have broader applications across surgical specialties in improving the patient experience and reducing preoperative anxiety.

title: The daily enactment of behavior change techniques among physically active university students with physical disabilities and chronic conditions
journal: Annals of Behavioral Medicine: A Publication of the Society of Behavioral Medicine
summary: This study examined behavior change techniques (BCTs) among university students with physical disabilities and chronic conditions, including endometriosis, which was identified as reducing physical activity by 18-31% compared to healthy peers. The research applied the COM-B model (Capability, Opportunity, Motivation) to understand BCT enactment, finding that motivation and opportunity were strong predictors of BCT usage across 15-24 techniques, while capability predicted only 2 BCTs. Task crafting and goal integration emerged as the most frequently used techniques to promote physical activity, whereas self-monitoring was underutilized. Notable barriers to BCT enactment included situational disruptions such as pain flare-ups and work commitments, with students having physical disabilities showing lower BCT enactment than those with chronic conditions alone. The findings suggest that interventions targeting motivation and opportunity, rather than capability alone, may be more effective at sustaining physical activity participation in populations with endometriosis and related conditions.

title: Follicular sphericity based on three-dimensional transvaginal ultrasound algorithms predicts ovarian responsiveness to in vitro fertilization
summary: This study demonstrates that follicular sphericity, measured by three-dimensional transvaginal ultrasound algorithms, serves as a reliable predictor of ovarian responsiveness and embryo quality during in vitro fertilization. Women with low follicular sphericity—characterized by more elliptical rather than perfectly spherical follicles—exhibited significantly higher oocyte retrieval rates, MII oocyte maturation rates, follicular output rates (FORT), ovarian sensitivity indices (OSI), and implantation rates compared to those with high sphericity. Conversely, high follicular sphericity was associated with reduced oocyte yield and embryonic development outcomes, with a negative correlation maintained even after adjusting for age, anti-müllerian hormone (AMH) levels, antral follicle count (AFC), and gonadotropin dosing. The 3D ultrasound approach using automated software (SonoAVC) overcomes the limitations of traditional 2D measurements by capturing three-dimensional follicular morphology, particularly advantageous in ovaries with multiple follicles exceeding twenty. Antral follicle count emerged as an independent factor influencing follicular sphericity, and the low sphericity group demonstrated higher basal ovarian volumes and greater volume expansion during controlled ovarian stimulation. These findings suggest that follicular sphericity algorithms could enhance the prediction of individual ovarian responsiveness and optimize treatment protocols for IVF candidates.

title: Evaluation of Suburethral Tissue Elasticity Using Strain Elastography in Women with Stress Urinary Incontinence
summary:
# Summary
