Stress urinary incontinence (SUI) is a multifactorial condition characterized by compromised suburethral support resulting from connective tissue laxity and extracellular matrix degradation, which leads to urethral hypermobility and reduced urethral closure pressure. The study evaluated strain elastography as an imaging modality to assess suburethral tissue biomechanics in women with SUI, demonstrating that affected women exhibit significantly elevated tissue elasticity compared to continent controls. Strain elastography achieved excellent reproducibility and effectively distinguished women with SUI from controls, with the midurethral level demonstrating superior diagnostic performance (AUC = 0.813, sensitivity = 0.65, specificity = 0.85, F1 score = 0.72). These findings establish strain elastography as a feasible, reproducible technique for in vivo assessment of suburethral biomechanics and suggest that elevated tissue elasticity at the midurethral level represents a reliable biomarker for SUI diagnosis, offering potential clinical utility for distinguishing affected individuals from asymptomatic women.

title: Artificial Intelligence in the Diagnosis and Imaging-Based Assessment of Pelvic Organ Prolapse: A Scoping Review
summary: This scoping review examines artificial intelligence applications in diagnosing and imaging-based assessment of pelvic organ prolapse (POP), demonstrating that deep learning architectures, particularly convolutional neural networks (CNNs), have emerged as the dominant computational approach for this clinical domain. CNNs are extensively employed for image segmentation and prolapse classification across multiple imaging modalities, including ultrasound and MRI, often enhanced through transfer learning methods such as ResNet-18 and VGG-16. Hybrid models combining CNNs with gradient boosting frameworks like XGBoost have shown superior diagnostic performance, achieving enhanced precision and recall in analyzing two-dimensional transperineal ultrasound data. Emerging Vision Transformer architectures demonstrate particular promise for multi-dimensional and multi-sequence MRI analysis, offering advantages in anatomical landmark localization and multi-label classification tasks. The clinical applications of these AI systems span compartment-specific POP classification, automated segmentation of pelvic structures, and anatomical landmark recognition. The review highlights that implementation of explainability tools such as Grad-CAM and SHAP remains underutilized, representing an important opportunity to enhance model transparency and build clinical trust in these AI-driven diagnostic systems.

title: Evaluating the Gaps in the Diagnosis and Treatment in Extra-Pulmonary Tuberculosis Patients Under National Tuberculosis Elimination Programme (NTEP) Guidelines: A Multicentric Cohort Study
summary:
# Summary

This multicentric cohort study evaluates diagnostic and treatment gaps in extra-pulmonary tuberculosis (EPTB) patients managed under India's National Tuberculosis Elimination Programme, revealing significant delays and diagnostic challenges across multiple organ systems. Extra-pulmonary TB affects various organs and presents diagnostic difficulties primarily due to limited patient health-seeking behavior and the complexity of microbiological confirmation, with patient-related delays accounting for approximately 77% of time before treatment initiation. Different EPTB manifestations showed distinct presentations: lymph node TB typically involved cervical lymph nodes with indolent progression, CNS TB commonly presented as meningitis with disseminated disease, and musculoskeletal TB frequently caused chronic back pain with late neurological complications and maximum treatment delays of 28 weeks. Diagnosis relied heavily on clinical-radiological assessment with complementary microbiological tests including Xpert MTB/RIF, cultures, and imaging modalities such as MRI and CT scanning, though confirmation rates were notably low for musculoskeletal and intestinal TB. The study identified critical gaps including insufficient access to microbiological testing, underutilization of imaging and histopathological techniques, inadequate physician training on NTEP guidelines adherence, and treatment-related adverse effects requiring therapy modification in approximately 37% of CNS TB patients. Enhanced microbiological testing capacity, improved physician training, and greater utilization of diagnostic techniques are essential to optimize EPTB management and reduce treatment delays.

title: DRIVE-KG: Enhancing variant-phenotype association discovery in understudied complex diseases using heterogeneous knowledge graphs
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates that DRIVE-KG, a heterogeneous knowledge graph framework, significantly improves endometriosis variant discovery and patient classification by integrating multi-omics data beyond individual genetic variants. The DRIVE-KG link prediction model identified 66 high-confidence previously unreported SNP-endometriosis associations, with 24.2% of top predictions involving variants linked to body mass index and obesity, 6% to lipid metabolism, and 4.5% to depressive disorders, revealing shared biological pathways between endometriosis and comorbid metabolic and psychiatric conditions. A graph convolutional network trained on DRIVE-KG achieved superior endometriosis patient classification (AUPRC of 0.738) compared to traditional genetic risk scores (AUPRC of 0.679), while also identifying meaningful stratification for adenomyosis and severe endometriosis grades. These findings suggest that heterogeneous integration of genomic, proteomic, and phenotypic data through knowledge graphs captures complex multi-omics relationships more effectively than single-modality approaches, offering promise for accelerating drug development and understanding mechanistic pathways in this historically understudied women's health condition.

title: Photoinduced Immobilization on Two-Dimensional Nano
summary:
# Summary

This study develops a photoinduced immobilization technique using borophene nanosheets for biosensing applications, with specific application to endometriosis diagnostics. Borophene, a single-atom-thick boron sheet with electron-deficient boron atoms, serves as a biocompatible platform that readily undergoes surface functionalization. UV irradiation cleaves disulfide bonds in immunoglobulin G antibodies, generating reactive thiol groups in the Fab regions that form stable covalent boron-sulfur bonds with borophene, resulting in antibody-borophene conjugates with optimal side-on orientation for target binding. These conjugates function as capture probes in a lateral flow immunoassay that detects high-mobility group box-1 protein (HMGB-1), a potential endometriosis biomarker in menstrual effluent, delivering diagnostic results within ten minutes. The technique combines the exceptional structural and electronic properties of borophene with the specificity of antibody-based detection, offering a promising rapid diagnostic approach for endometriosis and potentially other biomarker-based conditions.

title: Whole-body magnetic resonance imaging for cutaneous melanoma staging: A scientific review
summary:
# Summary

Cutaneous melanoma is an aggressive skin cancer arising from melanocytic cells with high metastatic potential, particularly in individuals exposed to ultraviolet radiation. Whole-body magnetic resonance imaging (WB-MRI), when combined with diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI), demonstrates superior performance for detecting bone, liver, and soft-tissue metastases (sensitivity 73%-90%, specificity up to 98%), while offering critical advantages including avoidance of ionizing radiation, high soft-tissue contrast, and suitability for young patients and pregnant women. DWI enhances lesion detection through functional assessment based on water molecule diffusivity and enables detection of non-FDG-avid metastases that PET/CT cannot identify, though CT remains superior for small pulmonary nodules and brain MRI remains the gold standard for cerebral involvement. Emerging technologies including artificial intelligence, radiomics, and deep learning algorithms improve lesion detection rates, reduce interobserver variability, and enable quantitative assessment of tumor characteristics as predictive biomarkers for disease progression. Despite limitations including longer scan duration, higher costs, and need for specialized expertise, WB-MRI findings influenced clinical management decisions in up to 24% of cases, making it an increasingly valuable tool for melanoma staging and assessment of treatment response.

title: Endometrial Cancer: A Daily Clinical Practice Guide for Radiologists
summary:
# Summary

Endometrial cancer is primarily diagnosed through postmenopausal uterine bleeding and is associated with multiple risk factors including obesity, prolonged estrogen exposure, Lynch syndrome, and modifiable lifestyle factors such as high-fat diet and physical inactivity. Molecular profiling reveals four distinct prognostic subtypes: POLE-mutated tumors with excellent prognosis (98% recurrence-free survival), dMMR and NSMP tumors with intermediate prognosis (72% and 74% respectively), and TP53-mutated tumors with the poorest outcomes. Pelvic MRI plays a critical role in staging, utilizing T2-weighted imaging, diffusion-weighted imaging, and dynamic contrast-enhanced sequences to assess myometrial invasion, distinguish endometrial from cervical cancer, and detect metastases, with reduced field-of-view DWI offering superior diagnostic accuracy. The FIGO 2023 classification integrates molecular markers into staging, while 18F-FDG PET/CT effectively identifies nodal and distant metastases, and adjuvant chemoradiotherapy significantly improves survival outcomes. Careful imaging differentiation is essential to exclude mimics such as adenomyosis, benign polyps, and cervical pathology, and to identify synchronous ovarian malignancy, which occurs in up to 30% of cases.

title: Sperm cell empowerment: X-ray-guided magnetic fields for enhanced actuation and localization of cytocompatible biohybrid microrobots
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates the development of sperm-templated biohybrid microrobots through electrostatic self-assembly of iron oxide nanoparticles onto sperm cell surfaces, enabling simultaneous magnetic actuation and X-ray-guided localization for the first time. Rotating magnetic fields induce traveling waves and rolling locomotion in nanoparticle-coated sperm clusters, which move faster than individual cells and can be visualized in real-time using X-ray fluoroscopy through nanoparticle-mediated radiation attenuation. The magnetic clusters achieve cargo loading and drug delivery capabilities with controllable speed determined by magnetic field frequency, while their enhanced acoustic impedance enables detection in medical ultrasound imaging. These biohybrid microrobots offer significant advantages including longer shelf-life, easy fabrication, and full directional control compared to live sperm-driven systems, with potential applications in assisted reproductive technologies, cancer drug delivery, and diagnostic imaging of sperm behavior within the female reproductive tract. The work addresses a critical trade-off between magnetic responsiveness, imaging detectability, and biocompatibility, establishing a platform for precision-targeted therapeutic delivery and improved understanding of reproductive health processes.

title: Does the Surgical Safety Checklist need a co-pilot? Comparing adherence in gynecological surgery through electronic medical records and OR Black Box video observations
summary:
# Summary

This observational study used OR Black Box video technology to evaluate adherence to the Surgical Safety Checklist in gynecological surgeries, including endometriosis procedures, revealing a significant discrepancy between reported and actual checklist completion. Video analysis showed substantially lower quality scores (47% overall mean) compared to electronic medical records (92-95% across phases), with the Sign-out phase demonstrating the lowest compliance at 80%. Critical safety items showed stark gaps: antibiotic prophylaxis indication (11% video vs. 69% EMR), blood loss assessment (4% vs. 69%), surgical site marking (0% vs. 64%), and equipment checks (2-20% vs. 69-71%) were rarely verbally confirmed despite being documented. The Time-out phase achieved optimal compliance when teams huddled around the patient, while the Sign-in phase was typically fragmented and unstructured, and engagement scores declined progressively from Sign-in (86%) through Sign-out (66%). These findings suggest that traditional documentation methods mask actual safety protocol failures, indicating the need for enhanced verification mechanisms such as a "co-pilot" oversight system to ensure genuine rather than superficial checklist adherence in operating room practice.

title: Sustained Benefit of Short-Term Levodopa Treatment on Inner Retinal Function in Patients With Diabetes
summary:
# Summary

Diabetic retinopathy represents a leading cause of blindness in working-age adults, with neuronal dysfunction preceding visible vascular lesions. Early neuronal changes in the diabetic retina involve alterations in glutamate regulation, microglial activation, and neuronal apoptosis, suggesting that oscillatory potential implicit time (OP IT) delays detected by electroretinography serve as sensitive biomarkers for preclinical disease. Short-term levodopa treatment, a dopamine precursor, significantly improved delayed OP IT within two weeks in diabetic patients, restoring measurements to normative ranges and normalizing the structural-functional correlations typically disrupted in diabetes. Remarkably, these functional improvements were sustained at five-year follow-up, with the majority of treated individuals maintaining reduced OP IT below baseline levels despite continued retinal thinning in outer retinal layers. These findings support dopamine supplementation as a neuroprotective intervention targeting early retinal neurodegeneration in diabetes, offering potential to prevent progression to clinically evident diabetic retinopathy and vision loss.

title: Narrative medicine intervention on the obstetric–gynaecological work floor to discuss social stigmas around heavy menstrual bleeding using cocreated site-specific poetry
summary: This study demonstrates that narrative medicine interventions, specifically cocreated site-specific poetry, effectively facilitate meaningful discussions about sensitive gynecological topics such as heavy menstrual bleeding in clinical settings. The research reveals that societal stigma and lack of awareness surrounding women's health issues constitute major barriers to care, leading to embarrassment, misdiagnosis, and inadequate treatment. Arts-based interventions leverage creative work as a "conversational medium" that provides a psychologically safe space for healthcare professionals to explore confrontational topics and develop greater empathy for patients' perspectives and needs. The narrative medicine workshop promoted interprofessional dialogue among obstetric-gynecological staff, helping clinicians see their daily work practices in new light while facilitating meaningful connections with colleagues. By combining creative writing and collaborative discussion, the intervention enhanced professional competencies in addressing sensitive topics and supported the interprofessional collaborative practice essential for delivering high-quality, patient-centered care. These findings suggest that implementing narrative medicine approaches in clinical gynecological settings is both feasible and beneficial for addressing the stigma surrounding women's health conditions.

title: Application of ultrasound in combination with other methods in gynecological disease: artificial intelligence, surgery, and drugs
summary:
# Article Summary: Ultrasound and AI in Gynecological Disease Diagnosis

This article examines the integration of transvaginal ultrasound with artificial intelligence and other clinical methods for diagnosing gynecological conditions, with particular emphasis on endometriosis and related malignancies. Endometriosis develops through retrograde menstruation and immune system dysfunction, causing characteristic symptoms of abnormal bleeding and pelvic pain. Transvaginal ultrasound, including color Doppler imaging, has emerged as a primary diagnostic tool for detecting endometriosis, adenomyosis, and deep rectosigmoid lesions, as well as for assessing myometrial infiltration in endometrial carcinoma staging. The article documents molecular features associated with endometrioid carcinoma, including microsatellite instability and mutations in PTEN, k-RAS, and FGFR2, with immunohistochemical markers like DNA mismatch repair proteins, p53, and p16 aiding diagnosis. Artificial intelligence significantly enhances multiple stages of breast cancer management, including detection, diagnosis, treatment planning, and risk assessment. Combined surgical and radiotherapeutic approaches prevent metastatic spread, while ultrasound combined with biomarkers such as CA125 and CEA improves diagnostic accuracy for ovarian metastases and other adnexal pathologies including torsion and benign tumors.

title: Research trends of global artificial intelligence application in obstetrics and gynecology from 1999 to 2025: a bibliometric analysis based on web of science
summary: This bibliometric analysis demonstrates that artificial intelligence, powered by machine learning and deep learning technologies, has become increasingly integrated into obstetrics and gynecology, with particular emphasis on diagnostic and screening applications. AI systems have achieved remarkable clinical performance, including 90% accuracy in cervical cytology analysis, 96% lesion segmentation accuracy in colposcopy imaging, and 87% diagnostic accuracy with the Gynecological AI Diagnostics model for conditions including abnormal uterine bleeding and pelvic pain disorders. Beyond diagnostics, AI applications extend to prenatal screening for fetal genetic disorders, prediction of surgical complexity and postoperative outcomes in advanced epithelial ovarian cancer, and improvement of pathologist accuracy in detecting serious tubal intraepithelial carcinoma by 10%. The integration of natural language processing further enables prediction of hospitalization duration and survival rates in high-grade serous ovarian cancer patients. These findings indicate that AI-assisted diagnosis and treatment, coupled with AI-driven health management and robotic surgery, represent the primary clinical focus areas as the field continues to expand from 1999 through 2025.

title: Enhancing senior physician-led multidisciplinary team rounds to improve patient care at Wallaga University Comprehensive Specialized Hospital, 2025
summary:
# Summary

Senior physician-led multidisciplinary team (MDT) rounds significantly improve clinical care delivery by enhancing communication, decision-making quality, and patient safety at healthcare institutions. The study demonstrates that active senior engagement in MDT discussions strengthens team collaboration and fosters a culture of teamwork while simultaneously promoting professional development across all staff levels. By integrating insights from multiple specialties, these structured rounds facilitate holistic patient care and streamline coordination across disciplines, thereby minimizing treatment delays and enabling early identification of potential complications. The research shows that 94% of participants recognized the value of senior-led MDT rounds for teamwork and discussion quality, with 97% acknowledging improved decision-making and 80% reporting enhanced patient outcomes. Conversely, the absence of regular multidisciplinary collaboration leads to fragmented care, gaps in treatment oversight, and delays in critical decisions that compromise patient safety. Overall, senior involvement in MDT rounds represents a high-impact strategy for increasing healthcare delivery effectiveness and ensuring comprehensive, safer patient care.

title: Long-Term Tumor Control After Carbon Ion Radiation Therapy Boost in Locally Advanced Cervical Clear Cell Adenocarcinoma
summary: Clear cell adenocarcinoma of the cervix (CCAC) is a rare, highly aggressive HPV-independent malignancy characterized by inherent radioresistance and poor prognosis with conventional treatments. This case report demonstrates that carbon ion radiation therapy (CIRT) offers superior effectiveness in overcoming this radioresistance through multiple mechanisms, including delivery of higher biological doses to tumors while sparing surrounding healthy tissues, triggering distinct cell death pathways, and promoting inflammatory cytokine release that stimulates systemic immune responses including potential abscopal effects. The patient, treated with neoadjuvant chemotherapy followed by mixed-beam radiation therapy incorporating a CIRT boost (38.4 GyE in 8 fractions), achieved complete metabolic response of para-aortic lymph node metastases and complete radiological response of the cervical lesion at follow-up imaging. Clinical data support these findings, showing that chemo-CIRT achieves 81% local control rates at 2 years compared to 70-74% with conventional radiotherapy, alongside superior overall survival rates of 88-98%. Notably, the patient's ATM gene mutation may have contributed to enhanced treatment sensitivity and the rare abscopal response observed. While late toxicities including grade 3 ureteral stenosis and lower-grade sacral insufficiency fracture, vaginal atrophy, and stenosis occurred, the sustained disease control demonstrates that CIRT boost represents a feasible and effective treatment strategy for locally advanced CCAC.

title: Cancer and fertility management: FIGO best practice advice
journal: International Journal of Gynaecology and Obstetrics
summary: Cancer treatments pose substantial threats to fertility through multiple mechanisms. Chemotherapy agents, particularly alkylating agents like cyclophosphamide, cause ovarian follicle damage and premature ovarian insufficiency in women, while pelvic radiation induces uterine fibrosis and myometrial damage that increase miscarriage and preterm birth risks. In males, chemotherapy can result in permanent azoospermia, while emerging therapies such as PARP inhibitors and immune checkpoint inhibitors carry uncertain long-term gonadotoxic effects. Multiple fertility preservation strategies effectively mitigate these risks, including oocyte and embryo cryopreservation, ovarian tissue freezing, and testicular sperm extraction, with frozen oocytes now demonstrating developmental potential equivalent to fresh oocytes. For gynecological cancers, fertility-sparing surgical approaches are safe for many early-stage tumors including borderline ovarian tumors, germ cell tumors, and early-stage cervical cancers, while protective measures such as gonadotropin-releasing hormone agonists, pelvic organ shielding, and intensity-modulated radiotherapy reduce gonadotoxic effects during cancer treatment. Comprehensive counseling regarding cancer treatment effects on fertility and timely discussion of preservation options remain essential for optimizing reproductive outcomes in cancer survivors.

title: Availability of deceased uterus donor: Is it a real case scenario in Spain?
summary: Uterus transplantation offers a treatment for absolute uterine factor infertility (affecting approximately 250 per million people) by enabling motherhood in women with historically untreatable uterine dysfunction. This study examined the feasibility of deceased donor uterine transplantation in Spain, finding that applying extended donor model criteria could substantially increase organ availability from 1.8 to 6.3 potential donors per million population compared to standard donor criteria. Deceased donor sources offer distinct advantages including elimination of surgical risks to donors, access to younger donors with longer vascular pedicles, and shorter graft procurement surgery time, though they present challenges such as warm ischemia exposure and inability to provide hormonal preparation like living donors allow. Key exclusion factors included advanced age (which reduces uterine artery elasticity and increases preeclampsia risk), high BMI, major abdominal surgery history, and menopausal status with associated tissue atrophy. Spain's established presumed consent legislation and leadership in organ donation (49.6 donors per million population) position it favorably to develop deceased donor uterine transplant programs, particularly in regions like Catalonia. The analysis suggests that expanding donor inclusion criteria while carefully screening for medical contraindications could substantially improve uterus availability for this transformative reproductive therapy.

title: Research progress of artificial intelligence in the early screening, diagnosis, precise treatment and prognosis prediction of three central gynecological malignancies
summary:
# Summary

This review examines artificial intelligence applications across three major gynecological malignancies, demonstrating significant breakthroughs in screening, diagnosis, treatment planning, and prognosis prediction. Deep learning models, particularly convolutional neural networks, substantially outperform traditional cytological methods in detecting cervical lesions, with AI-assisted systems achieving diagnostic accuracies exceeding 94% and reducing colposcopy referral rates by up to 20%. Beyond cervical cancer screening, AI technologies enhance endometrial and ovarian cancer detection through radiomics analysis and imaging feature extraction, while robotic-assisted surgical systems guided by AI algorithms reduce intraoperative blood loss and accelerate recovery. AI integration with multi-omics data, including genomic and pathological information, enables precise treatment personalization and early detection of recurrence through continuous monitoring of clinical indicators. These advances address critical limitations of conventional approaches, including the 20-30% missed diagnosis rate of Pap smears and false negatives inherent to HPV detection, positioning AI as a transformative tool for improving outcomes in gynecological oncology across diverse healthcare settings.

title: Impact of Digital Therapeutics for the Management of Adult Patients With Diabetes: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
summary:
# Summary

This systematic review and meta-analysis demonstrates that digital therapeutics (DTx) effectively reduce key metabolic markers in diabetes management across multiple patient populations. Digital therapeutic interventions significantly reduced HbA1c levels, fasting blood glucose, body mass index, and triglyceride levels, with particularly strong benefits observed in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus compared to type 1 diabetes. The mechanisms underlying DTx effectiveness include enhanced glycemic control through improved self-management behaviors, real-time insulin dosage adjustment, increased medication adherence, and reduced treatment burden that promotes patient compliance. FDA-approved DTx products such as Blue Star and Livongo Diabetes Program leverage continuous monitoring capabilities and personalized interventions to extend diabetes management into patients' daily lives. By enabling dynamic adjustment of therapeutic parameters and supporting lifestyle modifications based on behavioral change theories, digital therapeutics hold promise as a comprehensive approach to attenuating disease progression and improving health outcomes in diabetic populations.

title: Discovery and performance of DNA methylation panels for cancer detection and classification in blood
summary:
# Summary

This study develops and validates DNA methylation-based panels for non-invasive cancer detection and classification in blood using liquid biopsy approaches. An 8-marker panel successfully detects tumors across 14 cancer types with 91% average sensitivity and maintains high specificity, achieving an area under the curve of 0.78 in clinical plasma samples. A complementary 39-marker panel identifies tissue of origin with 69-98% accuracy across multiple cancer types. DNA methylation alterations prove superior to conventional protein biomarkers for cancer detection because these epigenetic changes are stable, recurring consistently in specific tumor types and distinct from healthy tissue. In validation studies, the panels correctly classified 87-100% of tumor samples while maintaining low false-positive rates (3.6% in normal tissue), and importantly, the 8-marker panel correctly identified non-cancer conditions including endometriosis as non-tumor samples with 99.9% accuracy in peripheral blood references. These findings demonstrate that methylation-based liquid biopsies offer a minimally invasive approach for cancer detection, classification, and monitoring that substantially outperforms existing diagnostic methods.

title: Anticlustering for sample allocation to minimize batch effects
summary: High-throughput sequencing enables large-scale molecular profiling of endometriosis and other diseases but introduces batch effects that can obscure true biological signals and mask actual disease-related differences. This study proposes anticlustering as an automated sample allocation method to minimize batch effects by distributing samples across batches while maximizing within-batch similarity. The Two-Phase Must-Link (2PML) anticlustering algorithm extends this approach to balance critical clinical variables such as disease stage and menstrual cycle phase while simultaneously enforcing must-link constraints that keep samples from the same individuals together. In comparative simulations, anticlustering outperformed existing methods, achieving superior balance in 84% of variables compared to OSAT and 78% compared to propensity score assignment, while maintaining computational speed and batch balance even with constraints applied. The approach supports both categorical and numeric variables through binary encoding and has already been implemented by the UCSF-Stanford Endometriosis Center for Discovery, Innovation, Training and Community Engagement. This automated batch assignment strategy represents a practical advancement for reducing non-biological variation in endometriosis research and other high-throughput molecular studies.

title: The Efficacy of Pembrolizumab Immunotherapy in the Treatment of Endometrial Cancer: A Systematic Review
summary:
# Summary

This systematic review examines pembrolizumab immunotherapy for endometrial cancer treatment, demonstrating significant efficacy particularly in deficient mismatch repair (dMMR) and microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) tumors. Pembrolizumab, a PD-1 inhibitor that blocks the interaction between PD-1 and its ligands (PD-L1/PD-L2) to restore T-cell activity, showed substantial improvements in progression-free survival and overall survival when used as monotherapy or combined with standard chemotherapy (carboplatin and paclitaxel) or the multikinase inhibitor lenvatinib. Notably, dMMR tumors—which possess high mutational burden and microsatellite instability arising from mismatch repair protein dysfunction—respond particularly well to pembrolizumab, while proficient MMR (pMMR) tumors with lower mutational burden showed poorer monotherapy response but benefited from combination treatment with lenvatinib. The review identifies molecular subtypes associated with endometrial cancer including POLE mutations, TP53 mutations, and Lynch syndrome, along with clinical risk factors such as obesity and hormone replacement therapy, while documenting manageable adverse effects including fatigue, peripheral neuropathy, anemia, and thrombocytopenia. Overall, pembrolizumab represents a promising therapeutic advance for patients with recurrent and advanced endometrial cancer, particularly those with dMMR/MSI-H phenotypes.

title: From Early Diagnoses to New Treatments for Liver, Pancreatic, Gastric, and Colorectal Cancers Using Carbon Nanotubes: New Chances Still Underexplored
summary:
# Summary

This article reviews the potential of carbon nanotubes (CNTs) as innovative tools for diagnosing and treating multiple gastrointestinal and hepatic cancers, which collectively exhibit poor prognoses with five-year survival rates below 20%. CNTs demonstrate remarkable versatility in oncology, functioning as drug delivery vehicles, gene therapy carriers, and enhanced photosensitizers for photothermal and photodynamic therapies while simultaneously reducing toxic side effects compared to conventional approaches. Key advantages of CNTs include their broad electromagnetic absorbance spectrum, ability to specifically target and accumulate at tumor sites, and capacity to facilitate endosomal escape for improved gene therapy efficacy. The article highlights successful applications such as ginsenoside Rg3-loaded CNTs, which enhanced antitumor immunity and inhibited triple-negative breast cancer growth in vivo, and methotrexate-conjugated multi-walled CNTs, which displayed superior anticancer effects at lower doses with minimal hemolytic toxicity. Despite these promising results, CNTs face challenges including poor water solubility, which can be mitigated through surface modifications that increase hydrophilicity and reduce toxicity. Overall, nanotechnology-based approaches utilizing CNTs represent underexplored opportunities to transform cancer diagnosis and treatment for currently difficult-to-treat malignancies.

title: Extracting Symptoms of Complex Conditions From Online Discourse (Subreddit to Symptomatology): Lexicon-Based Approach
summary: This study demonstrates the application of lexicon-based symptom extraction (LSE) and machine learning approaches to identify patient-reported symptoms of polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) from online discourse, achieving superior performance compared to large language models and standard medical extraction tools. The research revealed that PCOS manifests with diverse and varied symptoms that differ significantly among patients, with analysis identifying 28 potentially emerging symptoms and 8 self-reported comorbidities, including brain fog. A critical finding is the association between PCOS's heterogeneous symptomatology, insufficient treatment guidelines, and instances of medical gaslighting, highlighting diagnostic and clinical challenges affecting the estimated 116 million women worldwide with the condition. The study employed BioBERT-based k-means clustering and transformer-based models for natural language processing, with BioBERT demonstrating consistent superiority over BERT-Base and Phrase-BERT variants for symptom recognition. These computational methods achieved a mean F1-score of 86.10, substantially outperforming existing automatic medical extraction tools by at least 20-41 percent. The work underscores how leveraging patient-generated online data with advanced NLP techniques can enhance understanding of complex disease presentations and potentially improve clinical recognition of underdiagnosed conditions.

title: Embryo selection at the cleavage stage using Raman spectroscopy of day 3 culture medium and machine learning: a preliminary study
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates that machine learning combined with Raman spectroscopy of day 3 embryo culture medium can predict extended embryo culture outcomes and embryo quality independently of morphological assessment. The researchers analyzed metabolic signatures in spent culture medium from day 3 embryos using Raman spectroscopy, identifying key spectral peaks at 750, 938, and 1202 cm⁻¹ that correlate with blastocyst development potential. Multiple deep learning models, particularly multilayer perceptron (MLP) networks and ensemble stacking approaches, achieved high predictive accuracy for distinguishing embryos likely to develop into morphologically good versus poor blastocysts. This non-invasive, morphology-independent method offers a promising approach for selecting high-quality embryos at the cleavage stage, potentially reducing the need for extended culture while minimizing associated health risks such as preterm delivery and epigenetic changes linked to prolonged in vitro exposure. The findings suggest that spectral analysis of embryo metabolism could enable more informed embryo selection in assisted reproductive technologies, particularly relevant for endometriosis patients and others undergoing fertility treatment.

title: Opinion on “Artificial intelligence in sepsis early prediction and diagnosis using unstructured data in healthcare”
summary: This article examines the application of artificial intelligence, specifically the SERA algorithm, to early sepsis prediction and diagnosis using unstructured clinical data in healthcare settings. While AI models show promise in identifying sepsis through recognition of clinical variables such as tachycardia, tachypnea, altered mental status, and hypotension, they frequently generate false positives by misclassifying non-infectious systemic inflammatory response syndrome (SIRS) as early sepsis. The SERA algorithm addresses this critical limitation by reducing false positives by approximately 17%, thereby decreasing unnecessary antibiotic administration and mitigating clinician cognitive load and alarm fatigue. However, the article highlights an important gap: whether early sepsis identification through these AI tools causally improves actual patient outcomes remains an unresolved clinical question. The integration of unstructured data into AI-driven diagnostic frameworks represents a significant methodological advance, yet clinicians must balance improved predictive accuracy against the real risks of unnecessary antimicrobial therapy. Overall, while SERA demonstrates substantial potential for enhancing sepsis detection, further evidence is needed to establish its clinical utility in improving patient outcomes.

title: Ovario-protective effect of intra-ovarian injection of platelet rich plasma (PRP) after laparoscopic cystectomy of ovarian endometrioma: a randomized control trial
summary:
# Summary

Ovarian endometriomas damage cortical tissue and reduce ovarian reserve, as reflected by declining anti-Müllerian hormone (AMH) levels, and this damage is further exacerbated by laparoscopic cystectomy through tissue removal and post-operative inflammation, oxidative stress, and fibrosis. This randomized controlled trial demonstrates that intra-ovarian injection of platelet-rich plasma (PRP) effectively mitigates post-surgical ovarian reserve decline by preserving AMH levels in treated patients while control subjects experienced significant AMH decrements following cystectomy. PRP achieves this protective effect through multiple mechanisms including anti-inflammatory and antiangiogenic activities that reduce follicular atresia, stimulation of remaining germ cells and germ stem cells, and promotion of tissue healing without triggering additional inflammatory responses. The intervention showed no short-term safety concerns and both PRP alone and PRP combined with sclerotherapy preserved ovarian reserve, with combination therapy additionally improving antral follicle counts. These findings suggest intra-ovarian PRP injection represents a novel regenerative approach to maintain ovarian function and fertility potential in women undergoing endometrioma surgery.

title: Exploring the Fertility Needs of Women Before and After Oocyte Freezing for Medical and Non-Medical Reasons: A Scoping Review
summary: This scoping review examines oocyte cryopreservation as a fertility preservation strategy, emphasizing the critical role of female age in determining reproductive success. Female age at the time of oocyte retrieval emerges as the single most significant factor influencing outcomes, with optimal results achieved when freezing occurs before age 35, when egg quality, quantity, and fertilization rates are highest. Younger age at cryopreservation is associated with higher ovulatory responses, reduced ovarian stimulation cycles needed, and substantially higher live birth rates, with approximately 15-20 mature oocytes required to achieve one pregnancy. The review highlights that while egg freezing enhances women's reproductive autonomy by bridging the gap between peak fertility and readiness for parenthood—particularly benefiting women with endometriosis concerned about fertility decline and cancer patients facing gonadotoxic treatments—success is tempered by oocyte loss during thawing and variable pregnancy outcomes. Decision-making regarding oocyte freezing is influenced by multiple factors including financial costs, health risks, and procedure success rates, though comprehensive counseling and informed decision-making significantly reduce post-procedure regret. Anti-Müllerian hormone serves as a validated predictor of ovarian response, enabling better patient selection and optimization of fertility preservation strategies.

title: Adjuvant Chemotherapy Necessity in Stage I Ovarian Endometrioid Carcinoma: A SEER-Based Study Verified by Single-Center Data and Meta-Analysis
summary:
# Summary

This SEER-based study with meta-analysis evaluates adjuvant chemotherapy efficacy in stage I ovarian endometrioid carcinoma, a malignancy often associated with endometriosis. Adjuvant chemotherapy using paclitaxel-platinum regimens (cisplatin or carboplatin) significantly improves overall survival and reduces recurrence in stage IC disease, with 5-year survival benefits demonstrated across both low-grade and high-grade tumors. Lymphadenectomy similarly enhances survival outcomes, potentially by revealing occult metastatic disease, and is independently associated with chemotherapy administration. In contrast, chemotherapy confers no survival benefit for stage IA/IB patients, indicating that adjuvant treatment should be reserved for stage IC disease where survival gains are substantial. Adverse prognostic factors including advanced age, higher pathological stage, and poor tumor grade negatively impact survival regardless of treatment, underscoring the importance of grade-specific treatment stratification in stage IC patients.

title: Evaluation of Subcutaneous Contraception for Patient Self-Administration at North Florida/South Georgia Veterans Health System
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluated self-administered depot medroxyprogesterone acetate subcutaneous injection (DMPA-SC) as a contraceptive option for patients at a Veterans Health System facility. Self-administered DMPA-SC significantly reduces administrative barriers by allowing patients to manage their own injections, thereby increasing patient autonomy in contraceptive management. The approach demonstrates improved contraceptive continuation rates compared to traditional clinic-administered methods, reflecting the increased accessibility and reduced burden associated with self-administration. These findings align with World Health Organization recommendations to offer patients the option of self-administered injectable contraception. The results suggest that empowering patients to self-administer DMPA-SC may enhance adherence and persistence with long-acting reversible contraception in clinical practice.

title: Laparoscopic hyperspectral imaging for in vivo detection of the vagal nerve in upper gastrointestinal surgery
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluated laparoscopic hyperspectral imaging (HSI) for real-time, intraoperative detection of the vagal nerve during upper gastrointestinal cancer surgery, addressing the critical clinical need to prevent vagal nerve injury, which can cause severe complications including delayed gastric emptying, pyloric spasm, dumping, and gastroesophageal reflux. The TIVITA® Mini HSI system analyzes wavelength-specific tissue reflectance properties, including hemoglobin distribution, water content, and tissue oxygenation indices, to differentiate the vagal nerve from surrounding anatomical structures. Machine learning classifiers, particularly Linear Discriminant Analysis and Support Vector Machine algorithms applied to reflectance spectra, achieved 84-85% accuracy in binary nerve classification and 74% accuracy in multi-class tissue differentiation without requiring contrast agents or nerve stimulation. Raw reflectance spectra provided superior tissue differentiation compared to preprocessed data, and the study demonstrated HSI's advantage as a non-contact, label-free, continuous real-time visualization method compared to conventional intraoperative neuromonitoring techniques. Motion artifacts and inter-patient spectral variability presented limitations, though the findings represent the first successful application of laparoscopic HSI for vagal nerve imaging during minimally invasive surgery and suggest promising potential for broader nerve preservation applications.

title: Implications of digital fertility tracking for clinical care: a qualitative systematic review
summary: Digital fertility trackers have become widely adopted for managing infertility, tracking conception attempts, and diagnosing conditions like endometriosis and polycystic ovary syndrome, particularly when accessed as alternatives to costly or difficult-to-access clinical care. While these tools offer significant benefits—including improved menstrual cycle documentation, enhanced patient-provider communication, patient education, and affordability—they generate substantial risks when used without adequate medical oversight or strong evidence-based design. Poorly designed trackers with standardized algorithms fail to accommodate users with irregular cycles from endometriosis or other conditions, leading to false confidence, anxiety, self-blame, and a dangerous delegation of healthcare decision-making to patients without professional guidance. The shift toward digital tracking creates conflicts between app-generated predictions and clinical guidance, gendered healthcare labor burdens, and patient distrust when information inconsistencies arise, while privacy and data security concerns remain inadequately addressed. Healthcare providers recognize potential benefits but fear unrealistic patient expectations and increased appointment times, underscoring the critical need for integration between digital tools and clinical care rather than replacement of professional evaluation. Ultimately, digital fertility trackers can empower individuals and improve care delivery only when developed with rigorous medical evidence, designed inclusively for diverse reproductive conditions, and consistently aligned with clinical guidance.

title: Adding Anodal Transcranial Direct Current Stimulation to Mindfulness Meditation Abolishes Mindfulness-Induced Improvements in Emotion Regulation in Fibromyalgia
summary:
# Summary

This study examined how combining mindfulness meditation with transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) affects emotion regulation in fibromyalgia patients. Mindfulness-based interventions alone significantly improved emotion regulation through top-down cognitive reappraisal and broadened attention, with clinical responders showing sustained benefits lasting up to six weeks. However, adding anodal M1-tDCS to mindfulness meditation completely abolished these emotion regulation improvements, likely because increased motor cortex excitability from tDCS interfered with the mechanistic processes underlying mindfulness-based benefits. When sham tDCS was combined with mindfulness meditation, the emotion regulation improvements were preserved, demonstrating that active stimulation specifically disrupted the therapeutic effect rather than the combination approach itself. These findings suggest that while mindfulness meditation effectively targets the imbalance between overactive threat systems and underactive soothing systems in fibromyalgia through prefrontal mechanisms, concurrent anodal tDCS may paradoxically impair these compensatory neural processes despite tDCS alone showing potential for explicit emotion regulation in other populations.

title: Is it time to offer routine screening for iron deficiency in healthy adolescent females?
summary: Iron deficiency is a prevalent condition affecting 10-30% of adolescents worldwide, with particularly high rates among adolescent females, athletes, and individuals from low socioeconomic backgrounds due to increased iron demands during rapid physical development and menstrual blood loss. Iron is essential for multiple physiological processes including oxygen transport, energy metabolism, DNA synthesis, myelin formation, and neurotransmitter metabolism, and its deficiency manifests in diverse symptoms ranging from fatigue and reduced aerobic capacity to cognitive impairment, including memory loss, attention deficits, and depressive and anxiety symptoms that negatively impact academic performance. Current screening methods using hemoglobin testing lack adequate sensitivity (15%), but using serum ferritin thresholds below 30 μg/L or soluble transferrin receptor testing improves sensitivity to 92%, with inflammatory markers like C-reactive protein needed to distinguish true iron deficiency from inflammation-related ferritin elevation. Iron supplementation, particularly with alternate-day dosing regimens using ferrous sulphate, effectively reduces anemia, improves ferritin levels, and enhances cognitive functions such as verbal learning and memory, while also alleviating symptoms like fatigue and restless legs syndrome. Routine screening for iron deficiency in healthy adolescent females is justified not only for immediate health benefits but also because early identification and treatment may reduce the risk of adverse pregnancy outcomes later in life.

title: Advances in precision oncology using patient-derived organoids and functional biomaterials
journal: Frontiers in Cell and Developmental Biology
summary:
# Summary

Patient-derived organoids (PDOs) represent a transformative platform for precision oncology, enabling comprehensive drug screening, multi-omics profiling, and biomarker discovery while maintaining patient-specific mutations and epigenetic modifications that drive tumor heterogeneity and drug resistance. When integrated with functional biomaterials, extracellular matrix mimetics, and organ-on-chip systems, PDOs can model complex tumor–stroma–immune interactions and accurately predict individual patient responses to chemotherapy agents across multiple cancer types, including colorectal, gastric, and breast cancers, with sensitivities reaching 75%. Recent clinical advances have expanded the therapeutic arsenal with targeted agents such as mirvetuximab soravtansine for folate receptor-positive ovarian cancer, immune checkpoint inhibitors like durvalumab for small cell lung cancer, and antibody-drug conjugates including trastuzumab deruxtecan, which demonstrated improved progression-free survival in HER2-positive breast cancer. Emerging mechanistic insights, such as the identification of YBX1 as a platinum resistance driver and the Hippo/YAP1/c-Jun axis in chemotherapy resistance, highlight how PDO-based studies can reveal targetable pathways to overcome resistance and enhance therapeutic efficacy through combination approaches like irbesartan with standard chemotherapy.

title: Time-Lapse Imaging in IVF: Bridging the Gap Between Promises and Clinical Realities
summary:
# Summary

Time-lapse imaging (TLI) represents a significant advancement over traditional static embryo assessment by maintaining stable culture conditions while enabling continuous morphokinetic analysis of embryonic development. TLI reduces inter-observer variability and embryologist subjectivity through objective tracking of dynamic developmental parameters—including timing of cell divisions (t2, t3, t5), blastulation (tSB), and blastocyst expansion (tEB)—which are strongly associated with embryo viability and implantation potential. The technology detects developmental anomalies missed by static evaluation, such as delayed cleavage, irregular blastomere symmetry, and abnormal cytokinesis, which are linked to chromosomal abnormalities and reduced implantation success. Integration of artificial intelligence with TLI data further standardizes embryo scoring, minimizes human bias, and enhances predictive accuracy through algorithms like KIDScoreD5 and proprietary systems such as EmbryoScope and Eeva. Clinical evidence demonstrates that TLI improves implantation rates and clinical pregnancy rates (23% increase reported in one study), while also improving patient satisfaction and understanding of embryo quality. Despite mixed overall findings, subgroup analyses suggest particular advantages in challenging scenarios such as advanced maternal age and preimplantation genetic testing cycles.

title: Variation in Quality of Women’s Health Topic Information from Systematic Internet Searches
summary: This systematic review examined the quality and reliability of women's health information available through internet searches, revealing substantial variation in information quality across different health topics. Widely studied topics with established knowledge bases, particularly pregnancy and sleep, were associated with significantly higher quality articles that cited more scientific sources, while emerging or popularized topics such as gut health, microbiome, and holistic medicine showed lower quality scores and minimal scientific citations. Topics with inconsistent research foundations, notably cortisol and weight-related information, exhibited the greatest variability in article quality, suggesting inconsistent evidence translation to public-facing online content. The study identified concerning patterns where financial barriers to healthcare, medical deserts, language barriers, and experienced discrimination drive women to seek health information online, yet internet sources frequently contain unverified products, reinforce medical mistrust through echo chambers, and contribute to the Dunning-Kruger effect in self-diagnosis. The findings underscore that information quality directly correlates with the depth of underlying scientific research; gaps in academic research are reflected as gaps in reputable online resources. As artificial intelligence tools increasingly mediate health information access, the authors emphasize that internet literacy is now essential for women to critically evaluate the quality and accuracy of women's health information obtained online.

title: The Use of Stem Cells in Assisted Reproduction
summary:
# Summary

Infertility, a multifactorial condition associated with advanced maternal age, endometrial dysfunction, and male factor infertility, represents a significant clinical challenge for assisted reproduction. Stem cell-based approaches, particularly mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs), embryonic stem cells (ESCs), and induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs), demonstrate considerable regenerative properties and show promise in enhancing ovarian function and spermatogenesis. Stem cell transplantation enhances ovarian function by upregulating hormone expression, inhibiting granulosa cell apoptosis, and promoting neovascularization through paracrine activity, while also suppressing inflammation and reducing fibrosis. For endometrial restoration, MSC transplantation and stem cell-loaded scaffolds significantly reduce fibrosis, increase gland density and endometrial thickness, and improve implantation rates in intrauterine adhesion and primary ovarian insufficiency. Menstrual blood-derived stem cells and adipose tissue-derived stem cells offer particularly attractive clinical applications due to their accessibility and regenerative potential. These stem cell-based therapies, when combined with conventional assisted reproductive technologies such as IVF and intracytoplasmic sperm injection, address reproductive dysfunction at the cellular level and represent an emerging frontier in treating previously intractable causes of infertility.

title: The Effect of BeBo® Training and EMG-Biofeedback-Assisted Therapy on Pelvic Floor Muscle Function in Women After Vaginal Delivery and Cesarean Section—A Randomized Controlled Trial
summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated the BeBo® training method with and without electromyography (EMG) biofeedback for restoring pelvic floor muscle (PFM) function in women following vaginal delivery or cesarean section. Pregnancy and childbirth compromise PFM integrity through multiple mechanisms including large neonatal head circumference, high birth weight, prolonged labor, instrumental delivery, and perineal trauma; notably, even second-stage cesarean sections do not fully prevent this dysfunction. BeBo® training combined with EMG-biofeedback produced superior improvements in PFM endurance and the ratio of maximal contraction to resting tension compared to training alone, with the biofeedback-assisted approach particularly effective in the cesarean section group. The comprehensive BeBo® method—comprising awareness, mobilization, strengthening, relaxation, and integration exercises—enhanced neuromuscular function and myofascial flexibility, while EMG biofeedback provided real-time visual feedback via vaginal electrodes to optimize muscle activation patterns. These findings demonstrate that multimodal PFM training with biofeedback assistance effectively reduces stress urinary incontinence symptoms and restores core stability, balance, and sexual function in postpartum women.

title: Knowledge, attitudes, and practices toward assisted reproductive technology and painless egg retrieval among infertile women in the northwest region of China
summary: Among infertile women in northwest China, this study demonstrates that knowledge, attitudes, and practices regarding assisted reproductive technology (ART) and painless egg retrieval are interconnected components of treatment engagement. Education level directly influences knowledge acquisition, which in turn positively impacts both attitudes toward ART and actual treatment practices, with knowledge showing the strongest effect (β = 0.33). Notably, acceptance of ART and painless egg retrieval options inversely correlates with knowledge and practice scores, suggesting that women with lower acceptance may avoid seeking information or pursuing treatment. Multiple barriers to ART uptake were identified, including financial constraints, geographic disparities in healthcare access, cultural hesitancy, and the emotional burden of infertility, all of which discourage active information-seeking and treatment engagement. The findings emphasize that improving patient education about ART success rates, pain management options including sedation safety, and potential complications can enhance psychological outcomes and treatment satisfaction. Clear communication between healthcare providers and patients about personalized pain management strategies is essential to optimize the fertility treatment experience.

title: Assessing the Suitability of ChatGPT and DeepSeekAI for Parent's Education on Common Pediatric Respiratory Diseases
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluated ChatGPT and DeepSeekAI as tools for generating parental education materials on common pediatric respiratory diseases including croup, bronchiolitis, and pneumonia. Both AI platforms demonstrated comparable performance across readability metrics, with content generated at approximately a ninth-grade reading level (ChatGPT: 9.30; DeepSeekAI: 9.22), similar ease scores (~41), and equivalent reliability scores assessed using the modified DISCERN tool. While both platforms proved suitable for generating patient education content and can help parents understand disease presentations, symptom recognition, and treatment decisions, neither tool met the recommended sixth- to eighth-grade readability standard for health education materials. The findings suggest that ChatGPT and DeepSeekAI are comparable tools for supporting public health education and parental decision-making in resource-limited settings, though AI-generated medical content requires accessibility optimization, currency verification, and reliability assessment to maximize clinical utility and patient outcomes.

title: Evaluating ChatGPT as a Standalone Tool for Patient Education: A Review of Frequently Asked Questions by Patients With Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
summary: This study evaluated ChatGPT version 4o as a patient education tool for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), a leading global cause of morbidity and mortality. The AI model demonstrated high reproducibility in COPD-related responses (93.2% consistency across iterations) but achieved only moderate accuracy (61% mean score), with 79.5% of responses being only partially accurate. While ChatGPT-4o avoided outright misinformation, it frequently omitted critical clinical details such as spirometry in diagnostic explanations and the importance of proper inhaler technique and adherence for effective disease control. The research found that oversimplification and incomplete content, even when factually correct, can lead to patient misunderstanding of COPD management. The study concludes that although generative AI chatbots show scalable potential for medical education, ChatGPT-4o cannot serve as a reliable standalone tool for unsupervised patient education but may be useful as a supervised supplementary resource when accompanied by healthcare professional guidance.

title: Molecular classification and fertility-sparing outcomes in endometrial cancer and atypical endometrial hyperplasia
summary:
# Summary

The ProMisE molecular classification system, which assesses mismatch repair (MMR) proteins, p53 status, and POLE gene mutations, provides significant prognostic value for patients with endometrial cancer and atypical endometrial hyperplasia undergoing fertility-preserving treatment. The p53 wild-type (p53wt) subtype demonstrates the most favorable outcomes for fertility preservation, achieving a 79.1% complete response rate at nine months, a recurrence rate of only 19.7%, and the highest pregnancy rate of 57.7%. Conversely, the p53 abnormal (p53abn) subtype exhibits the worst prognosis with a 71.4% recurrence rate, 57.1% complete response rate, and zero pregnancy achievement, indicating unsuitability for hormonal therapy. While the POLE-ultramutated subtype shows excellent short-term response rates and favorable prognosis, the MMRd subtype demonstrates intermediate outcomes with a 30.4% recurrence rate and 31.5% pregnancy rate. Extended treatment duration exceeding 12 months significantly reduces recurrence risk, whereas elevated BMI and p53abn mutations independently increase recurrence risk, underscoring the importance of molecular classification in personalizing treatment strategies for fertility-sparing interventions.

title: Multi-omics prognostic marker discovery and survival modelling: a case study on multi-cancer survival analysis of women’s specific tumours
summary:
# Summary

This multi-omics study developed integrated machine learning models to predict survival outcomes across women-specific cancers (breast, cervical, ovarian, and endometrial), identifying miRNA expression as a consistently dominant prognostic marker. Using Random Forest-based feature selection, miRNA signatures appeared in the optimal feature combination for all four cancer types, with specific miRNAs such as miR-150, miR-9, miR-4662a, and miR-1287 shared across multiple cancer types and 143 gene targets conserved across all cancers. Functional enrichment analysis revealed convergent biological pathways including IL-17 signaling, developmental programs (branching morphogenesis, neurogenesis), epithelial-mesenchymal transition, and cell adhesion mechanisms across all cancers, alongside hormone-driven signaling (estrogen and progesterone) specific to breast, cervical, and endometrial cancers. The study demonstrated that integrating multiple omics modalities—particularly combining miRNA with either gene expression (breast cancer) or DNA methylation (ovarian and cervical cancers)—significantly improved survival prediction accuracy compared to single-omics approaches. These findings highlight shared molecular vulnerabilities in gynecologic malignancies that may reflect systemic inflammatory and developmental processes, with potential implications for endometriosis-related cancer mechanisms and precision medicine strategies for hormone-responsive tumors.

title: Association of State Insurance Mandates for Fertility Treatment With Multiple Embryo Transfer After Preimplantation Genetic Testing for Aneuploidy
summary:
# Summary

State-mandated insurance coverage for fertility treatment is associated with safer in vitro fertilization practices, specifically reducing multiple embryo transfer (MET) by 24% in cycles using preimplantation genetic testing for aneuploidy (PGT-A) compared to states without mandates. While MET increases live birth odds by 70% and improves cumulative pregnancy success, it simultaneously increases the risk of multiple gestations fivefold, which carries substantial maternal and neonatal morbidity including hypertensive disorders, gestational diabetes, prematurity, and cerebral palsy. Single embryo transfer effectively controls multiple gestation risk while maintaining favorable outcomes, particularly when combined with PGT-A, which reduces monozygotic twinning and improves IVF efficiency by selecting euploid embryos. Racial and ethnic disparities exist, with African American and Hispanic individuals more likely to undergo MET and less likely to access PGT-A cycles, while older age and increasing numbers of retrieved oocytes influence transfer decisions. These findings underscore how insurance policy and access to genetic testing can promote safer reproductive practices aligned with clinical guidelines while addressing the competing patient interests of expedited family building and reduced treatment costs.

title: Integrated proteomic and metabolomic analysis elucidates the effects and mechanisms of Qiziyusi decoction on IVF outcomes in advanced maternal age infertility
summary: Advanced maternal age is associated with infertility and poor IVF outcomes, primarily due to age-related declines in oocyte quality and quantity after age 35. The traditional Chinese medicine formula Qiziyusi decoction (QZYSD), composed of twelve botanical ingredients including Rehmannia glutinosa, Cervus nippon antler powder, and Lycium barbarum, significantly improves IVF outcomes in women with advanced maternal age infertility. Integrated proteomic and metabolomic analysis of follicular fluid identified that QZYSD enhances endometrial thickness, increases oocyte retrieval, and elevates high-quality embryo production through regulation of key proteins (C8A, CPB2, PON1, IGHV3-9, VNN1) and metabolic pathways including protein digestion/absorption and pantothenate/coenzyme A biosynthesis. Women receiving QZYSD showed elevated estradiol levels on HCG administration day and improved cumulative clinical pregnancy rates compared to untreated advanced maternal age controls. The mechanism appears to involve QZYSD's mitigation of oxidative stress and modulation of the follicular fluid microenvironment, particularly through glycine, serine, and threonine metabolism pathways. These findings demonstrate that QZYSD is a promising therapeutic approach for improving reproductive outcomes and clinical pregnancy rates in advanced maternal age infertility.

title: Targeted UDP-glucose ceramide glucosyltransferase stable overexpression induces a metabolic switch improving cell performance at high cell density
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates that controlled overexpression of UDP-glucose ceramide glucosyltransferase (UGCG) in HEK293SF-3F6 cells significantly improves transfection efficiency and viral-like particle (VLP) production at high cell densities by triggering a metabolic shift from glycolysis to mitochondrial fatty acid oxidation. UGCG catalyzes the initial step of glycosphingolipid biosynthesis, and moderate UGCG expression levels (10-40 RPU promoter strength) enhanced transfection efficiency approximately 2-fold and improved VLP productivity at densities up to 8 × 10⁶ cells/mL, while excessive expression (100 RPU) was detrimental. Constitutive UGCG-overexpressing cells exhibited increased maximum viable cell density, reduced glucose uptake and lactate production, and lower accumulation of inhibitory metabolic byproducts (ammonia and sodium), indicating more efficient metabolic utilization. An inducible doxycycline-regulated system proved most effective, achieving a 5.5-fold improvement in transfection efficiency and 4-fold enhancement in VLP productivity under high-density batch conditions. Proteomic analysis revealed upregulation of lipid metabolism and β-oxidation enzymes alongside downregulation of glycolytic pathways, mechanistically explaining the enhanced cellular performance. These findings establish that precisely controlled UGCG expression optimizes biopharmaceutical manufacturing by overcoming the cell density effect that typically impairs transfection efficiency at high cell concentrations.

title: ESUR consensus MRI for endometriosis: indications, reporting, and classifications
summary:
# Summary

This ESUR consensus article establishes standardized MRI protocols and reporting classifications for endometriosis diagnosis and surgical planning. While transvaginal ultrasonography (TVUS) serves as the first-line imaging technique and accurately assesses rectosigmoid involvement, MRI is recommended as a second-line modality when TVUS is inconclusive, negative in symptomatic patients, or when assessing candidates for surgery, particularly for detecting deep-pelvic endometriosis in anatomically challenging locations like the uterosacral ligaments and parametrial regions where TVUS has limited precision. Among classification systems, the deep-pelvic endometriosis index (dPEI) score emerged as the most externally validated radiological classification, demonstrating efficiency, reproducibility, and predictive utility for operating time, hospital stay duration, and postoperative complications across multicenter studies. Structured reporting with compartmentalized anatomical review and standardized terminology significantly enhances communication between radiologists and surgical teams while improving patient counseling and ensuring key diagnostic features are not overlooked. The consensus emphasizes that radiological classifications better capture the comprehensive extent of pelvic involvement compared to laparoscopic surgical classifications, which have limited visualization of retroperitoneal spaces and organ internal structures, making MRI-based reporting critical for optimizing surgical planning in this complex benign disease.

title: “How did it go?”—investigation of physician’s experience during embryo transfer and subsequent pregnancy outcomes
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary: This study examines factors affecting embryo transfer success in assisted reproduction, with implications for endometriosis patients undergoing in vitro fertilization. Preimplantation genetic testing for aneuploidy (PGT-a) emerged as a significant predictor of positive beta-human chorionic gonadotropin levels, with more than 2.5-fold increased odds, while procedural factors such as ease of transfer, visibility, mucus presence, and embryo age showed no predictive value for pregnancy outcome. Interestingly, objective transfer characteristics including blood on the catheter and cervical mucus were not associated with pregnancy outcomes, suggesting that embryo quality factors like genetic status are more important than technical transfer parameters. The study found that visibility correlated with transfer ease and was negatively associated with body mass index, though mock transfer procedures did not predict actual transfer difficulty. Structural uterine and cervical features such as tortuosity, endocervical crypts, and polyps are identified as anatomical challenges affecting transfer difficulty, emphasizing the importance of ultrasound guidance, improved embryo selection, optimized endometrial preparation, and atraumatic catheter designs as key innovations for improving transfer success rates.

title: Healthcare Providers’ Perspectives on the Involvement of Mental Health Providers in Chronic Pain Management
summary: Chronic non-malignant pain frequently co-occurs with anxiety and depression, creating complex management challenges that are often exacerbated by reliance on opioid therapy, which carries significant risk for opioid use disorder. Healthcare providers' willingness to integrate mental health professionals into pain management is primarily driven by their beliefs about their own capabilities, their expectations of positive outcomes from collaboration, and the quality of the patient-provider relationship. Provider confidence, reinforcement through peer support systems, and skills training in psychological assessment and support are strongly associated with favorable attitudes toward interdisciplinary pain care models. Systemic barriers in healthcare environments—including limited specialist availability, fragmented referral pathways, and inadequate training in psychiatric aspects of pain management—significantly impede the implementation of integrated care, particularly in contexts like Saudi Arabia. Interventions that enhance provider self-efficacy through skills-building workshops, multidisciplinary feedback, and peer mentorship can effectively foster supportive provider beliefs and promote adoption of holistic, patient-centered approaches that move beyond biomedical or opioid-focused treatment paradigms.

title: Imaging-Based Pre-Operative Differentiation of Ovarian Tumours—A Retrospective Cross-Sectional Study
summary: This retrospective imaging study evaluated magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) protocols for pre-operative differentiation of ovarian tumors, comparing the diagnostic performance of the rFIGO staging system, O-RADS-MRI scoring system, and apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) measurements. Both rFIGO staging and O-RADS-MRI scoring demonstrated excellent discriminatory power for identifying primary malignant ovarian tumors, significantly outperforming ADC measurements alone, with all primary malignancies classified as O-RADS-MRI score 4 or 5. Notably, malignant tumors exhibited lower ADC values (mean 0.71 × 10⁻³ mm²/s) compared to benign and borderline tumors (mean 1.10 × 10⁻³ mm²/s), though this quantitative parameter had only moderate predictive accuracy. However, neither imaging scoring system reliably detected adverse features in borderline tumors such as microinvasion, peritoneal implants, or micropapillary architecture, indicating a significant diagnostic limitation. The incorporation of diffusion-weighted imaging and ADC maps into O-RADS-MRI assessment protocols may help reduce misclassification risk and improve differentiation between borderline and malignant lesions, particularly for indeterminate cases where rFIGO stage greater than I indicates likely malignancy.

title: Oocyte maturation triggers in high-responders: a report on 1,217 consecutive cycles
summary: This analysis of 1,217 assisted reproduction cycles demonstrates that gonadotropin-releasing hormone agonist (GnRHa) triggering effectively manages ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome (OHSS) in high-responder patients while maintaining excellent clinical outcomes. GnRHa triggers induce a physiological dual surge of luteinizing hormone and follicle-stimulating hormone, which promotes oocyte maturation through FSH-mediated mechanisms and reduces OHSS risk by decreasing vascular endothelial growth factor synthesis following the rapid decline in LH levels. However, GnRHa triggering alone causes premature corpus luteum involution leading to luteal phase deficiency; this limitation is effectively overcome by supplementing with human chorionic gonadotropin and estradiol during the luteal phase, which restores implantation and pregnancy rates to levels comparable with conventional hCG triggering. The freeze-all strategy, particularly when combined with agonist triggering, completely prevents severe OHSS while maintaining cumulative pregnancy outcomes, making this approach optimal for young patients with polycystic ovary syndrome or high anti-Müllerian hormone levels. Clinical outcomes with the agonist-trigger protocol matched or exceeded those achieved with traditional hCG triggering across embryological and reproductive measures, establishing agonist triggering followed by freeze-all as the superior treatment strategy for high-responder populations.

title: Adaptive multi-omics integration framework for breast cancer survival analysis
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates that adaptive multi-omics integration significantly enhances breast cancer survival prediction by simultaneously analyzing genomic mutations, gene expression signatures, DNA methylation patterns, and microRNA dysregulation. Breast cancer arises from complex interactions among genetic, epigenetic, and environmental factors that create molecular heterogeneity, with traditional single-omics approaches failing to capture the interactions between these biological layers. The authors integrated genomics, transcriptomics, and epigenomics data through a multi-omics framework incorporating genetic programming and graph-based deep learning methods, achieving a concordance index of 78.31% for survival prediction on the TCGA breast cancer cohort. Key regulatory mechanisms identified include DNA methylation and miRNA suppression of gene expression, which collectively influence protein production and cancer progression. The proposed adaptive multi-omics integration framework outperforms previous methods by identifying complex molecular patterns and robust biomarkers that better stratify patients and predict therapeutic response, offering an improved approach to personalized cancer management.

title: Advancing women’s health: innovative applications of sacral neuromodulation in pelvic floor dysfunctions
summary:
# Summary

Sacral neuromodulation (SNM) represents an innovative neuromodulatory therapy that addresses a spectrum of pelvic floor dysfunctions by electrically stimulating the S3 nerve root, thereby modulating both peripheral and central neural pathways involved in micturition and continence. The treatment works by activating pelvic afferent pathways and enhancing parasympathetic nervous system activity, which reduces bladder hypersensitivity, detrusor overactivity, and urgency symptoms while simultaneously enhancing bladder emptying and external anal sphincter tone. SNM demonstrates high clinical efficacy across multiple conditions including overactive bladder, urinary retention, urgency urinary incontinence, fecal incontinence, and chronic pelvic pain, with landmark studies reporting 85% therapeutic success rates and significant improvements in quality of life measures.
