Novel technological advances, including variable-frequency stimulation protocols that outperform traditional constant-frequency approaches, CT-guided and ultrasound-guided electrode placement, and remote programming systems, have substantially improved surgical precision, patient compliance, and accessibility. Additionally, emerging evidence suggests SNM may activate anti-inflammatory pathways and provide benefits for female sexual dysfunction, positioning it as a transformative treatment option for women experiencing complex pelvic floor dysfunctions.

title: A web-based intervention to promote healthy lifestyles in women under 45 years: a randomized controlled trial (RCT) for breast cancer prevention
summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated a web-app-based intervention designed to promote healthy lifestyle behaviors for breast cancer prevention in women under 45 years. The study demonstrated that approximately 31.2% of new breast cancer cases are attributable to modifiable risk behaviors, with healthy diet, regular physical activity, and maintenance of healthy body weight serving as critical protective factors. The web-app intervention, based on the Behavior Change Wheel Model, successfully improved adherence to both dietary recommendations and physical activity guidelines in the intervention group compared to controls, with dietary behavior improvements being more pronounced than physical activity changes. Notably, previous adherence to health recommendations predicted post-intervention adherence, suggesting that baseline behavioral patterns influenced treatment response. The findings support the effectiveness of digital technology-based interventions in facilitating lifestyle behavior change through educational components that enhance psychological capability. This study provides evidence that web-app platforms can be effective tools for implementing scalable, accessible breast cancer prevention strategies targeting modifiable risk factors in young women.

title: Efficacy and safety evaluation of a reusable advanced hemostatic device (Vi-Sealer) during total laparoscopic hysterectomy in South Korea (KGOG4009/Vi-TLH trial): study protocol for a multicenter, open-label, non-inferiority randomized controlled trial
summary: The Vi-TLH trial is a multicenter randomized controlled trial designed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of the Vi-Sealer, a reusable advanced hemostatic device, against LigaSure and other established hemostatic devices during total laparoscopic hysterectomy for benign gynecologic disease. Advanced hemostatic devices, including ultrasonic and electrothermal bipolar energy devices, achieve vessel sealing through distinct mechanisms—ultrasonic devices denature tissue proteins while electrothermal devices use high-current, low-voltage bipolar energy—and can safely seal vessels up to 7 mm in diameter with minimal thermal damage to surrounding tissues. Previous evidence demonstrates that advanced hemostatic devices reduce operative time and blood loss by up to 25%, providing the clinical rationale for comparing the Vi-Sealer's performance in hemostasis and tissue transection. The study protocol includes a cost analysis component alongside efficacy and safety assessments, addressing both clinical and economic considerations relevant to surgical practice. By comparing a reusable device (Vi-Sealer) against established alternatives through a non-inferiority design, this trial aims to determine whether the Vi-Sealer offers equivalent or superior outcomes while potentially reducing healthcare costs through device reusability.

title: Development and validation of an 18F-FDG PET/CT radiomic nomogram for predicting axillary lymph-node status after neoadjuvant chemotherapy for breast cancer: a multicenter study
summary:
# Summary

This multicenter study developed and validated an 18F-FDG PET/CT radiomics nomogram to predict axillary lymph-node status in breast cancer patients undergoing neoadjuvant chemotherapy, addressing a clinically important question since accurate assessment could potentially reduce the morbidity associated with axillary lymph-node dissection. The researchers used tumor-to-liver ratio (TLR) radiomics rather than conventional standardized uptake value metrics, combined with batch harmonization methods (Combat and Limma) to address technical variability inherent in multicenter imaging data. Data preprocessing through discretization techniques, particularly fixed bin width with 32 bins, significantly enhanced model robustness and stability. The optimal radiomics model achieved an AUC of 0.798 for predicting axillary lymph-node status, which improved to 0.811 when combined with clinical characteristics including estrogen receptor, progesterone receptor, HER2 status, and molecular subtype. These findings demonstrate that properly harmonized radiomics features extracted from pre-treatment 18F-FDG PET/CT imaging can effectively predict treatment response and lymph-node involvement after neoadjuvant chemotherapy, potentially enabling more personalized surgical planning and reducing patient morbidity.

title: Navigating fertility care in the telehealth era: association of consultation mode with patient engagement and pregnancy outcomes
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary:
# Summary

This study examined whether initial fertility consultations conducted via telehealth versus in-person visits affected patient engagement and pregnancy outcomes. Telehealth consultations were associated with comparable ongoing pregnancy rates (44.4%) to in-person visits (47.1%), demonstrating that initial telehealth visits do not compromise fertility treatment outcomes. However, patients who had in-person consultations were 55% more likely to complete diagnostic testing, though telehealth patients who did initiate testing were 18% more likely to achieve pregnancy and complete diagnostic workup. Telehealth utilization was higher among patients with higher education, female gender, urban residence, and private insurance, and telehealth patients presented with lower ovarian reserve markers (lower AMH and AFC, higher FSH) compared to in-person visitors. Overall, the findings suggest that telehealth represents an effective tool to increase access to fertility care and reduce barriers to treatment initiation while maintaining equivalent pregnancy outcomes, supporting continued insurance reimbursement for remote reproductive consultations.

title: Enhancing frozen-thawed embryo transfer outcomes and treatment personalization through machine learning models
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary:
# Summary

This study develops machine learning models to predict frozen-thawed embryo transfer (FET) outcomes and enable personalized treatment strategies for infertility patients. The XGBoost algorithm demonstrated superior performance with an area under the curve of 0.7904 using female-specific features and 0.7895 with combined male and female features, significantly outperforming random forest, logistic regression, and deep neural networks. Key predictors of clinical pregnancy included younger female and male age, higher anti-Müllerian hormone and antral follicle count, lower basal follicle-stimulating hormone, better embryo quality (particularly 4AA blastocysts), and use of the GnRH-a long protocol, while male-specific features alone had limited predictive value. The study validated these models on an external cohort and demonstrated through decision curve analysis that XGBoost provided optimal clinical utility with the best calibration characteristics. These findings suggest that machine learning-based prediction models integrating clinical and embryological factors can refine treatment protocols and enhance pregnancy success rates through precision medicine approaches in reproductive medicine.

title: Automatic uterus segmentation in transvaginal ultrasound using U-Net and nnU-Net
summary:
# Summary

Transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) is essential for diagnosing reproductive pathologies, but conditions like endometriosis and adenomyosis remain difficult to identify reliably, creating a need for automated segmentation approaches. This study compared two deep learning models, U-Net and nnU-Net, for automated uterus segmentation in TVUS images from a dataset of 124 adenomyosis patients, testing their performance across different imaging modalities including still images, video screenshots, and three-dimensional volume screenshots. The nnU-Net model significantly outperformed U-Net across all imaging types, achieving superior performance with a Dice score of 0.9689 ± 0.002 on three-dimensional screenshots, demonstrating greater stability and faster training without the need for manual hyperparameter tuning. The nnU-Net's self-configuring framework enabled it to adapt more effectively to different data distributions, whereas U-Net required careful image resolution optimization and showed limited benefit from standard preprocessing and augmentation techniques. These findings establish the feasibility of automated uterus segmentation on TVUS scans and position the nnU-Net as a recommended framework for supporting clinicians in gynecological imaging interpretation, potentially reducing diagnostic delays in complex reproductive pathologies.

title: Summary from the 2025 International Society for Magnetic Resonance in Medicine workshop on body MRI: Unsolved problems and unmet needs
summary:
# Summary: MRI Advances for Body Imaging and Endometriosis Diagnosis

Current pelvic MRI protocols face significant technical challenges, particularly motion artifacts that degrade image quality and obscure small lesions characteristic of deep pelvic endometriosis. Advanced quantitative MRI techniques are emerging as valuable tools for tissue characterization: T1 and T2 mapping enable assessment of organ function and fibrosis, while multiparametric MR elastography can distinguish between fibrosis and inflammation by measuring tissue elasticity, viscosity, and structural heterogeneity. Apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) measurements have proven useful for disease staging and treatment monitoring across multiple cancer types, suggesting potential applications in characterizing endometriotic lesions. Innovative contrast agents such as ferumoxytol offer direct evaluation of macrophages and inflammation, which are hallmark features of endometriosis pathology. Artificial intelligence represents a transformative solution to these diagnostic challenges, with applications spanning motion correction, artifact mitigation, image acquisition optimization, organ segmentation, and lesion detection. Integration of these AI-enhanced quantitative imaging approaches could substantially improve detection and characterization of endometriotic disease while streamlining clinical workflow.

title: Origami Crane Training Improves Laparoscopic Surgical Skills for Gynecological Surgery
summary: This study demonstrates that origami crane training significantly enhances laparoscopic surgical skills in gynecological procedures by improving bimanual dexterity, hand-eye coordination, and depth perception. Surgeons who completed the training protocol showed measurable improvements in intraoperative performance, including reduced vaginal cuff suturing time and fewer technical errors in the second half of their training cohort compared to the first half. The research confirms that surgical volume correlates with better operative outcomes, with high-volume surgeons demonstrating shorter operative times and reduced blood loss during procedures such as hysterectomy. The study notes that vaginal cuff dehiscence remains a notable complication associated with total laparoscopic hysterectomy, a technically demanding procedure requiring both precision and speed. Additionally, the research acknowledges that endometriosis cases present particular challenges due to significant variations in gynecological tumor size and tissue adhesion, which may affect standardization of surgical approaches. Time-trial based training methods offer distinct advantages in maintaining motivation and establishing clearly defined performance goals during long-term skill development.

title: Artificial intelligence for ovarian cancer diagnosis via ultrasound: a systematic review and quantitative assessment of model performance
summary:
# Artificial Intelligence for Ovarian Cancer Diagnosis via Ultrasound: Key Findings

Ovarian cancer remains a leading cause of cancer-related mortality in women, with diagnosis challenging due to limited transvaginal ultrasound resolution and radiologist interpretation variability; however, early detection dramatically improves five-year survival rates from 20–30% in advanced stages to 80–95% in early stages. Artificial intelligence models, particularly convolutional neural networks, have demonstrated promising diagnostic performance for ovarian cancer detection on ultrasound imaging, achieving mean accuracy of 92.3%, sensitivity of 91.6%, and specificity of 90.1%, with automated segmentation significantly outperforming manual approaches. Despite these high reported accuracies, the evidence reveals critical methodological limitations: most studies lack prospective validation, rely on small single-institution datasets, and suffer from inadequate external validation, leading to overfitting and restricted generalizability. AI model performance substantially declines when applied to populations with different ethnic, geographic, or technological characteristics, and performance does not meaningfully correlate with dataset size, suggesting that data quality and segmentation methodology are more important than volume. The clinical translation of AI-based ultrasound diagnostics requires prospective multicenter studies, standardized validation protocols (TRIPOD-AI and STARD-AI guidelines), and integration into clinical workflows before these tools can meaningfully reduce diagnostic inconsistencies and improve patient outcomes.

title: Rapid and minimally invasive preimplantation genetic testing for aneuploidies (PGT-A) based on polar body and nanopore sequencing: a viable alternative to conventional trophectoderm-based PGT-A?
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluates polar body (PB)-based preimplantation genetic testing for aneuploidy (PGT-A) using third-generation nanopore sequencing as an alternative to conventional trophectoderm biopsy. Trophectoderm biopsy suffers from embryonic mosaicism, which leads to false-positive aneuploidy diagnoses and reduces cumulative live birth rates; in contrast, polar body analysis eliminates mosaic artifacts by analyzing single cells from the first and second polar bodies, thereby detecting maternal meiotic errors while avoiding embryo damage and permitting cleavage-stage embryo transfer. Nanopore sequencing achieved high accuracy for chromosomal aneuploidy detection with rapid 9-hour turnaround times, and simultaneous biopsy and co-amplification of both PB1 and PB2 yielded the highest euploidy rate (61.11%) with minimal mosaicism. The concordance rate between nanopore and conventional next-generation sequencing was 96.81%, and PB-based analysis demonstrated higher overall euploidy rates (55.4%) compared to blastocyst-stage PGT-A (43.1%), with notably higher concordance in advanced maternal age patients where maternal meiotic errors predominate. These findings support polar body-based PGT-A with nanopore sequencing as a viable, minimally invasive alternative that reduces unnecessary embryo discard and improves reproductive outcomes.

title: Could artificial intelligence-powered colonoscopies change the future of colorectal cancer screening?
summary:
# Summary

Colorectal cancer ranks as a leading cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide, accounting for approximately 9.6% of new cancer cases and 9.3% of cancer deaths, yet conventional colonoscopy misses up to 22% of polyps during screening. AI-powered colonoscopy systems using deep learning algorithms significantly enhance adenoma detection rates by analyzing real-time endoscopic images, enabling earlier intervention and improved patient outcomes compared to standard procedures. Multiple advanced machine learning approaches, including gradient boosting machines, random forests, neural networks, and computer-aided detection (CADe) algorithms, have demonstrated substantial improvements in both adenoma and polyp detection. Technical innovations such as dilated residual convolution, transfer learning, atrous spatial pyramid pooling, and vision transformer architectures further optimize polyp segmentation and feature capture. Additionally, emerging applications of artificial intelligence, including ChatGPT-based systems, show promise in improving surveillance management compliance and screening interval recommendations, while complementary biomarkers like stool DNA testing, SEPTIN9 methylation, and microbiota profiles (Fusobacterium nucleatum, Proteobacteria) provide additional diagnostic support. These integrated AI-assisted approaches address critical limitations of conventional colonoscopy, such as operator-dependent variability and inadequate bowel preparation, potentially transforming colorectal cancer screening effectiveness and reducing cancer-related mortality.

title: Return rates for the use of ovarian tissue cryopreserved prior to gonadotoxic treatment as fertility preservation: a systematic review
summary: Gonadotoxic treatments including chemotherapy, radiation therapy, and hematopoietic stem cell transplantation cause premature ovarian insufficiency and infertility in cancer patients and those undergoing treatment for benign conditions. Ovarian tissue cryopreservation (OTC), involving surgical removal of ovarian tissue prior to treatment and subsequent autologous transplantation, serves as an effective fertility preservation strategy with live birth rates ranging from 25% to 41% following tissue reimplantation. Return rates for using preserved tissue vary significantly by patient age and cancer type, with women aged 30 years or older showing higher utilization rates (approximately 15%) compared to younger patients, and those with breast cancer demonstrating lower return rates (18%) than those with gastrointestinal or gynecological malignancies. Young age at cancer treatment, pregnancy achievement, and fear of disease recurrence influence decisions regarding tissue utilization, while psychosocial factors such as reduced likelihood of marriage among cancer survivors affect fertility planning. Slow freezing remains the standard cryopreservation technique, though vitrification is emerging as an alternative approach. This systematic review demonstrates that OTC is a viable fertility preservation option recognized by major reproductive medicine societies since 2019, particularly for patients at high risk of treatment-induced ovarian failure.

title: Multi-omics strategies for biomarker discovery and application in personalized oncology
summary:
# Summary

Multi-omics approaches integrating genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics, and epigenomics have become fundamental to biomarker discovery and personalized cancer treatment. Key clinical applications include tumor mutational burden as a predictor of pembrolizumab response, gene expression signatures like Oncotype DX and MammaPrint for breast cancer stratification, and epigenetic biomarkers such as MGMT promoter methylation in glioblastoma that predict temozolomide benefit. Post-translational modifications including phosphorylation, acetylation, and ubiquitination regulate protein function and serve as biomarker targets, while metabolomic alterations like 2-hydroxyglutarate in IDH-mutant gliomas provide both diagnostic and therapeutic insights. Advanced technologies including single-cell multi-omics, spatial transcriptomics, and proteomics now enable characterization of tumor heterogeneity at unprecedented resolution. Comprehensive cancer databases (DriverDBv4, GliomaDB, HCCDBv2) combined with standardized batch correction methods and reference material initiatives (GIAB, MAQC/SEQC, CPTAC) support the development of reproducible, clinically validated multi-omics biomarkers for precision oncology.

title: Barriers to translating continuous monitoring technologies for preventative medicine
summary: This review examines barriers to translating continuous monitoring technologies into preventative medicine applications across diverse clinical domains. The article surveys multiple monitoring approaches including wearable devices (continuous glucose and blood pressure monitors), imaging technologies (RGB and infrared cameras, remote photoplethysmography), radiofrequency sensors, and population-level surveillance methods (wastewater analysis, search-engine data mining). Radiofrequency-based sensors show particularly broad clinical applicability, having been tested for monitoring Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's disease, endometriosis, Crohn's disease, atopic dermatitis, and other conditions. While individual-level technologies like continuous glucose monitoring have demonstrated clear benefits for disease management and quality of life, and population-level approaches have proven effective for tracking infectious disease spread and substance abuse, the article highlights significant implementation challenges in translating these technologies to routine preventative healthcare. The gap between technological capability and clinical deployment reflects practical, regulatory, and adoption barriers that must be addressed to realize the full potential of continuous monitoring in preventative medicine.

title: Integrating interval jump rope exercise into a school setting improve body composition, cardiorespiratory fitness and glycolipid metabolism parameters in adolescents with overweight and obesity: a randomized controlled trial
summary: This randomized controlled trial demonstrates that school-based interval jump rope exercise significantly improves multiple metabolic and fitness parameters in adolescents with overweight and obesity. The intervention produced substantial gains in body composition (reducing body mass, BMI, and body fat), cardiorespiratory fitness, and glycolipid metabolism markers including fasting blood glucose, fasting insulin, and triglycerides. High-intensity interval training-based jump rope exercise (JRE-2) proved more effective than moderate-intensity protocols (JRE-1), with JRE-2 reducing low-density lipoprotein cholesterol and producing greater reductions in body mass and BMI. These findings align with the mechanistic understanding that exercise reduces chronic low-grade inflammation while enhancing cardiovascular fitness and muscle strength—protective effects particularly important given that childhood obesity is strongly linked to cardiovascular risk factors and disease development. The study supports jump rope as a practical, efficient exercise modality that can be readily implemented in school settings to combat metabolic dysfunction in adolescent populations.

title: Radiology-based artificial intelligence for predicting targeted therapy response in pan-cancer: a comprehensive review
summary:
# Summary

This comprehensive review examines how artificial intelligence applied to medical imaging can predict targeted therapy response across multiple cancer types. Targeted therapies improve survival and quality of life by inhibiting specific oncogenic drivers, yet treatment efficacy is complicated by tumor heterogeneity, resistance mechanisms, mutation subtypes, and tumor-microenvironment interactions that collectively influence therapeutic sensitivity. Medical imaging offers a non-invasive, repeatable approach to characterize tumor biology, and artificial intelligence has transformed imaging analysis from qualitative assessment to quantitative prediction through both direct strategies (using AI to learn phenotypic signatures reflecting drug sensitivity) and indirect strategies (inferring actionable molecular alterations like EGFR mutations and ALK rearrangements). The review covers diverse AI methodologies including machine learning algorithms (random forest, support vector machines), deep learning architectures (CNNs, RNNs, transformers, graph neural networks), and radiomics frameworks that extract features from CT, MRI, PET, and ultrasound imaging, with emerging multimodal approaches integrating imaging with clinical and molecular data. Lung and breast cancers represent the most studied applications, though work has expanded to gastric, colorectal, and renal cell carcinomas, with specific models like EfficientNetV2-L for NSCLC and DeepTEPP for HER2+ breast cancer demonstrating clinical promise. Key challenges include ensuring reproducibility, robustness against domain shift, proper model calibration, and adequate interpretability through explainable AI techniques such as saliency maps, attention heatmaps, and SHAP analyses.

title: Overcoming Oral Cavity Barriers for Peptide Delivery Using Advanced Pharmaceutical Techniques and Nano-Formulation Platforms
summary:
# Summary

Therapeutic peptides offer high specificity and potency but face significant bioavailability challenges due to susceptibility to proteolytic degradation by gastric enzymes (pepsin), intestinal endopeptidases (trypsin, chymotrypsin), and salivary enzymes, as well as their large molecular size and hydrophilicity that restrict passive diffusion across epithelial barriers. The oral cavity, particularly the buccal and sublingual mucosa, represents a promising alternative delivery route due to high permeability, dense vascularization enabling rapid systemic absorption while bypassing hepatic first-pass metabolism, though this route is hindered by rapid salivary clearance and enzymatic degradation. Nanoparticle-based delivery systems—including lipid-based carriers (liposomes, niosomes) and polymeric platforms (chitosan, PLGA)—overcome these barriers by protecting peptides from enzymatic degradation, enhancing mucoadhesion to prolong residence time, and improving mucosal penetration through engineered size and surface properties. Niosomes demonstrate particular promise, exhibiting high encapsulation efficiency and sustained release potential suitable for oral peptide delivery. These nano-formulation strategies, exemplified by approved therapies like oral semaglutide, demonstrate the growing viability of peptide administration through non-invasive routes.

title: The Diagnostic Accuracy of Magnetic Resonance Imaging Versus Transvaginal Ultrasound in Deep Infiltrating Endometriosis and Their Impact on Surgical Decision-Making: A Systematic Review
summary:
# Summary

Deep infiltrating endometriosis (DIE) is a chronic condition arising from complex endocrine, inflammatory, and immune mechanisms involving local estrogen production, progesterone resistance, and neuroangiogenesis, which collectively cause fibrosis, adhesion formation, and pain hypersensitivity in affected women. Both transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) serve as primary diagnostic modalities for DIE, but with complementary strengths: TVUS demonstrates high accuracy and specificity for posterior compartment disease, particularly rectosigmoid lesions, while MRI provides superior sensitivity for anterior compartment, multi-compartmental, uterosacral ligament, and bladder lesions due to its multiplanar imaging and broader anatomical coverage. TVUS performance is heavily dependent on operator expertise and standardized protocols such as the IDEA framework and 3D imaging, whereas MRI offers greater reproducibility and consistency across different practitioners. Imaging results directly inform surgical decision-making by enabling preoperative planning, appropriate multidisciplinary team assignment (including colorectal and urological surgeons), and accurate lesion localization for anastomotic planning, with evidence showing that MRI addition to standard workup significantly improves surgical team assignment. The integration of both modalities, supported by standardized frameworks, enhances diagnostic confidence and enables optimal surgical outcomes in managing this complex condition.

title: Intraoperative Platelet-Rich Plasma Application Improves Scar Healing After Cesarean Section: A Prospective Observational Pilot Study
summary: This prospective pilot study demonstrates that intraoperative platelet-rich plasma (PRP) application significantly improves cesarean wound healing through multiple biological pathways. PRP enhanced scar healing by accelerating epithelialization, reducing inflammation, and promoting extracellular matrix deposition and collagen remodeling, mechanisms mediated by growth factors including PDGF, TGF-β, VEGF, and EGF. Clinical outcome measures showed marked improvements by postoperative day 40, with scar scores decreasing across all scales (POSAS, Vancouver, Manchester, and REEDA), pain scores declining substantially (VAS and NRS), and hematological recovery including elevated hemoglobin and hematocrit with reduced leukocyte counts. Exploratory analyses revealed important correlations: hemoglobin and platelet counts correlated with scar aesthetics, while leukocyte counts associated with inflammatory markers. PRP appeared well-tolerated and contributed to neurosensory modulation and nociceptive suppression, potentially explaining reduced postoperative discomfort. These findings support PRP as a promising adjunctive intervention for optimizing scar outcomes and postoperative recovery after cesarean delivery.

title: Natural procreative technology (NaProTechnology) for infertility: take-home baby rate and clinical outcomes in a 5-year single-center cohort of 1,310 couples
summary:
# Summary

This single-center study evaluated Natural Procreative Technology (NaProTechnology), a systematic approach employing the Creighton Model FertilityCare System to diagnose and treat infertility through menstrual cycle biomarkers, in a cohort of 1,310 infertile couples followed over five years. NaProTechnology achieved notably high take-home baby rates (83.7% in women aged 18–30 years, declining to 40.8% in those over 40 years) even in patients with unfavorable prognostic factors including advanced maternal age, prolonged infertility duration, endometriosis, and previous failed assisted reproductive technology attempts. Independent predictors of successful pregnancy included younger maternal age (18–30 years), recurrent pregnancy loss as the infertility diagnosis, shorter duration of previous infertility (7–12 months), and specific diagnoses such as endometriosis, functional hormonal disorders, male factor, and endometrial disorders. Treatment approaches integrated medical interventions targeting identified pathology—including ovulation induction with clomiphene citrate, letrozole, or gonadotropins; hormonal supplementation with progesterone; cervical mucus enhancement; and surgical procedures including hysteroscopy, laparoscopy, and tubal repair—with 98.1% of patients identified as having at least one infertility cause. The study demonstrates that NaProTechnology restores natural fertility potential through physiologic correction of identified dysfunctions, offering an effective alternative or complement to assisted reproductive technologies in infertile populations.

title: High patient and surgeon satisfaction with ChatGPT-generated responses to real patient questions regarding total knee arthroplasty
journal: Journal of Orthopaedic Surgery and Research
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluated ChatGPT's utility in addressing patient concerns regarding total knee arthroplasty (TKA), demonstrating high satisfaction among both surgeons (4.72/5) and patients (4.13/5) with the AI-generated responses. ChatGPT effectively improved patient communication and education regarding common perioperative concerns, including operation costs, postoperative pain, and procedure details, while reducing patient anxiety through enhanced comprehension of medical information. The responses demonstrated strong professionalism (4.41/5) and accuracy (4.36/5) ratings, though readability presented a challenge, with original responses requiring a 9th-grade reading level that significantly improved when simplified. Notably, surgeon satisfaction was associated with hospital level and surgical experience, with more experienced surgeons rating accuracy higher, suggesting that AI-generated medical communication requires appropriate clinical validation depending on provider expertise. These findings support ChatGPT's potential as an adjunctive tool for perioperative patient communication, particularly when readability is optimized for diverse educational backgrounds.

title: An exploratory single arm study to evaluate the role of an Ayurvedic treatment protocol as a prerequisite for in vitro fertilization in women with diminished ovarian reserve incorporating multi-omics approaches: study protocol
summary:
# Summary

This exploratory study investigates Ayurvedic treatment as a preparatory intervention for in vitro fertilization in women with diminished ovarian reserve (DOR), employing multi-omics analysis to characterize treatment mechanisms. DOR, associated with aging, autoimmune diseases, endometriosis, and pelvic inflammatory disease, presents clinically with irregular menstruation, amenorrhea, and reduced anti-mullerian hormone levels. The Ayurvedic protocol incorporates traditional cleansing therapies (snehapāna, svedana, vamana, virecana, and basti karma) followed by formulations designed to restore ovarian function and reproductive health. The study employs transcriptomics, proteomics, and metabolomics analysis of follicular fluid to identify molecular biomarkers and therapeutic targets, examining genes and proteins involved in oocyte maturation, steroidogenesis, oxidative stress, and cumulus-oocyte signaling. Primary outcomes include changes in serum anti-mullerian hormone and antral follicle count, while multi-omics approaches will elucidate how Ayurvedic interventions improve ovarian reserve and potentially inform conventional fertility treatments.

title: AI-Driven Advances in Women’s Health Diagnostics: Current Applications and Future Directions
summary:
# Summary

Artificial intelligence is transforming women's health diagnostics across multiple clinical domains, with particular advances in obstetrics, gynecology, and reproductive medicine. For endometriosis specifically, microRNA assays such as DotEndo and multi-omics signatures from platforms like Orion and Columbia enable noninvasive diagnosis and prediction of disease subtypes, advancing beyond invasive laparoscopy. Beyond endometriosis, AI-enhanced imaging technologies including 3D ultrasound (Voluson SWIFT) and cervical screening systems improve diagnostic accuracy, while machine learning models integrating cell-free RNA, routine clinical factors, and dedicated algorithms enhance preeclampsia prediction. Digital biomarkers derived from wearables and continuous physiological monitoring—including glucose, heart rate variability, and contraction activity—support fertility assessment and earlier detection of clinical deterioration in maternal-fetal medicine. The field is moving toward integrating multi-omics signals with clinical features to guide diagnosis, prognosis, and therapy selection across gynecologic oncology and complex reproductive conditions, while simultaneously reducing clinician documentation burden through ambient intelligence and natural language processing tools.

title: EndoCompass Project: Artificial Intelligence in Endocrinology
summary: Artificial intelligence is poised to transform endocrine care, with prominent applications already emerging in diabetes management through closed-loop systems that integrate continuous glucose monitoring with automated insulin infusion. However, realizing AI's full potential in endocrinology requires addressing critical challenges in data sharing, including compliance with European regulations (GDPR and the European AI Act) and adoption of innovative paradigms such as federated transient sharing to enable access to massive omic datasets—including DNA/RNA sequencing, epigenomics, and metabolomics—while protecting patient privacy. A major concern is that AI models risk exacerbating healthcare disparities because training datasets systematically underrepresent women, racial minorities, and individuals from lower socioeconomic backgrounds, populations that actually show disparities in endocrine disease prevalence and treatment response. To mitigate bias, the field is advancing fairness-aware algorithms, reweighting and resampling techniques, adversarial debiasing, and personalized AI models that account for demographic and physiological variability. The EndoCompass project, a collaborative initiative between the European Society of Endocrinology and the European Society of Paediatric Endocrinology, emphasizes that trustworthy AI systems require continuous performance monitoring, explainability, and inclusive clinical validation across diverse populations to ensure equitable outcomes across all endocrine disorders.

title: Radiomics-Based Machine Learning for the Detection of Myometrial Invasion in Endometrial Cancer: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
summary: This systematic review and meta-analysis demonstrates that radiomics-based machine learning, particularly deep learning approaches, shows strong potential for noninvasive detection of myometrial invasion in endometrial cancer, a critical parameter for FIGO staging and surgical planning. Deep learning models achieved superior diagnostic performance with sensitivity of 0.81 and specificity of 0.86, outperforming conventional machine learning approaches that yielded sensitivity of 0.77 and specificity of 0.81, though both substantially exceeded the interobserver variability inherent in manual radiologist assessment. MRI-based radiomic features, incorporated in 89% of reviewed studies, enable automated lesion segmentation and feature extraction that preserve data integrity and reduce subjective variability compared to manual approaches. The meta-analysis found that positive machine learning predictions corresponded to a 66% posttest probability of myometrial invasion, while negative predictions indicated a 90% probability of absent invasion, supporting clinical utility. However, significant limitations emerge from the predominance of single-center study designs, reliance on internal rather than external validation, and the "black box" nature of deep learning models, which can be partially addressed through explainable AI techniques such as saliency maps. Future advancement requires multicenter validation studies and larger prospective datasets to establish robust, generalizable artificial intelligence-based diagnostic tools for endometrial cancer assessment.

title: Presenting a conceptual model for decision support systems in infertility: A developmental study
summary:
# Summary

This developmental study presents a conceptual model for clinical decision-support systems (DSSs) to improve infertility diagnosis and management, addressing a condition affecting 8–12% of couples worldwide. Artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies integrated into DSSs enhance diagnostic accuracy, treatment selection, and clinical decision-making while reducing healthcare costs and clinician workload. The proposed system incorporates multiple data categories including demographic information, medical history, laboratory parameters (anti-Müllerian hormone, antral follicle count, gonadotropin levels), sperm analysis, and reproductive history to predict treatment outcomes and optimize IVF cycle management. Key factors identified as essential for accurate decision-support include patient age, body mass index, previous treatment results, and specific conditions such as polycystic ovary syndrome and endometriosis, with expert monitoring and follow-up identified as the most critical component (94% agreement). The study demonstrates that machine learning can identify complex patterns in biomedical data to generate evidence-based recommendations for infertility management, establishing a framework for implementing AI-driven clinical support in reproductive medicine.

title: Successful Robot‐Assisted Resection of a Retroperitoneal Cystic Tumor With Fluorescent Ureteral Stent and Firefly Imaging
summary:
# Summary

This case report demonstrates the clinical application of fluorescence-guided surgery using a fluorescent ureteral catheter combined with the da Vinci Firefly imaging system for safe robot-assisted resection of a retroperitoneal schwannoma that was adherent to the left ureter. The fluorescent ureteral stent enhanced real-time visualization of the ureter during dissection, significantly improving anatomical landmark identification and reducing the risk of iatrogenic ureteral injury—a major complication concern during minimally invasive surgery due to limited tactile feedback. The near-infrared fluorescence imaging technology enabled surgeons to provide enhanced dissection accuracy and prevent direct mechanical and ischemic injury to the ureter while safely separating the benign Schwann cell tumor from adjacent vital structures. The technique also allowed intraoperative assessment of colonic perfusion using Firefly imaging. While the patient experienced a minor postoperative chyle leak that resolved with conservative management and octreotide therapy, fluorescence-guided techniques demonstrate promising potential for improving surgical safety and outcomes in complex retroperitoneal tumor resections near critical anatomical structures.

title: Adolescent Voices in Action—Co-Designing Digital Sexual and Reproductive Health Knowledge Translation Interventions: Community-Based Participatory Action Project
summary:
# Summary

This study employed a community-based participatory action research approach to co-design digital sexual and reproductive health (SRH) interventions with adolescents, addressing critical gaps in accessible health information. Adolescents require comprehensive SRH education to prevent negative health outcomes such as unintended pregnancy and sexually transmitted infections, yet face substantial barriers including stigmatization, provider prejudice, restrictive policies, and lack of culturally safe resources. Digital knowledge translation interventions, particularly website-based platforms with multimedia content like infographics and videos, have demonstrated effectiveness in reducing stigma, improving health outcomes, and increasing sexual health knowledge and service access. The study utilized co-design methodology with adolescent advisory group members participating equally throughout the design-thinking process, identifying six priority SRH resource areas and informing the development of the PEER website through strategies such as gamification and flexible scheduling. Co-designing with adolescents as equal partners enhanced intervention credibility, cultural relevance, and youth-friendliness while incorporating trauma-informed facilitation, inclusive language, and human-centered design principles to ensure the resulting solutions addressed actual adolescent needs and promoted safer spaces for health discussions.

title: Adjuvant therapy guided by molecular classification in endometrial cancer: survival benefit and safety profile
summary: Molecular classification-guided adjuvant therapy significantly improves outcomes in endometrial cancer patients compared to standard treatment approaches. Patients receiving molecularly-tailored therapy demonstrated substantially better disease-free survival (HR=0.52) and overall survival (HR=0.45), alongside higher objective response rates (65.1% versus 43.1%) and improved circulating tumor DNA clearance. Notably, this personalized approach substantially reduced severe adverse events, with grade ≥2 toxicities occurring in only 38.7% of patients versus 63.7% in the control group. Molecular subtyping identified distinct prognostic groups, with POLE-ultramutated tumors exhibiting favorable prognosis while p53-abnormal tumors predicted worse outcomes, enabling risk-stratified treatment planning. Immune therapy was preferentially administered within the molecularly-guided protocol for appropriate patient subsets. These findings establish molecular classification as a valuable predictive tool that optimizes both efficacy and safety of adjuvant endometrial cancer treatment.

title: Global trends in the uptake of specialist diagnostic ultrasound and MRI scans for endometriosis: An international cross‐sectional survey
journal: International Journal of Gynaecology and Obstetrics
summary:
# Summary

This international cross-sectional survey documents a significant paradigm shift in endometriosis diagnosis away from invasive laparoscopy toward non-invasive imaging modalities, with transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) now recognized by major clinical guidelines as primary diagnostic tools. The survey reveals that ultrasound is currently the most widely adopted diagnostic method globally (77.8%), with higher uptake in Asia, South America, and Europe, while MRI is more prevalent in Europe (64.1%). Despite substantial improvements in diagnostic imaging accuracy that reduce unnecessary surgical exposure—previously affecting one-third of laparoscopy patients—significant barriers persist, including inadequate clinician training and expertise, particularly among gynecologists, as well as cost and accessibility limitations. Early diagnosis using non-invasive imaging offers substantial clinical benefits, including reduced diagnostic delays that currently average 6.8 years, improved treatment optimization, enhanced quality of life, and prevention of complex pain syndromes. Emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence are being integrated into ultrasound education to address training gaps and improve diagnostic capability. Implementation of these non-invasive approaches promises substantial economic and health system benefits by eliminating unnecessary procedures while expanding diagnostic accessibility globally.

title: Predictive value of MR imaging IVIM and T2 mapping in malignant transformation of endometriosis
summary: Endometriosis can undergo malignant transformation into endometriosis-associated ovarian cancer (EAOC), primarily manifesting as endometrioid or clear cell carcinoma subtypes, which together account for approximately 10–15% of all ovarian cancers. The molecular basis involves CYP1B1-mediated estrogen overproduction and menstrual blood reflux-induced oxidative stress, both triggering DNA damage and cell proliferation. This study demonstrates that magnetic resonance imaging parameters—specifically IVIM-derived D and D* values and T2 mapping—serve as independent predictors of malignant transformation, with decreased D values reflecting increased cell density and decreased T2 values indicating reduced intracellular water content associated with malignancy. The combined model incorporating all three parameters (D + f + T2) achieved superior diagnostic accuracy (AUC = 0.874) compared to individual parameters, with optimal cutoff values established at D = 1.10 × 10⁻³ mm²/s, f = 37.30%, and T2 = 119.65 ms. These quantitative imaging biomarkers, combined with elevated serum HE4 levels, provide a non-invasive approach to identifying endometriosis at high risk for malignant transformation, potentially improving early detection and clinical management of EAOC.

title: A meta-analysis of the effects of high-intensity interval training on circulatory system-related indicators in sedentary populations
summary: This meta-analysis demonstrates that high-intensity interval training (HIIT) produces significant cardiovascular benefits in sedentary populations by reducing both systolic and diastolic blood pressure, improving vascular function through enhanced flow-mediated dilation and reduced arterial stiffness, and lowering resting heart rate. Beyond hemodynamic improvements, HIIT enhances cardiorespiratory fitness and maximal oxygen uptake while improving metabolic health through upregulation of GLUT-4, enhanced skeletal muscle glucose uptake, and improved insulin sensitivity. The analysis confirms that sedentary behavior contributes to obesity, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease through mechanisms including endothelial dysfunction, arterial stiffness, and elevated blood pressure—pathologies that HIIT effectively reverses. These findings establish HIIT as a time-efficient, evidence-based intervention capable of comprehensively addressing the circulatory and metabolic dysfunction associated with physical inactivity, with benefits including improved vascular elasticity, endothelial function, and lipid metabolism.

title: Cultural Biases and Clinical Variation
summary:
# Summary

Cultural and gender biases significantly contribute to clinical variation in women's diseases, including endometriosis, accounting for 20-30% of the observed variation in diagnostic and treatment approaches. Endometriosis, classified as a women's disease, exhibits substantially higher coefficients of variation compared to generic diseases with established protocols, reflecting inconsistent clinical management across settings. Gender bias manifests in multiple ways, including physicians underestimating female patients' pain severity and prescribing different treatment modalities (painkillers for men versus psychotherapy for women), while cognitive biases are involved in approximately 79% of primary care diagnostic errors. Social stigma and cultural taboos affect both patients' willingness to seek care and physicians' diagnostic decisions, perpetuating disparities in disease recognition and treatment. As healthcare systems increasingly implement AI-based decision-support tools, there is risk that these biases become embedded in algorithms, highlighting the need for standardized evidence-based protocols, auditing systems, and algorithmic fairness checks to reduce unwarranted clinical variation and improve diagnostic accuracy for conditions like endometriosis.

title: Comparison of Transvaginal and Transperineal Ultrasonographic Uterocervical Angle Measurements in Low-Risk Pregnancies at 24–34 Weeks’ Gestation
summary:
# Summary

The uterocervical angle (UCA) measured by ultrasound is a promising parameter for predicting preterm birth, with a wider UCA potentially creating a more direct pathway that increases fetal pressure on the cervix and contributes to premature cervical opening. Combined assessment of cervical length and UCA between 24–34 weeks of gestation significantly improves predictive accuracy for spontaneous preterm birth compared to either measurement alone. This study compared transvaginal ultrasound (TVUS), the current gold standard, with transperineal ultrasound (TPUS), a less invasive alternative that avoids direct cervical compression and preserves natural cervical anatomy. While UCA measurements showed strong positive correlation between the two techniques (r = 0.833), TPUS measurements were consistently wider and cervical length measurements were slightly shorter than TVUS values, likely due to differences in probe positioning and acoustic artifacts. TPUS offers clinical advantages as an alternative imaging modality in patients who find TVUS uncomfortable or in contraindicated scenarios such as placenta previa, though it remains susceptible to acoustic shadowing from pelvic structures. The findings support TPUS as a viable complementary tool for cervical assessment in pregnancy, though further outcome studies are needed to validate its use in preterm birth prediction.

title: PLGA-Based Co-Delivery Nanoformulations: Overview, Strategies, and Recent Advances
summary:
# Summary

Poly(lactic-co-glycolic acid) (PLGA) is an FDA- and EMA-approved biodegradable copolymer that serves as a versatile platform for advanced drug delivery systems, particularly co-delivery nanoformulations capable of simultaneously delivering multiple therapeutic agents. PLGA-based nanoparticles leverage their tunable degradation kinetics, biocompatibility, and ability to encapsulate diverse cargo—including small molecules, proteins, peptides, nucleic acids, and gene therapeutics—to overcome limitations of conventional drug delivery. Co-delivery systems utilizing PLGA achieve synergistic therapeutic effects and overcome multidrug resistance by enabling controlled, targeted release while reducing systemic side effects and improving drug solubility and bioavailability. The degradation profile of PLGA can be precisely controlled through manipulation of the lactide-to-glycolide ratio, molecular weight, and terminal group chemistry, allowing customization of release kinetics for specific therapeutic applications. Multiple fabrication techniques—including single and double emulsion-solvent evaporation, nanoprecipitation, and spray drying—enable scalable production of PLGA nanoparticles with optimized encapsulation efficiency and particle characteristics. Surface functionalization with targeting ligands and biocompatible polymers further enhances tissue specificity and therapeutic potential, positioning PLGA-based co-delivery platforms as promising approaches for advancing combination therapies.

title: Anoscrotal distance and urogenital anomalies in ART-conceived male infants: a retrospective cohort study
summary:
# Summary

This retrospective cohort study examined anogenital distance (ASD), a biomarker of prenatal androgen exposure, in male infants born following assisted reproductive technology (ART) compared to naturally conceived controls. Male infants conceived via frozen-thawed embryo transfer (FET)—whether in natural cycles or hormone replacement therapy cycles—exhibited significantly shorter neonatal ASD (24.6–24.9 mm) compared to naturally conceived infants (26.2 mm), suggesting altered intrauterine hormonal environments during ART procedures. Importantly, shorter ASD correlated with increased risk of hypospadias and cryptorchidism in newborns and impaired spermatogenesis and lower testosterone in adults. The consistency of reduced ASD across both FET protocols (natural cycle and hormone replacement) indicates that factors inherent to assisted reproduction itself, rather than exogenous hormone exposure alone, likely compromise fetal androgen signaling during critical developmental windows. These findings highlight how ART procedures—potentially through alterations in placental steroidogenesis, epigenetic modifications, or embryo culture conditions—may subtly but measurably impact male genital development and future reproductive health.

title: Temperature Mapping During MR‐Guided Cryoablation Using a FLORET UTE Sequence
summary:
# Temperature Mapping During MR-Guided Cryoablation Using FLORET UTE Sequence

This study presents a novel FLORET-UTE (Fermat Loop, Orthogonal to Readout Trajectory–Ultrashort Echo Time) sequence for quantitative three-dimensional temperature mapping during cryoablation procedures, addressing a critical clinical need to visualize the lethal isotherm (≤−20°C) that defines tissue necrosis boundaries. Unlike conventional MR thermometry methods that fail in frozen tissue, the FLORET-UTE approach exploits signal properties from subzero temperatures, enabling temporal resolution below 20 seconds with ±2°C accuracy and substantially outperforming previously reported radial-UTE and STIR-UTE methods. The authors established a mono-exponential calibration model between signal intensity and temperature (R²=0.95) that generalized well across independent datasets, with prospective validation against fiber-optic temperature probes demonstrating root-mean-square errors of 0.93°C–1.73°C and excellent agreement (bias −0.08°C). The spiral-based FLORET trajectory with frequency-constrained gradient design provides efficient k-space sampling while reducing gradient-induced artifacts and enhancing robustness to eddy currents, particularly important for the dynamic frozen tissue environment. These findings demonstrate that FLORET-UTE imaging provides a promising framework for volumetric real-time thermometry guidance during cryoablation, though the method's applicability remains limited to subzero temperatures and may require tissue-specific calibration across different imaging parameters and field strengths.

title: Histopathology Images‐Based Deep Learning Prediction of Histological Types in Endometrial Cancer
summary:
# Summary

Researchers developed EC-AIHIS, a deep convolutional neural network designed to predict aggressive versus nonaggressive histological types in endometrial cancer using histopathology images. The model achieved high diagnostic performance with an AUC of 0.911 (82% sensitivity, 83% specificity) in internal validation and demonstrated robustness across external datasets and images from different scanners. Notably, EC-AIHIS outperformed junior pathologists individually but showed superior utility when used as an assistive tool, improving junior pathologists' accuracy from 78-79.6% to 92.7-94.8%. The model's predictions correlated with molecular subtypes of endometrial cancer and provided prognostic stratification for the p53 abnormal subtype, with histomorphological features including densely packed nuclear regions, complex papillary structures, and high-grade cytologic features driving the predictions. These findings highlight deep learning's potential to standardize histological classification for accurate staging and treatment planning in endometrial cancer, particularly supporting clinical decision-making in distinguishing low-grade from high-grade mimics.

title: Comparative analysis of clinical feature–based machine learning models for predicting myofascial pelvic pain syndrome: a single-center retrospective study
summary: Myofascial pelvic pain syndrome (MPPS) is a chronic pelvic pain condition characterized by muscle stiffness, myofascial trigger points, and vaginal spasms that frequently co-exists with endometriosis and dysmenorrhea. The condition involves dysfunction of pelvic floor muscles including the levator ani, obturator internus, coccygeus, and piriformis, with central sensitization contributing to prolonged pain in most patients. This study applied six machine learning algorithms to clinical features for MPPS prediction, with the random forest algorithm demonstrating superior performance (AUC = 0.956, accuracy = 0.91) compared to XGBoost, LightGBM, and traditional approaches like logistic regression and support vector machines. The random forest model's advantages include improved stability through variance reduction across multiple decision trees and better recall and accuracy metrics than competing algorithms, making it optimal for early diagnosis of MPPS. Early detection is critical since untreated MPPS can progress to urological, gynecological, and colorectal complications. These findings suggest machine learning-based clinical prediction models, particularly random forest algorithms, offer valuable tools for timely MPPS identification in patients presenting with pelvic pain syndromes.

title: Comparing paclitaxel-platinum with ifosfamide-platinum as the front-line chemotherapy for patients with advanced-stage uterine carcinosarcoma
journal: Journal of the Chinese Medical Association : JCMA
summary:
# Summary

Uterine carcinosarcoma (UCS) is a rare but highly lethal malignancy where advanced-stage disease presents with poor prognosis, particularly when associated with deep myometrial invasion or lymphadenopathy. This study compared paclitaxel-platinum (PP) and ifosfamide-platinum (IP) regimens as front-line adjuvant chemotherapy following primary cytoreductive surgery, with PP demonstrating superior efficacy through significantly longer median progression-free survival (23.1 months versus 4.9 months) and median overall survival (28.7 months versus 9.5 months). The PP regimen offered additional clinical advantages including reduced bone marrow suppression and lower overall toxicity compared to IP, while maintaining higher response rates. Complete resection without residual tumor, combined with adjuvant chemotherapy or multimodal chemoradiotherapy, substantially improved survival outcomes and reduced recurrence rates. These findings support paclitaxel-platinum as a preferred first-line chemotherapy option for advanced-stage UCS, though both regimens remain acceptable treatment approaches when optimized surgical cytoreduction is achieved.

title: High-frame-rate ultrasound analysis of gastric peristalsis using the peristaltic analyzer: a pilot feasibility study
summary:
# Summary

This pilot feasibility study demonstrates that the Peristaltic Analyzer (PA), a high-frame-rate ultrasound technology utilizing submicron-scale displacement detection, can effectively visualize and quantify gastric peristalsis in real-time. Gastric peristalsis, driven by rhythmic slow waves from interstitial cells of Cajal and modulated by vagal innervation and smooth muscle contractions, plays a pivotal role in regulating gastric emptying, mixing, and grinding of food. The PA system employs color-coded visualization and vector arrow modes to capture peristaltic wave characteristics including frequency, propagation velocity, amplitude, and direction, with measurements falling within normal physiological ranges in healthy subjects. Beyond functional assessment, the PA technology shows promise for early detection and longitudinal monitoring of gastric motility disorders such as gastroparesis, functional dyspepsia, and postoperative gastric dysmotility, as well as potential applications in distinguishing benign from malignant gastric conditions through gastric wall stiffness assessment. Integration of artificial intelligence for automatic recognition could further enable non-invasive, intelligent diagnosis of functional gastrointestinal disorders, positioning the PA as a superior alternative to traditional assessment methods like barium radiography and electrogastrography.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Routine IVF Practice
summary:
# Summary

Artificial intelligence is transforming routine IVF practice by significantly improving embryo selection precision, treatment personalization, and outcome prediction compared to traditional morphological assessment methods. Traditional embryo evaluation relies on subjective, labor-intensive manual scoring with high inter-observer variability and limited predictive power for clinical outcomes like live birth, whereas AI algorithms analyze embryo images and time-lapse microscopy videos to identify subtle morphological patterns invisible to human observers. Leading AI platforms such as ERICA, iDAScore, LifeWhisperer Viability, and IVY integrate multimodal data including embryo morphokinetics, patient demographics, ovarian stimulation parameters, and endometrial receptivity markers to predict blastocyst formation, implantation potential, chromosomal status, and ovarian response with 10-25% higher accuracy than traditional assessment. While external validation studies demonstrate clinical effectiveness and AI often matches or exceeds embryologist accuracy, performance variability across clinics due to differences in patient populations, laboratory conditions, and imaging equipment remains a significant challenge requiring robust regulatory validation by agencies such as the FDA and EMA. Hybrid decision-making models where AI augments rather than replaces embryologist expertise represent the most immediate clinical value, reducing embryologist workload by 30-50% while improving consistency in embryo selection for elective single-embryo transfer strategies.

title: Development of medical freezing measures in women during the last decade from 2014 to 2023: registry data of the tri-national network FertiPROTEKT
summary:
# Summary

Between 2014 and 2023, fertility preservation procedures in women expanded substantially across the tri-national FertiPROTEKT network, with oocyte cryopreservation and ovarian tissue cryopreservation serving as the primary methods to preserve fertility in cancer patients and individuals with non-malignant conditions. Vitrification emerged as the gold standard cryopreservation technique, significantly improving oocyte and embryo survival rates compared to older slow-freezing methods, while a marked shift in clinical practice favored the less invasive oocyte cryopreservation procedure over ovarian tissue cryopreservation, particularly in private fertility centers where oocyte cycles increased by 197%. Endometriosis and gender dysphoria became increasingly relevant new indications for fertility preservation during the latter half of the decade, with endometriosis-related cryopreservation cases rising from 4 to 99 procedures. Procedural selection was guided by patient age and the gonadotoxic potential of planned cancer treatments, with women aged 31–40 years predominantly undergoing oocyte cryopreservation while younger patients favored ovarian tissue preservation. The distribution of procedures reflected institutional infrastructure and reimbursement policies, with private centers leading oocyte procedures and public university hospitals maintaining dominance in ovarian tissue cryopreservation, establishing FertiPROTEKT and collaborating international networks as essential frameworks for evidence-based fertility counseling and guideline development.

title: Aktuelle Evidenz zum intraoperativen Ultraschall
summary:
# Summary

Intraoperative ultrasound (IOUS) represents a transformative technology across multiple surgical specialties by enabling real-time visualization and precise tissue differentiation during complex procedures. IOUS improves intraoperative orientation and decision-making while significantly reducing complications through accurate identification of anatomical structures, blood vessels, and lesions that may be nonpalpable or occult. Particularly in endometriosis management, IOUS facilitates complete identification and removal of endometrial lesions while enabling parenchyma-sparing approaches. Contrast-enhanced IOUS (CE-IOUS) further enhances diagnostic capability by differentiating benign from malignant tissues with sensitivities of 90–96%, demonstrating superiority over preoperative imaging in detecting additional pathology such as hepatic metastases. Clinical outcomes demonstrate IOUS effectiveness in reducing incomplete resection rates (R1), perioperative stroke risk, operative morbidity, and failed puncture rates across multiple surgical domains including hepatic, vascular, neurological, and breast surgery. This evidence establishes IOUS as a reliable quality control tool and a superior alternative to traditional guidance methods such as palpation and wire marking.

title: Intravasation is not only a complication but also a radiological finding for tubal pathologies in hysterosalpingography
summary:
# Summary

Intravasation during hysterosalpingography—the leakage of contrast media into myometrial vessels and surrounding lymphatic structures—serves as a strong radiological indicator of tubal occlusion, occurring in 16.4% of cases with occlusion compared to only 2.6% overall. Multiple predisposing factors contribute to intravasation, including cavitary surgical procedures, endometrial inflammation, tubal occlusion itself, and specific phases of the menstrual cycle, with the site of intravasation correlating with the location of tubal occlusion in most cases. The odds of tubal occlusion increase approximately 55-fold when intravasation is present, with bilateral tubal occlusion frequently accompanied by bilateral intravasation and approximately 99.8% of patients without occlusion showing no intravasation. Beyond its pathological significance, intravasation demonstrates high specificity and positive predictive value for detecting tubal occlusion, making it a clinically relevant finding rather than merely an imaging artifact. The mechanism involves contrast agent infiltration through the myometrium and parametrium into the ovarian venous system, with drainage patterns following anatomical pathways to the renal circulation. These findings suggest that intravasation observed on hysterosalpingography should be recognized as a valuable diagnostic marker for assessing tubal pathology in infertility evaluation.

title: Circulating hsa‐miR‐29c‐3p and VEGF‐A levels predict the response to FOLFIRI plus aflibercept in elderly metastatic colorectal cancer patients
summary:
# Article Summary

This study identifies circulating hsa-miR-29c-3p and VEGF-A as predictive biomarkers for response to FOLFIRI plus aflibercept in elderly metastatic colorectal cancer patients. Aflibercept is a recombinant fusion protein that blocks VEGF-A, VEGF-B, and placental growth factor to inhibit tumor angiogenesis, a process essential for cancer progression. The researchers found that elevated baseline VEGF-A levels correlated with worse progression-free survival and time-to-treatment failure, whereas higher circulating miR-29c-3p levels—which directly targets and inhibits VEGF-A to suppress angiogenesis—associated with improved outcomes and disease control. Notably, the combination of plasma VEGF-A and miR-29c-3p levels emerged as the strongest predictor of treatment response, better than either biomarker alone, and both independently stratified patients by progression-free survival and time-to-treatment failure. These findings suggest that integrating miR-29c-3p and VEGF-A assessment into clinical practice could enable personalized treatment selection and enhance prognostic accuracy for elderly metastatic colorectal cancer patients receiving anti-angiogenic therapy.

title: Modes of action of traditional Chinese medicine for diabetic infertility: from molecular pathways to clinical evidence
journal: American Journal of Clinical and Experimental Immunology
summary: Diabetes causes infertility through interconnected immunometabolic, oxidative, inflammatory, and endocrine disturbances that impair reproductive function. Traditional Chinese medicine addresses these pathological mechanisms through multiple convergent pathways, including enhancement of insulin sensitivity, inhibition of pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-α, IL-6) and NF-κB signaling, and activation of metabolic regulators such as PI3K/Akt and AMPK. TCM interventions also improve endothelial function, nitric oxide bioavailability, and mitochondrial protection while stabilizing the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis and the gut-microbiota-immune axis. These immunomodulatory effects translate to clinical improvements in spermatogenesis, semen quality, ovulation, endometrial receptivity, and implantation capacity. Overall, TCM shows promise as an adjunctive strategy for managing diabetic infertility and related metabolic reproductive disorders including insulin resistance and polycystic ovary syndrome.

title: First live births after adipose-derived stem cells and platelet-rich plasma intraovarian administration
summary: This case report documents the first documented natural conceptions in women over age 45 following intraovarian administration of adipose-derived stem cells (ADSCs) combined with platelet-rich plasma (PRP). Both adipose-PRP treatment components were involved in improving ovarian reserve, with one 46-year-old patient with diminished ovarian reserve achieving pregnancy after failed conventional fertility treatments, and a 45-year-old with endometriosis—a condition commonly associated with multiple failed IVF attempts—conceiving via frozen embryo transfer following the intervention. The combined adipose-PRP treatment demonstrated efficacy in improving fertility outcomes in women with extremely diminished ovarian reserve, suggesting a potentially promising regenerative medicine approach for this difficult-to-treat population. These findings represent a notable advancement in addressing age-related decline in ovarian function and endometriosis-related infertility, though further research is needed to establish optimal protocols and efficacy in larger patient cohorts.

title: Generating Control Groups for Organ Impairment Studies: A Case‐Study Comparing Statistical and Population Pharmacokinetic‐Based Matching Approaches
summary: This study compared statistical matching versus population pharmacokinetic (PopPK) modeling approaches for generating appropriate control groups in organ impairment studies, using upadacitinib and elagolix as case examples. Both hepatic and renal impairment significantly increase drug exposures, necessitating careful study design to assess safety impacts. Population pharmacokinetic analyses identified weight and sex as significant covariates affecting drug disposition for both compounds, while estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) was not a significant predictor despite renal impairment's known effects on drug exposure. The study demonstrated that statistical matching methods and virtual control populations derived from PopPK modeling yielded different results when evaluating hepatic and renal impairment effects on pharmacokinetics, highlighting the importance of methodological choice in organ impairment study design. These findings provide practical guidance for researchers designing clinical studies in patients with organ dysfunction, particularly relevant for drugs like elagolix used in endometriosis treatment where patient populations may have variable organ function.

title: One-Class Genetic
summary: This study demonstrates the application of one-class genetic algorithm (OGA) optimization to improve infrared spectroscopy-based classification models for endometriosis, COVID-19, and dengue detection. The combined OGA-PRM-OCPLS model substantially outperformed traditional approaches, achieving superior classification performance for endometriosis diagnosis by identifying disease-specific spectroscopic biomarkers at characteristic wavelengths (2371, 1990, 1612, and 1095 nm) associated with lipid and protein structural features. Variable selection via genetic algorithms enhanced classifier sensitivity and selectivity across all three conditions, with the OGA-DD-SIMCA model achieving 100% sensitivity for dengue classification. The spectroscopic signatures revealed distinct biochemical profiles: endometriosis samples showed lipid and amide-related absorbance patterns, while COVID-19 and dengue samples displayed characteristic C-H and nucleic acid-associated bands. These findings establish one-class classifier models as powerful computational tools for identifying clinically relevant samples through automated feature extraction from infrared spectroscopy data. The approach offers promise for developing robust, non-invasive diagnostic methods for endometriosis and other conditions where traditional diagnostic approaches are limited.

title: Multimodal machine learning for staging laparoscopy: a combined image analysis and morphologic tool for the discrimination of peritoneal metastasis
summary: This study developed a novel multimodal machine learning (MML) model combining image analysis and morphologic assessment to distinguish peritoneal metastasis (PM) from benign lesions during staging laparoscopy, achieving superior diagnostic performance compared to existing approaches. The MML model, utilizing a random forest algorithm, attained an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.88 with 72% sensitivity and 78% specificity, outperforming both image-based deep learning models (AUC 0.72) and morphology-only approaches (AUC 0.86), as well as expert surgeons' assessments (AUC 0.78). The analysis identified neovasculature and marked nodularity as the strongest morphologic predictors of malignancy, while flat lesions and absence of marked contours were associated with benign pathology. By integrating visual and morphologic features, the MML framework enhanced both diagnostic accuracy and clinical interpretability, with potential to reduce unnecessary biopsies and serve as an intraoperative decision-support tool. The study's findings demonstrate that multimodal approaches leverage complementary information to improve peritoneal metastasis detection across various gastrointestinal and gynecologic malignancies including gastric, colorectal, ovarian, and pancreatic cancers.

title: Rates and risk factors of oocyte immaturity: toward personalized selection for rescue in vitro maturation
journal: Journal of Assisted Reproduction and Genetics
summary:
# Summary

This study identifies key factors affecting oocyte maturation failure in assisted reproductive technology and develops personalized selection criteria for rescue in vitro maturation (IVM). Oocyte maturation is essential for embryonic developmental competence, involving coordinated nuclear processes including germinal vesicle breakdown and first polar body extrusion. The research demonstrates that oocyte immaturity rates—averaging 19% overall with higher arrest at the germinal vesicle stage—are significantly influenced by modifiable clinical parameters including ovarian stimulation duration, trigger type, and the interval between ovulation trigger and oocyte denudation. Specifically, shorter stimulation protocols (<11 days), GnRH-agonist triggers, and 36–39 hour trigger-to-denudation intervals substantially reduce immaturity rates, while rescue-IVM emerges as an effective therapeutic approach for patients with poor ovarian response or diminished ovarian reserve who exceed a 51% oocyte immaturity threshold. The study indicates that metaphase I oocytes retain reasonably high developmental competence when matured in vitro within 6 hours, establishing evidence-based criteria for identifying candidates most likely to benefit from rescue-IVM intervention.

title: Rectal neuroendocrine tumors: Update
summary:
# Summary

Rectal neuroendocrine tumors (RNETs) are the most commonly detected gastrointestinal NETs and demonstrate more favorable survival outcomes compared to colonic lesions, with male patients showing significantly better survival rates. Clinical risk factors for RNET development include metabolic syndrome, elevated serum triglycerides, glucose, cholesterol, and ferritin levels, as well as positive history of malignancy and family cancer history. Tumor characteristics associated with metastatic potential include size greater than 1 cm, invasion deeper than T1, lymphovascular invasion, and chromogranin A (CgA) expression, which correlates with larger tumors, lymph node metastasis, and shorter disease-free survival. Advanced imaging techniques including 68Ga-labeled somatostatin analogs PET-CT and contrast-enhanced CT are superior for detecting metastases and predicting tumor grade. Laparoscopic surgical approaches demonstrate superior outcomes compared to open surgery for RNETs ≤4 cm, with fewer complications and shorter hospital stays, while endoscopic resection techniques including EMR with ligation or cap, and ESD show high complete resection rates for appropriately selected small tumors.

title: HFSOF: A Hierarchical Feature Selection and Optimization Framework for Ultrasound-Based Diagnosis of Endometrial Lesions
summary:
# Summary

This study presents HFSOF, a hierarchical machine learning framework designed to improve ultrasound-based diagnosis of endometrial lesions by addressing inherent limitations in both conventional imaging and deep learning approaches.
