Endometrial pathologies including fibroids, carcinoma, hyperplasia, and polyps significantly impact menstrual function and fertility, yet ultrasound diagnosis remains highly operator-dependent and subjective despite being the preferred first-line imaging modality. The framework employs Kernel Principal Component Analysis (KPCA) to capture nonlinear feature relationships characteristic of ultrasound data, followed by ensemble-based feature selection using Information Gain, chi-square statistics, and symmetrical uncertainty to eliminate redundancy, and finally Whale Migration Algorithm optimization coupled with Support Vector Machine classification. KPCA proved particularly advantageous for ultrasound analysis because endometrial ultrasound features exhibit strong nonlinearity, complex scattering effects, and speckle noise that linear dimensionality reduction methods like standard PCA fail to preserve. This hierarchical approach addresses critical gaps in deep learning reproducibility and interpretability while reducing sensitivity to limited sample sizes and imaging protocol variability, offering a more stable and reliable strategy for automated endometrial lesion classification.

title: MRI in Chronic Pudendal Neuralgia: Diagnostic Criteria and Associated Pathologies
summary:
# Summary

This study establishes diagnostic criteria for chronic pudendal neuralgia using MRI, with particular emphasis on diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) techniques. Pudendal neuralgia, caused by compression or entrapment of the pudendal nerve within the pelvic cavity, is a severely disabling neuropathic condition affecting the perineal and genital regions, with significantly higher prevalence in women. The research demonstrates that DWI with b-value 600 improves visualization of the pudendal nerve and reveals characteristic hyperintensity abnormalities indicative of neuropathy, overcoming signal interference from adjacent vascular structures that limit conventional T2-weighted imaging. Among 81 patients, MRI identified DWI abnormalities in 42 patients, with 23 of these showing pudendal nerve pathology associated with multiple extra-neural abnormalities including piriformis muscle hypertrophy, ligamentous lesions (sacrotuberous and sacrospinous), pelvic floor muscle ruptures, perineal fibrosis, and vascular abnormalities. The clinical presentation includes burning and stabbing pain worsened by sitting but relieved by standing or lying, with pain relief confirmed by pudendal nerve blocks, establishing a comprehensive imaging protocol for accurate diagnosis of this underrecognized condition.

title: Comparison of Effects of General Versus Spinal Anesthesia on Spermiogram Parameters and Pregnancy Rates After Microscopic Subinguinal Varicocelectomy Surgery: Retrospective Cohort Analysis
summary:
# Summary

Varicocele, an abnormal dilation of testicular veins occurring in approximately 40% of infertile men, causes male infertility by impairing sperm quality through multiple mechanisms including impaired perfusion, testicular hyperthermia, oxidative stress, and hormonal imbalances. Microscopic subinguinal varicocelectomy (MSV) is the preferred surgical treatment, but this study compared whether spinal versus general anesthesia during MSV affects postoperative semen parameters and fertility outcomes. General anesthesia triggers systemic stress responses, oxidative stress, testosterone suppression, and testicular hypoperfusion through vasodilation, whereas spinal anesthesia with bupivacaine minimizes systemic effects while improving testicular blood flow and suppressing surgical stress. Patients receiving spinal anesthesia demonstrated superior outcomes at 24-month follow-up, including greater improvement in sperm motility from three months onward, natural pregnancy achievement in 42.1% versus 32.9% in the general anesthesia group, and faster recovery with reduced pain and morphine consumption. While spinal anesthesia was associated with higher rates of urinary retention and post-dural puncture headache, the substantially improved fertility outcomes support its preferential use during varicocelectomy surgery.

title: The Effect of Fibrin Sealants on Tubal Reanastomosis: A Comprehensive Review of the Literature
summary:
# Summary

Fibrin sealants represent a promising biologic alternative to traditional microsuturing for tubal reanastomosis, the surgical restoration of fallopian tube continuity in patients with tubal factor infertility. Composed of fibrinogen and thrombin that polymerize into a fibrin clot, these sealants facilitate tissue approximation while reducing surgical time and technical complexity compared to suture-intensive techniques. Critical to their effectiveness is the use of intraluminal splints and minimal anchoring stitches, which maintain proper alignment of anastomosed segments and achieve tubal patency rates up to 75% with pregnancy success rates reaching 83% in animal models. Fibrin sealants generate local biologic barriers that significantly reduce peritubal adhesions—a major cause of tubal narrowing, ectopic pregnancy risk, and fertility impairment—while promoting epithelial regeneration and mucociliary function restoration. The anatomic location of anastomosis influences outcomes, with isthmic-isthmic repairs demonstrating superior results compared to ampullary repairs, which are prone to fistulas and stenosis. Overall, fibrin-based techniques with appropriate splinting overcome the foreign body reactions and granuloma formation triggered by traditional microsuturing, offering improved clinical outcomes for tubal reanastomosis.

title: Acupuncture-induced changes in locus coeruleus connectivity and memory improvement in patients with amnestic cognitive impairment
summary:
# Summary

The locus coeruleus (LC), a brainstem region producing norepinephrine and critical for memory encoding, consolidation, and retrieval, is significantly impaired in amnestic mild cognitive impairment (aMCI), characterized by reduced functional connectivity with key brain regions including the inferior parietal lobule, cerebellum, and prefrontal cortex. Acupuncture treatment modulates LC functional connectivity patterns, specifically enhancing connections between the left LC and left inferior parietal lobule and between the right LC and right posterior cerebellum in aMCI patients. These acupuncture-induced increases in locus coeruleus connectivity correlate with improvements in immediate recall performance and overall cognitive function, with verum acupuncture producing greater therapeutic benefits than sham treatment. The findings suggest that acupuncture exerts its cognitive benefits through restoration of disrupted LC circuits, supporting its potential as a non-pharmacological intervention for memory deficits in early cognitive decline.

title: Comparison of the metabolome of follicular fluid in GnRH agonist versus antagonist protocols during in vitro fertilization cycles
summary:
# Summary

This metabolomic comparison study reveals that GnRH agonist and antagonist protocols induce distinct biochemical profiles in follicular fluid during in vitro fertilization, with the agonist protocol demonstrating superior clinical outcomes. The GnRH agonist protocol was associated with increased retrieval of oocytes and a follicular microenvironment significantly enriched in specific fatty acids, particularly 11,14,17-eicosatrienoic acid, homo-γ-linolenic acid, pentadecanoic acid, and eicosapentaenoic acid. These fatty acid biomarkers showed strong positive correlations with the number of high-quality Day 3 embryos and blastocysts, with 14 metabolites overall demonstrating predictive power for embryo quality. Pathway enrichment analysis identified upregulation of unsaturated fatty acid biosynthesis and amino acid metabolism pathways in the agonist protocol, mechanisms potentially underlying its superior embryo development outcomes. While the GnRH antagonist protocol offered practical advantages including reduced ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome risk and shorter treatment duration, the findings establish follicular fluid metabolites as potential biomarkers for oocyte quality and suggest that targeting lipid metabolism may optimize assisted reproductive outcomes.

title: Importance of Preconception Reproductive Genetic Screening in Routine Clinical Care
summary:
# Summary

Preconception reproductive genetic screening (PRGS) has become an increasingly important preventive strategy in reproductive healthcare, enabled by advances in next-generation sequencing that support expanded, population-neutral carrier screening for conditions including cystic fibrosis, fragile X syndrome, spinal muscular atrophy, and hemoglobinopathies. Major professional organizations including ACOG and ACMG now recommend offering carrier screening ideally before conception to identify at-risk couples and facilitate informed reproductive decision-making, with options including in vitro fertilization with preimplantation genetic testing and use of donor gametes. Artificial intelligence tools enhance the scalability and precision of variant interpretation, while integrated genetic counseling remains essential to ensure individuals understand testing limitations and follow nondirective approaches to reproductive autonomy. Emerging approaches such as polygenic risk scores for conditions like endometriosis and digital health technologies show promise for improving accessibility and reducing costs, though their clinical utility remains limited and requires further validation. Key challenges include reducing variants of uncertain significance through strategies like trio-based sequencing, addressing algorithmic bias and data privacy concerns with digital tools, and ensuring equitable access through improved insurance coverage and informed consent practices, as currently significant disparities exist between countries offering subsidized genetic counseling and the limited coverage available in the United States.

title: Rapid Serological Detection of Anti‐GM‐CSF Autoantibodies in Autoimmune Pulmonary Alveolar Proteinosis Using a Novel Immunochromatographic Test
summary:
# Summary

This study describes the development and validation of a novel immunochromatographic test (ICT) for rapid detection of anti-GM-CSF autoantibodies (GMAbs), which are essential biomarkers for diagnosing autoimmune pulmonary alveolar proteinosis (APAP). GMAbs, which are present in high concentrations in the lungs and serum of APAP patients but only in trace amounts in healthy individuals, function by neutralizing the biological activity of GM-CSF. The newly developed ICT demonstrated excellent diagnostic performance with 100% sensitivity and 98.83% specificity across 211 APAP cases and 171 non-APAP lung disease controls, with overall concordance of 98.95% compared to ELISA. The test offers a practical advantage over ELISA by enabling rapid serological diagnosis in clinical settings and facilitating timely implementation of GM-CSF inhalation therapy, though ELISA confirmation is recommended for clinically suspected cases with negative ICT results or when quantitative GMAb assessment is needed to monitor disease progression or treatment response.

title: Investigating the role of artificial intelligence in the diagnosis and prediction of endometriosis using ultrasound images: a systematic review
summary:
# Summary

Endometriosis is a chronic, estrogen-dependent inflammatory disease characterized by ectopic endometrial growth outside the uterus, causing multiple debilitating symptoms including pelvic pain, dysmenorrhea, dyspareunia, menorrhagia, and infertility. Current diagnosis relies on the gold standard of laparoscopic visualization with histologic confirmation, but artificial intelligence applied to transvaginal ultrasound imaging offers significant promise in improving diagnostic accuracy and prediction. Deep learning algorithms, particularly convolutional neural networks and models like ConvNeXt, achieved superior performance with accuracies of 0.89–0.93 and AUC values around 0.90, outperforming traditional machine learning approaches (accuracy 0.80–0.85) such as Support Vector Machines and Random Forest, which nonetheless showed consistent performance with AUC values of 0.75–0.80. Deep learning excelled at image-based classification and automated lesion segmentation, while traditional machine learning algorithms proved more effective for predictive modeling and risk estimation, with techniques like transfer learning and data augmentation enhancing model training. This systematic review underscores that both machine learning and deep learning approaches demonstrate high diagnostic potential for endometriosis, positioning artificial intelligence as a valuable tool for improving clinical detection and patient outcomes.

title: Preliminary clinical study on the synergistic effects of prebiotics and β-hydroxy-β-methylbutyrate in improving muscle function and intestinal barrier function in elderly patients with sarcopenia
summary: This clinical study demonstrates that combined supplementation with β-hydroxy-β-methylbutyrate (HMB) and fructooligosaccharides (a prebiotic) synergistically improves muscle function and intestinal barrier integrity in elderly sarcopenic patients. HMB enhances muscle protein synthesis by activating the mTORC1 signaling pathway while simultaneously inhibiting the ubiquitin-proteasome pathway and mitochondrial apoptosis, thereby increasing skeletal muscle mass and grip strength. Prebiotics promote beneficial bacterial proliferation and enhance intestinal mucosal barrier integrity, while the combination of both agents significantly reduces circulating endotoxin, diamine oxidase, D-lactic acid, and systemic inflammation markers including C-reactive protein and neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio. The study addresses sarcopenia's multifactorial pathogenesis involving gut dysbiosis, chronic inflammation, and nutritional imbalance, which are interconnected through a mechanism where increased intestinal permeability allows endotoxin translocation, triggering inflammatory responses that inhibit muscle protein synthesis and accelerate muscle breakdown. These findings suggest that the synergistic approach targeting both direct muscle anabolism and intestinal barrier restoration may offer superior therapeutic outcomes compared to either intervention alone in managing age-related muscle loss.

title: The diagnostic accuracy of wearable digital technology in detecting fertility window and menstrual cycles: a systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis
summary: This systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis demonstrates that wearable digital technology (WDT) achieves high diagnostic accuracy of 0.88 for detecting fertility windows and menstrual cycles, substantially outperforming traditional methods such as basal body temperature measurement (0.75 accuracy) and calendar estimation. The fertility window is regulated by cyclic hormonal fluctuations including estradiol, progesterone, luteinizing hormone, and follicle-stimulating hormone, with progesterone elevating basal body temperature by 0.3-0.7°C and increasing resting heart rate while reducing heart rate variability during the luteal phase. WDT monitors multiple physiological parameters including heart rate, body temperature, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation, with ring-type devices using multi-physiological parameters and random forest algorithms showing the greatest detection efficiency. Lifestyle factors such as night shift work, psychological pressure, and intensive exercise disrupt hormonal homeostasis and can reduce WDT accuracy, particularly in individuals with irregular menstrual cycles. The technology detects the fertility window with 0.79 sensitivity and 0.80 specificity, comparable to electronic hormone testing systems, enabling accurate identification of the optimal conception window spanning five days before to 24 hours after ovulation. These findings support WDT as a promising tool for fertility tracking and pregnancy optimization while highlighting the importance of menstrual regularity and lifestyle stability in maintaining diagnostic accuracy.

title: Glow Tech in Adult Female Acne: Preliminary Real‐World Experience
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluated a multi-modal "Glow Tech" treatment sequence for adult female acne in patients with underlying hormonal conditions including polycystic ovary syndrome and endometriosis. The protocol combined systemic spironolactone to address androgenetic triggers with sequential topical treatments: carbon peel with 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser to reduce *Cutibacterium acnes* load through keratolytic effects, followed by PDRN (an A2A receptor agonist) to create a favorable anti-inflammatory environment prior to application of a 10% retinoic acid mask. Supportive homecare with hyaluronic acid and glycerin maintained barrier integrity, while adapalene-benzoyl peroxide served as spot treatment for active lesions. Quantitative analysis demonstrated significant efficacy, with mean inflammatory grade-activity scores declining from 2.38 to 0.95, supporting the superior effectiveness of this combined therapeutic approach over monotherapy alone. These preliminary findings suggest that addressing both systemic hormonal drivers and local inflammatory mechanisms through sequential multi-modal intervention offers clinically meaningful benefits for acne in women with endocrine-associated skin disease.

title: A multicenter deep learning framework integrating radiomics and vision transformers for comprehensive ovarian tumor analysis from ultrasound imaging
summary:
# Summary

This multicenter study develops an integrated deep learning framework combining radiomics and Vision Transformers to improve ovarian tumor analysis from ultrasound imaging, addressing the significant diagnostic challenge posed by operator-dependent variability in ultrasound interpretation. The framework leverages complementary analytical approaches: handcrafted radiomic features capturing quantitative descriptors of tumor texture and intensity, combined with deep features extracted from ResNet-50 and Vision Transformer architectures that automatically learn high-level spatial patterns. For segmentation, the UNETR transformer-based model achieved superior performance (96.2% Dice similarity coefficient), while for classification, a combined feature model integrating radiomics with RFE-selected features and TabTransformer demonstrated the highest accuracy across training and validation cohorts. For prognostic prediction, TabTransformer outperformed alternative classifiers with a concordance index of 0.847 for progression-free survival, showing consistent generalization across multiple clinical centers. The proposed framework effectively reduces inter-operator variability and provides interpretable, personalized clinical decision support for comprehensive ovarian tumor characterization, including benign lesions such as endometriomas, cystadenomas, and mature cystic teratomas alongside malignant tumors.

title: Beyond waste: menstrual blood as a powerful diagnostic tool for women’s health
summary:
# Summary

Menstrual blood represents a valuable noninvasive biofluid that reflects the state of the female reproductive system and has emerged as a powerful diagnostic tool for multiple women's health conditions. The latest scientific evidence demonstrates that menstrual blood can be tested for endometriosis, cervical cancer, genital and reproductive tract infections, and metabolic-endocrine imbalances, establishing its diagnostic utility across diverse clinical domains. Recent technological advancements, including digital droplet ELISA, next-generation sequencing, and wearable in-pad biosensors, have significantly enhanced the sensitivity, specificity, and accessibility of menstrual blood-based diagnostics. As a largely unexamined biological specimen, menstrual blood diagnostics hold considerable promise for early disease detection, personalized medicine approaches, and fertility evaluation. These developments represent a paradigm shift in women's health research, transforming what was historically dismissed as biological waste into a breakthrough diagnostic resource with substantial clinical applications.

title: Promoting Preconception Care Through Occupational Health Checkups: Development and Pilot Evaluation of a Digital Tool for Japanese Women
summary: This pilot study evaluated a digital preconception care (PCC) tool delivered through Japanese workplace health checkups, addressing the need for reproductive health awareness given trends of delayed marriage and declining fertility. The digital tool successfully improved reproductive health awareness and demonstrated high perceived usefulness (92.8% overall), with younger women aged 18-26 rating it as particularly useful, while older women aged 35-40 reported greater difficulty understanding the content, suggesting age-related differences in digital literacy. Importantly, the tool was highly accepted across all age groups when assessed for personal health relevance, and it effectively promoted intention to seek medical consultation, indicating that digital PCC content can prompt users to engage with healthcare providers. The findings demonstrate that workplace health checkups represent a practical and feasible platform for delivering PCC messaging, capitalizing on occupational health infrastructure to reach women at critical life stages. However, the study acknowledges that increased awareness and intention to seek consultation do not automatically translate to behavioral change, suggesting that future implementations may require age-tailored design and follow-up strategies to ensure sustained healthcare engagement. Overall, digital PCC tools integrated into workplace settings show promise as a scalable approach to reducing pregnancy-related risks through early identification and management of reproductive health issues.

title: Multimodal Large Language Models for Cystoscopic Image Interpretation and Bladder Lesion Classification: Comparative Study
summary:
# Multimodal Large Language Models for Cystoscopic Image Interpretation

Cystoscopy is the gold standard for bladder lesion diagnosis but suffers from clinically significant limitations, including false-negative rates of 10%-40% and difficulty detecting subtle abnormalities such as carcinoma in situ, which white-light cystoscopy misses in up to one-third of cases. This study evaluated multimodal large language models (MM-LLMs) that integrate visual recognition, contextual reasoning, and language generation to assist with cystoscopic image interpretation across diverse bladder pathologies including tumors, inflammatory changes, endometriosis, and structural abnormalities. OpenAI-o3 demonstrated superior performance compared to competing models, achieving 88.3% lesion detection accuracy with 92% sensitivity and 73.1% specificity, along with high anatomical recognition satisfaction (84.5%) and findings identification (76%), though performance declined substantially for complex tasks like lesion reasoning (52.5%) and final diagnosis (48.2%). The model achieved 73.5% accuracy for biopsy indication decisions and 62.8% for malignancy classification, with in-context learning further improving performance. While open-source, domain-specific models substantially underperformed general-purpose MM-LLMs, the findings indicate that reasoning-optimized models show meaningful assistive potential for generating interpretable cystoscopy analyses and supporting clinical decision-making in bladder cancer diagnosis and surveillance.

title: Knowledge, attitudes, and practices on menopause among female health workers in Vietnam – findings from a cross-sectional study
summary: This cross-sectional study of female health workers in Vietnam demonstrates a strong interconnected relationship between knowledge, attitudes, and practices (KAP) regarding menopause management. Higher educational attainment was significantly associated with better knowledge of menopause, which in turn predicted more positive attitudes and adaptive coping strategies; knowledge was strongly correlated with clinical practice levels. The study identified menopause as causing multiple symptom clusters including vasomotor disturbances (hot flashes and night sweats), sleep problems, mood changes, and urogenital complaints. Importantly, women with limited knowledge or negative attitudes toward menopause were less prepared to manage symptoms effectively, whereas understanding the physiological and emotional changes of menopause led to greater acceptance and adaptation. The findings support a causal knowledge-attitude-practice model, where educational interventions addressing knowledge gaps—particularly regarding hormone replacement therapy—could simultaneously improve health workers' attitudes and clinical practices. Workplace-based educational programs combining up-to-date clinical information with behavioral-change strategies are recommended to enhance menopausal symptom management and patient counseling capacity.

title: Fertility Outcomes in Men with Nonobstructive Azoospermia Due to Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism After Gonadotropin Therapy
summary:
# Summary

Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (HH) is a treatable endocrine cause of non-obstructive azoospermia characterized by low serum testosterone with inappropriately low or normal gonadotropin levels, distinguishing it from primary testicular failure. The standard treatment approach employs gonadotropin replacement therapy, typically initiating with human chorionic gonadotropin to stimulate Leydig cell testosterone production, followed by follicle-stimulating hormone to support Sertoli cell function and spermatogenesis when needed. In this study of 35 men with HH-related azoospermia, gonadotropin therapy induced sperm appearance in the ejaculate in 77% of cases, while the remaining 23% underwent microdissection testicular sperm extraction with successful sperm retrieval in 88% of those cases. Seven couples proceeded to intracytoplasmic sperm injection-assisted in vitro fertilization, achieving clinical pregnancy in 55% of cycles and five live births including two twin pregnancies. These results demonstrate that an integrated approach combining hormonal induction, microsurgical sperm retrieval, and assisted reproduction enables biological fatherhood in men with HH-related azoospermia, with success rates dependent on baseline testicular volume, history of cryptorchidism, and markers of Sertoli cell activity.

title: Advanced artificial intelligence in piRNA and PIWI-like protein research: A systematic review of recurrent neural networks, long short-term memory, and emerging computational techniques
summary:
# Summary

PIWI-interacting RNAs (piRNAs) are small non-coding RNAs that regulate genes and suppress transposable elements, with emerging roles in cancer diagnosis and prognosis. Advanced artificial intelligence models, particularly long short-term memory (LSTM) networks and graph convolutional networks (GCNs), have been systematically evaluated for detecting piRNAs and predicting piRNA-disease associations, with LSTM achieving the highest detection accuracy of 92.3% and sensitivity of 94%. Multiple specialized deep learning architectures—including convolutional neural networks, recurrent neural networks, and hybrid LSTM-GCN models—have demonstrated superior performance compared to traditional machine learning approaches, with several achieving accuracies exceeding 90-98% in piRNA classification and disease association prediction. These computational advances enable piRNA-based cancer diagnostics, including colorectal and breast cancer patient stratification, achieving clinically relevant accuracy levels of 85.7-96.4%. The integration of emerging techniques such as contrastive learning, attention mechanisms, and graph variational autoencoders continues to enhance piRNA detection and analysis, with clear applications toward personalized medicine and improved cancer prognostication through piRNA expression profiling.

title: Oxidized cellulose polymer outperforms traditional hemostasis in preserving ovarian reserve during LESS cystectomy: a prospective comparative study
summary:
# Oxidized Cellulose Polymer Superior for Ovarian Reserve Preservation in Laparoscopic Cystectomy

This prospective comparative study evaluated three hemostatic methods during laparoscopic ovarian cystectomy, demonstrating that oxidized cellulose polymer outperforms traditional bipolar coagulation and suturing in preserving ovarian reserve. While all three methods achieved equivalent hemostatic efficacy, oxidized cellulose polymer minimized postoperative decline in key ovarian reserve markers—anti-Müllerian hormone (AMH), antral follicle count (AFC), and peak systolic velocity (PSV)—with only 5.9% AMH decline compared to 15.4% greater decline with bipolar coagulation. The superior performance reflects the mechanism by which bipolar coagulation induces thermal injury (200–400°C) damaging the follicular vascular network, while suturing causes ischemic changes, inflammatory cascades, direct follicle puncture, and compromised ovarian blood supply through mechanical compression and tissue edema. Oxidized cellulose polymer provides safe hemostasis through compression without these tissue-damaging mechanisms, resulting in shorter operative time, minimal hemoglobin drop (16.5 g/L), and reduced hospital stay. Notably, endometriosis patients showed heightened vulnerability to ovarian reserve compromise with suturing, experiencing significantly greater AMH decline, highlighting the clinical benefit of oxidized cellulose polymer for this population.

title: ESUR female pelvis group approach to cystic female pelvic lesions
summary:
# Summary

This ESUR guidelines article presents a comprehensive imaging approach to cystic female pelvic lesions, emphasizing the complementary roles of ultrasound and MRI in diagnosis. Ultrasound serves as the primary imaging modality for initial evaluation, while MRI provides superior soft tissue characterization and is particularly valuable for complex or equivocal cases and determining the anatomical origin of pelvic masses. The article describes characteristic MRI signal patterns for benign lesions including endometriomas (T1 hyperintensity with T2 shading and dark spot signs), hemorrhagic cysts, corpus luteum cysts (high DWI signal with rim enhancement), and simple cysts like follicles and paraovarian cysts. For complex lesions, the O-RADS MRI risk stratification system is recommended for assessing malignancy risk, with specific signal characteristics distinguishing benign entities (serous/mucinous cystadenomas with smooth septation) from borderline tumors and malignancies (which demonstrate solid tissue with intermediate or suspicious enhancement patterns). The summary emphasizes that MRI features combined with systematic risk assessment enable accurate differentiation of the diverse spectrum of cystic pelvic pathology.

title: Machine learning-based prediction model for chronic post-surgical pelvic pain syndrome: a comprehensive analysis using SHAP interpretability
summary:
# Summary

This study developed a machine learning model to predict treatment response in chronic post-surgical pelvic pain syndrome (CPSPP) patients undergoing magnetic stimulation therapy. The XGBoost algorithm achieved superior predictive performance (AUC = 0.94) compared to other models, with SHAP interpretability analysis revealing that baseline pain severity (VAS score), stimulation frequency (optimal range 50–80 Hz), and stimulation intensity were the most influential predictors of treatment outcomes. CPSPP pathophysiology involves complex interactions between inflammatory, neurological, infectious, musculoskeletal, and psychosomatic factors, leading to sensitization mechanisms that alter pain processing pathways. Following magnetic stimulation therapy, 27.4% of patients achieved significant clinical improvement with mean VAS reduction of 2.05 points, alongside improvements in quality of life (SF-36) and depression scores (PHQ-9), with 77.4% experiencing no adverse events. The model identified a nonlinear interaction between baseline pain levels and stimulation frequency, suggesting that optimal stimulation parameters must be individualized based on initial pain severity. These findings demonstrate the utility of machine learning-based predictive models with explainable AI for personalizing CPSPP treatment strategies and improving clinical outcomes.

title: Evaluating the Feasibility of Using Historical Placebo Control in Osteoarthritis Trials
summary:
# Summary

This study evaluated the feasibility of using historical placebo controls from previous osteoarthritis trials to reduce recruitment burden in contemporary randomized controlled trials. The researchers compared pain reduction outcomes across three trials using the visual analogue scale and applied multiple statistical methods—including propensity score matching, direct covariate adjustment, and nearest-neighbor matching—to align baseline characteristics between historical and contemporary placebo groups. While baseline pain was strongly correlated with pain reduction, and various demographic factors (age, BMI, sex) influenced placebo responses, the matching techniques failed to sufficiently account for systematic differences between trial cohorts, with pain outcomes varying significantly even among placebo patients with similar baseline characteristics. These findings underscore substantial placebo effect variability in osteoarthritis trials and highlight critical selection bias challenges when attempting to replace contemporaneous placebo arms with historical control data. The results suggest that although historical placebo arms could theoretically reduce ethical and practical recruitment barriers, their use requires careful methodological consideration to prevent biased trial conclusions.

title: Confocal Laser Endomicroscopy: Real-Time Histology at the Fingertips: A Comprehensive Review of Current Applications of Endomicroscopy in Barrett Esophagus, Inflammatory Bowel Disease, and Colorectal Lesions
summary: Confocal laser endomicroscopy (CLE) is an innovative diagnostic modality that enables real-time in vivo optical biopsies and histological visualization of living tissue, substantially advancing endoscopic evaluation since its introduction in 2004. The technology exists in three variants—endoscope-based, probe-based, and needle-based CLE—each suited for examining different anatomical regions, from the upper and lower gastrointestinal tract to extraluminal and cystic lesions. CLE has demonstrated high feasibility and diagnostic accuracy across multiple gastrointestinal conditions including Barrett's esophagus, inflammatory bowel disease, colorectal neoplasia, gastric pathology, and pancreatobiliary diseases, with specialized classification systems such as the Mainz Confocal Barrett's Classification achieving diagnostic accuracies exceeding 96%. A major clinical advantage of CLE is its capacity to reduce unnecessary biopsies, resections, and follow-up procedures through optical imaging that provides immediate tissue-level information, thereby improving diagnostic yield and specificity compared to conventional white-light endoscopy. Fluorescent dyes such as acriflavine, cresyl violet, and fluorescein enhance visualization of epithelial structures and facilitate the identification of metaplastic and neoplastic changes in real time, streamlining clinical decision-making during endoscopic procedures.

title: Optimization of Intraoperative Near-Infrared Fluorescence Mapping with Indocyanine Green for Sentinel Lymph Node Detection in Cervical and Endometrial Cancer
summary:
# Summary

Intraoperative near-infrared fluorescence imaging using indocyanine green (ICG) represents a highly sensitive method for real-time sentinel lymph node (SLN) detection in cervical and endometrial cancer, where SLN metastasis serves as a critical adverse prognostic factor. The study optimized ICG-based mapping using the VENERA-green endoscopic system, which excites ICG fluorescence at 800 nm and detects emission in the 830–1000 nm range, achieving 100% SLN detection across all 20 patients with an average detection time of 15 minutes. ICG fluorescence intensity peaks at 10–15 minutes post-injection and exhibits concentration-dependent redshift that follows a logarithmic function, with ICG protein binding in lymph stabilizing optimal optical properties at absorption peak 804 nm and emission 835–855 nm. ICG substantially outperforms alternative tracers, demonstrating bilateral detection rates of 100% compared to 73.5% with methylene blue plus 99mTc, while avoiding ionizing radiation exposure and minimizing tissue autofluorescence contribution. Importantly, ICG-based SLN mapping identified metastases in only 2.8% of detected nodes, with bilateral detection superior in laparotomy (100%) versus laparoscopy (77%), enabling precision-guided surgery that reduces unnecessary lymphadenectomy and its associated complications including lymphedema and lower urinary tract dysfunction.

title: How to Study the Menstrual Cycle as a Continuous Variable: Implementing Phase-Aligned Cycle Time Scaling (PACTS) with the `menstrualcycleR` package
summary:
# Summary

This article presents Phase-Aligned Cycle Time Scaling (PACTS), a computational method implemented in the menstrualcycleR package that addresses critical methodological challenges in menstrual cycle research by standardizing how ovulation timing is aligned across cycles. The major problem solved is that traditional forward and backward counting methods fail to account for follicular phase variability—the primary source of cycle length variation—leading to misalignment of hormone trajectories, particularly estradiol peaks around ovulation. PACTS significantly reduces variance in estradiol and progesterone metabolite measurements and improves alignment of periovulatory hormone surges compared to fixed-day counting approaches, thereby enhancing statistical power and precision in continuous cycle modeling. The method has substantial clinical relevance for hormone-sensitive conditions including premenstrual dysphoric disorder, catamenial epilepsy, and menstrual migraine, where accurate cycle phase alignment is essential for diagnosis and understanding symptom timing. By enabling standardized operationalization of menstrual cycle phases across studies, PACTS improves methodological rigor and reproducibility, laying groundwork for robust meta-analyses and evidence-based clinical guidelines for menstrual cycle-related disorders including endometriosis.

title: Maintaining Bilateral Arterial Catheters to Achieve Delayed Endpoint Embolization in Uterine Artery Embolization and Prostatic Artery Embolization: A Technical Perspective
summary:
# Summary

This technical article describes a delayed endpoint embolization strategy using bilateral femoral arterial catheters for uterine and prostatic artery embolization procedures. The key innovation addresses a critical clinical problem: embolic particles redistribute and restore arterial flow minutes after achieving conventional stasis endpoints, leading to incomplete target tissue necrosis and long-term failure rates approaching 50%. By maintaining bilateral catheter access, clinicians can perform delayed angiographic reassessment and administer "top-up" embolization to achieve more complete infarction. The technique employs trickle embolization (intermittent particle delivery) to promote uniform, denser, and more distal particle deposition compared to rapid bolus injection, which causes heterogeneous infarction. Clinical outcomes demonstrated 97% fibroid infarction rates in uterine artery embolization with 100% complete infarction achieved after top-up procedures, and comparable improvements in prostatic artery embolization with 90% transition zone infarction and meaningful symptom reduction. The bilateral access approach reduced fluoroscopy time and procedure duration while enhancing procedural efficiency through alternating particle delivery from both sides, ultimately providing a physiologically driven and durable embolization strategy.

title: Separation of sperm based on rheotaxis mechanism using a microfluidic device
summary:
# Summary

A rheotaxis-based microfluidic device was developed to select high-quality sperm by exploiting their natural ability to swim upstream against fluid flow, offering a significant improvement over traditional sperm preparation methods used in assisted reproductive technology. The device demonstrated substantially superior outcomes compared to conventional direct swim-up methods, achieving 98.10% progressive motility versus 44.13% in controls, while simultaneously improving sperm morphology, mitochondrial membrane potential, and dramatically reducing DNA fragmentation (DFI of 2.93 versus 21.13 in controls). By mimicking physiological conditions found in the female reproductive tract and minimizing shear stress through optimized channel dimensions and flow velocities, the microfluidic device selectively isolates motile, morphologically normal sperm with intact DNA while expelling immotile cells. This approach circumvents the DNA damage caused by centrifugation in traditional techniques and addresses a key limitation of intracytoplasmic sperm injection, which bypasses natural sperm selection barriers and may compromise embryonic development. The rheotaxis-based selection resulted in improved embryo quality characteristics and has potential to enhance outcomes in fertility treatments by ensuring only the healthiest sperm are selected for reproductive procedures.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Insurer-Sponsored Health Coaching on Hospitalization and Costs in India: A Retrospective Cohort Study
summary:
# Summary

This retrospective cohort study in India evaluated an insurer-sponsored health coaching program targeting patients with chronic conditions including type 2 diabetes, hypertension, obesity, and dyslipidemia. The structured nine-month coaching intervention, which incorporates personalized goal setting, self-monitoring, and multidisciplinary support from nutritionists and physicians, demonstrated significant clinical benefits: participants showed improvements in HbA1c, blood pressure, and cholesterol levels, with greater engagement (measured by coaching call frequency) strongly associated with better symptom resolution. Notably, the program reduced both hospitalization rates and total healthcare claims paid, indicating substantial cost savings for the insurer while improving disease management and quality of life. However, female participants showed lower program uptake and engagement despite reporting more symptoms, suggesting potential barriers to women's participation that warrant attention in program design and delivery strategies.

title: The role of MRI in detection and staging of upper urinary tract cancer: a systematic review of the literature
summary:
# Summary

This systematic review demonstrates that magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) significantly enhances the detection and staging of upper urinary tract tumors (UUTT), with advanced sequences substantially outperforming standard approaches. Dynamic contrast-enhanced (DCE) imaging and diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) achieve high diagnostic sensitivity (92.5% and 93.2%, respectively) for UUTT diagnosis, while standard T1-weighted and T2-weighted sequences alone prove insufficient. The apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) derived from DWI provides valuable prognostic information by correlating with histological grade, metastasis risk, and enabling discrimination between inflammatory lesions and urothelial tumors using an ADC cutoff of 1.5 × 10³ mm²/s. DWI demonstrates particular utility for muscle invasion staging, achieving 81–88% sensitivity and 96–100% specificity for discriminating T3b and T3a tumors, addressing a critical clinical limitation of computed tomography urography which cannot reliably differentiate muscle-invasive from non-infiltrating lesions. These findings underscore the importance of incorporating advanced MRI sequences into diagnostic protocols for UUTT, especially given that approximately two-thirds of patients present with invasive disease carrying substantially worse prognosis than non-muscle-invasive tumors.

title: Bisacodyl micro-enema before MRI of rectal tumors: effects on rectum, image quality and patient acceptance
summary:
# Summary

Bisacodyl micro-enema administration before MRI of rectal tumors enhances image quality through multiple mechanisms: the agent increases submucosal width, induces submucosal edema, and extends luminal fluid distribution throughout the rectum, with fluid reaching tumor level in 90% of cases. These effects significantly reduce susceptibility and motion artifacts on diffusion-weighted imaging in both imaging planes while improving tumor delineation, reader confidence, and inter-reader agreement without causing excessive rectal distension. The micro-enema was well-tolerated by patients overall, with only mild or absent anal discomfort, nausea, and abdominal pain, though 17% experienced self-limiting rectal bleeding at application and 28% reported technical difficulties with enema administration. This preparation method achieves diagnostic accuracy comparable to endorectal ultrasound and represents a reliable approach for staging early rectal cancer, particularly as screening programs detect earlier-stage tumors and organ-saving treatment options expand.

title: The relationship between oocyte maturation and follicle size: A comparative analysis of 2D and 3D ultrasound
journal: Turkish Journal of Obstetrics and Gynecology
summary:
# Summary

This comparative study evaluated two-dimensional (2D) and three-dimensional (3D) ultrasound techniques for predicting oocyte maturation in assisted reproductive technology. Both 2D follicle diameter and 3D follicle volume independently predicted metaphase II (MII) oocyte development, with optimal cutoff values of ≥17.05 mm and ≥1.83 cm³, respectively. However, 3D ultrasound demonstrated greater sensitivity for detecting mature oocytes and higher accuracy in follicular volume measurements, while 2D measurements reduced false positives and remained reliable for routine trigger timing decisions. The study identified that 3D automated software like SonoAVC reduces observer dependence and clinician time, though measurement accuracy can be compromised by closely adjacent follicles or misidentification of vascular structures. Notably, MII oocyte numbers directly correlate with embryo quality, implantation rates, and live birth outcomes, making accurate maturation prediction clinically significant. The findings suggest a complementary approach where 2D measurements guide routine decisions while 3D volume assessment provides additional value in borderline cases and low follicle count scenarios.

title: Ultrasound in Women's Health: Mechanisms, Applications, and Emerging Opportunities
summary:
# Ultrasound in Women's Health: Key Findings Summary

This comprehensive review establishes ultrasound as a versatile therapeutic and diagnostic platform for addressing female-specific health needs, including endometriosis management. Therapeutic ultrasound operates through multiple physical mechanisms—including acoustic streaming, cavitation, and thermal effects—to enable targeted interventions such as neuromodulation, immunomodulation, drug delivery, and thermal ablation of diseased tissue. The review highlights how innovations in soft and conformable transducers, along with miniaturized wearable and implantable devices, expand ultrasound's accessibility for longitudinal and preventive care in women. Point-of-care ultrasound (POCUS) demonstrates diagnostic accuracy comparable to CT or MRI while offering significant advantages including lower cost, reduced time to treatment, and shorter hospital admission lengths, particularly in out-of-hospital emergency settings. Advanced imaging techniques such as shear wave elastography, quantitative ultrasound imaging, and acoustic radiation force impulse imaging enable precise tissue characterization and measurement of elasticity. The review emphasizes ultrasound's particular relevance for low- and middle-income countries, where handheld devices empower mid-level providers to detect obstetric emergencies and gynecologic conditions with sensitivity comparable to specialist-performed procedures.

title: Delivering living medicines with biomaterials
summary:
# Summary

This comprehensive review examines the emerging field of living medicines—genetically engineered cells and microorganisms designed for therapeutic purposes—and the critical role of biomaterials in their delivery. Living medicines leverage sophisticated genetic programming to enable mammalian cells, bacteria, and viruses to perform complex therapeutic functions such as dynamic drug production, immune modulation, and targeted cancer treatment, as exemplified by CAR-T cell therapies and oncolytic viral therapies. However, these living therapeutics face significant obstacles including limited tissue infiltration, immune recognition as foreign entities, and challenges in achieving specific targeting, which biomaterials help address through strategic encapsulation and controlled release mechanisms. Biomaterials ranging from nanoscale carriers enabling intravenous administration to bulk hydrogels supporting long-term cell encapsulation and implantation provide the physical and chemical scaffolding needed to protect living medicines while facilitating nutrient transport and cell-host interactions. Key design considerations include material stiffness, porosity, and degradation kinetics—natural polymers like collagen enable enzymatic degradation while synthetic polymers support hydrolytic breakdown—as demonstrated in successful products like Apligraf and Dermagraft for tissue repair. Despite promising clinical translations, persistent challenges remain including tumor infiltration resistance, off-target toxicity in CAR-T therapies, and the risk of systemic dissemination with microbial therapies, necessitating continued innovation in biomaterial design to optimize living medicine efficacy and safety.

title: Nomogram Prediction Model and Prognostic Comparison of Cervical Clear Cell Carcinoma and Cervical Endometrioid Adenocarcinoma: A SEER Database Study
summary: This SEER database study develops a predictive nomogram model to stratify overall survival risk in two rare non-HPV-associated cervical adenocarcinomas: cervical clear cell carcinoma (CCAC) and cervical endometrioid adenocarcinoma (CEAC). CCAC exhibits significantly more aggressive clinical behavior, presenting with endophytic growth patterns, higher rates of parametrial involvement (40%), pelvic lymph node metastasis (47%), and distant organ metastasis (5.9%), particularly to the lungs, resulting in substantially lower five-year survival (54.2% versus 74.6% for CEAC). While CEAC primarily affects younger and middle-aged women and is associated with endometriosis, CCAC predominantly occurs in older populations and is linked to diethylstilbestrol exposure, with TP53 mutations enriched in HPV-independent tumors. Multivariate analysis identified age, disease stage, metastasis status, primary site surgery, and chemotherapy as independent predictors of overall survival, with surgical treatment improving outcomes while chemotherapy and radiotherapy reduced survival rates. The developed nomogram successfully quantifies prognostic risk in both histotypes, enabling clinicians to personalize treatment strategies and improve patient counseling based on individual risk stratification.

title: Artificial intelligence in obstetrics and gynecology: Evaluating ChatGPT and Google Gemini in answering patient questions
journal: International Journal of Gynaecology and Obstetrics
summary: This study evaluated ChatGPT and Google Gemini's performance in answering obstetric and gynecological patient questions across multiple clinical topics. ChatGPT demonstrated superior overall completeness and accuracy for obstetrical topics, with average scores ranging from 5.5 to 6.0 across prenatal labs, extended carrier screening, gestational diabetes, polycystic ovary syndrome, pelvic inflammatory disease, and mammograms, though it fabricated or provided inaccurate references in approximately 37% of cases. Google Gemini showed comparable or superior performance in gynecologic topics, with particularly strong accuracy in pelvic inflammatory disease (6.0) and cervical smears (5.92). Both AI systems demonstrated clinically relevant capabilities, including superior accuracy compared to gynecologists in diagnosing endometriosis presenting with acute abdominal pain, though neither tool adequately addressed guidelines for conditions like urinary incontinence. A critical limitation identified across both platforms was the lack of emotional intelligence in medical discussions, which is essential for patient understanding and treatment adherence. These findings suggest that while large language models show promise in gynecological education and clinical decision support, they require oversight to prevent reference fabrication and enhanced capabilities to address the emotional and nuanced aspects of patient care.

title: In vivo photoacoustic imaging of swine ureters injected with methylene blue
summary: This study demonstrates that methylene blue serves as an effective photoacoustic contrast agent for visualizing the ureter in vivo, addressing a critical clinical need to prevent iatrogenic ureteral injury during pelvic surgeries. Methylene blue was injected into swine ureters and visualized using photoacoustic imaging with 750-nm laser light, producing robust acoustic signals in both the ureteral adventitia and lumen with optimal image quality 20-30 minutes post-injection. The technology is particularly valuable for complex surgical cases where the ureter is obscured by endometriosis, pelvic malignancies, or scar tissue—situations where 54% of hysterectomies and numerous other procedures risk ureteral damage that goes undetected in 50-70% of cases. Importantly, methylene blue administration combined with laser application produced no measurable renal function impairment, as confirmed by blood urea nitrogen and serum creatinine biomarkers. The methylene-blue-enhanced photoacoustic imaging approach provides a noninvasive intraoperative guidance tool that could significantly reduce the incidence of postoperative complications resulting from unrecognized ureteral injury, such as renal insufficiency and permanent loss of renal function.

title: Vagus Nerve Stimulation by Focused Ultrasound Attenuates Acute Myocardial Ischemia/Reperfusion Injury Predominantly Through Cholinergic Anti-inflammatory Pathway
summary:
# Summary

Focused ultrasound stimulation (FUS) of the vagus nerve provides cardioprotection against myocardial ischemia/reperfusion injury predominantly through activation of the cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway. FUS treatment significantly reduced arrhythmias including premature ventricular contractions, ventricular tachycardia, and ventricular fibrillation, while also decreasing infarct size, myocardial fibrosis, and preserving left ventricular ejection fraction. The mechanism involves FUS-mediated increases in acetylcholine levels, which binds to alpha-7 nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (α7nAChR) on immune cells to suppress pro-inflammatory cytokines including tumor necrosis factor-alpha, interleukins 1 and 6, and other inflammatory mediators. The critical role of the vagal pathway was confirmed by the finding that vagotomy or atropine treatment completely abolished FUS cardioprotection. These findings establish FUS neuromodulation as a noninvasive neuromodulatory technique that counterbalances sympathetic nervous system hyperactivity during reperfusion injury by engaging the vagus nerve-mediated anti-inflammatory response.

title: Adhesive hydrogel delivering ALA prevents the malignant transformation of oral leukoplakia
summary:
# Summary

Oral leukoplakia is a precancerous lesion with significant malignant potential that can progress to oral squamous cell carcinoma. This study developed an adhesive hydrogel (ALA-Gel) incorporating 5-aminolevulinic acid (ALA) to prevent malignant transformation through photodynamic therapy (PDT). The ALA-Gel delivers ALA topically to leukoplakic lesions while avoiding systemic side effects associated with conventional administration routes; upon laser irradiation at 632 nm, ALA is metabolized to protoporphyrin IX, which generates high levels of reactive oxygen species that induce cytotoxicity in dysplastic oral cells while maintaining compatibility with normal keratinocytes and fibroblasts. The hydrogel's wet-adhesion properties, enhanced by polydopamine and chitosan incorporation, enabled prolonged intraoral retention (up to 3 hours in rat models) and sustained ALA release at lesion sites. In a 4-nitroquinolin-1-oxide-induced rat oral leukoplakia model, ALA-Gel-mediated PDT suppressed epithelial cell proliferation by reducing Ki-67 and p53 expression, effectively preventing malignant progression. Preliminary clinical trials (n=30) demonstrated superior therapeutic efficacy (86.6% response rate) compared to conventional therapy, with complete remission achieved in 33.3% of patients, while maintaining normal speech and swallowing function with improved patient comfort.

title: Preoperative chemotherapy in colorectal peritoneal metastatic disease – a real-world observational cohort study
summary: This real-world observational study examined preoperative chemotherapy strategies in colorectal cancer patients with peritoneal metastases, comparing targeted therapy (incorporating bevacizumab or EGFR antibodies) with doublet chemotherapy regimens. Preoperative targeted therapy, particularly VEGF-targeted bevacizumab, substantially improved overall survival outcomes compared to EGFR-targeted approaches, with median survival of 32 months versus 15 months respectively. The superior efficacy of targeted therapy was attributed to higher conversion rates to successful cytoreductive surgery combined with hyperthermic intraperitoneal chemotherapy, increased overall response rates (52% versus 36%), and better outcomes in patients who achieved successful surgical cytoreduction. Notably, irinotecan-based treatment regimens also demonstrated survival advantages, while further disease control through preoperative chemotherapy substantially increased surgical success rates to 75-100%. Although preoperative bevacizumab showed increased postoperative morbidity after surgery, its targeted approach produced a significant positive impact on liver metastases control and overall patient outcomes in the peritoneal metastatic setting.

title: Lights camera action: Randomized control trial evaluating the impact of a preoperative instructional video on patient satisfaction following minimally invasive gynecologic surgery
summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated preoperative video counseling in patients undergoing minimally invasive gynecologic surgery and found that supplemental video instruction significantly improved patient satisfaction, understanding of preoperative instructions, and knowledge of recovery precautions compared to standard care alone. Patients receiving video counseling were 30% more likely to report receiving easy-to-understand instructions and 33% more likely to be counseled on warning signs requiring immediate attention, demonstrating that audiovisual education enhances short-term knowledge retention. While the intervention did not significantly reduce pre-procedural anxiety in the intention-to-treat analysis, audiovisual counseling provided more standardized patient information and improved overall patient experience across multiple measures. These findings are particularly relevant given that preoperative anxiety is associated with poor wound healing, prolonged hospital stays, and suboptimal pain control, making improved patient preparation potentially beneficial for surgical outcomes. The study supports integrating multimedia counseling into standard preoperative protocols for minimally invasive gynecologic procedures, which already offer advantages including reduced hospital stays, decreased opioid use, and increased patient satisfaction.

title: Female fertility preservation: 25 years of progress, expanding indications and future prospects
summary: Over 25 years of advancement in fertility preservation (FP) techniques has dramatically expanded clinical applications beyond cancer patients to include various systemic conditions affecting reproductive health. Three primary cryopreservation approaches—oocyte, embryo, and ovarian tissue preservation—form the foundation of modern FP strategies, with innovations like vitrification significantly improving oocyte cryopreservation outcomes. Emerging techniques including random-start ovarian stimulation protocols with anti-oestrogen agents, robotic surgery, neovascularizing extracellular matrices, and adjuvant pharmacotherapy have enhanced ovarian tissue cryopreservation success rates. Future prospects include in vitro follicle growth, in vitro gametogenesis, in vitro purging of cancer cells, and non-suppressive gonadoprotective pharmacotherapy, which collectively aim to address reproductive ageing and delay menopause. Additionally, GnRH analogues show promise in preventing chemotherapy-induced premature ovarian insufficiency when used alongside cancer treatments. These evolving FP strategies now offer fertility preservation options that extend beyond traditional cancer indications, fundamentally transforming reproductive medicine and clinical practice.

title: 5-Hydroxymethylcytosine profiles in circulating cell-free DNA serve as potential biomarkers for diagnosis and classification of adenomyosis
summary:
# Summary

This study demonstrates that 5-hydroxymethylcytosine (5hmC) profiles in circulating cell-free DNA represent promising non-invasive biomarkers for adenomyosis diagnosis and molecular classification. Researchers identified a 10-marker 5hmC signature with high diagnostic accuracy (sensitivity 0.88, specificity 0.87, AUC 0.91) that outperforms traditional indicators like CA125 and CA199, while also distinguishing between intrinsic and extrinsic adenomyosis subtypes through a paired-tissue-driven biomarker strategy. Mechanistically, the study reveals a 5hmC-APP-HOXD9 regulatory axis implicated in adenomyosis progression, where APP overexpression upregulates HOXD9 to enhance endometrial cell migration and fibrosis—effects suppressible by pharmacological blockade. This liquid biopsy approach addresses a critical clinical need, as adenomyosis currently requires surgical pathology for definitive diagnosis, and timely identification could reduce unnecessary hysterectomy rates in women of childbearing age. The findings suggest plasma 5hmC profiling could enable early, subtype-specific adenomyosis management through minimally invasive testing.

title: Rectus Abdominis Fat Grafting: Outcomes and Safety
journal: Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery Global Open
summary: Rectus abdominis fat grafting (RAFG) is a surgical technique that enhances abdominal muscle contour and definition by injecting purified autologous fat into the rectus abdominis muscles, building upon Coleman's revolutionary fat grafting methodology. In a cohort of 184 patients, RAFG demonstrated a low complication rate of 1.09%, with the most common complications being fat necrosis and surgical-site infection, while no instances of fat embolism were reported. Patient satisfaction improved substantially on the BODY-Q abdomen scale from 12.5 preoperatively to 26.2 postoperatively, indicating significant aesthetic benefit. Fat necrosis results primarily from inadequate vascular supply and ischemia, while infection occurs when pathogenic microorganisms colonize grafted fat and is treated with antibiotics ranging from clindamycin for mild cases to linezolid for methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus infections. Critical safety factors include avoiding active tobacco use, which impairs tissue oxygenation and increases risks of necrosis and infection, as well as employing small-volume anatomically distributed fat grafts guided by ultrasound to identify perforating vessels and minimize complications while achieving long-lasting aesthetic outcomes. The monocyte-to-lymphocyte ratio emerged as a predictor of postoperative fat volume retention, highlighting the role of inflammatory markers in graft viability.

title: Fascial Plane Blocks for Analgesia in Non-Operating Room Anesthesia Settings
summary:
# Summary

Ultrasound-guided fascial plane blocks represent an effective regional anesthesia technique that significantly reduces systemic analgesic requirements and opioid-related side effects in non-operating room settings. By delivering local anesthetics directly to targeted fascial planes, these blocks provide somatic and visceral analgesia while minimizing exposure to systemically administered opioids, which carry substantial risks including respiratory depression, hemodynamic instability, nausea, ileus, urinary retention, and cognitive dysfunction. Specific fascial plane blocks—including SAP, TTMP, PECS, ESP, and fascia iliaca blocks—have demonstrated substantial benefits across diverse procedures, reducing postoperative pain scores, decreasing requirements for sedatives and analgesics, and lowering the incidence of opioid-related complications such as postoperative nausea and vomiting. The analgesic efficacy of these blocks derives from both local effects via nerve blockade of specific sensory and motor nerves and potentially from systemic absorption of local anesthetics, which may contribute anti-inflammatory and antihyperalgesic effects. These findings support fascial plane blocks as a valuable anesthetic strategy that enhances patient comfort and safety while substantially reducing dependence on systemic opioids in procedural settings.

title: Deep Learning Based Computer-Aided Detection of Prostate Cancer Metastases in Bone Scintigraphy: An Experimental Analysis
summary: This study evaluates deep learning approaches for detecting prostate cancer skeletal metastases in bone scintigraphy images, a critical clinical task given the skeleton's role as the most frequent site of metastatic spread. Fourteen convolutional neural network models were tested, with DenseNet121 and its attention-enhanced variant (DenseNet121-attention) demonstrating superior performance, achieving AUC-ROC scores of 96.0±1.2 and 96.3±1.1 respectively. The DenseNet architecture's dense connectivity patterns enhanced gradient flow through the network, while attention mechanisms further improved the models' ability to localize clinically relevant regions within images. Transfer learning-based approaches substantially outperformed conventional machine learning classifiers, with deep learning models like ResNet-101 showing consistent and superior diagnostic accuracy across sensitivity, specificity, and ROC-AUC metrics. Although DenseNet121-attention produced the highest AUC-ROC, it exhibited greater variability in specificity compared to standard DenseNet121, suggesting a trade-off between overall detection performance and diagnostic consistency. These findings demonstrate that modern deep learning architectures, particularly those incorporating residual and dense connectivity patterns with attention mechanisms, significantly enhance the detection of skeletal metastases in prostate cancer patients.

title: Phenol for pilonidal sinus disease: a nationwide survey of practice patterns and safety gaps among surgeons in Türkiye
summary:
# Summary

This survey of Turkish surgeons reveals that while phenol effectively treats pilonidal sinus disease—reducing procedure time, length of stay, and wound complications compared to excisional surgery—substantial safety gaps exist in clinical practice. Patient-level adverse events occur in over 20% of cases, with skin burns being the most common complication, while operators experience eye burns, headache, and respiratory symptoms in 5.3% of cases. Phenol exposure is associated with multiple systemic toxicities including infertility, low birth weight, endometriosis, and myelotoxic effects, yet only 34.8% of respondents reported dedicated ventilation and 62.1% lacked institutional exposure-prevention policies. Critical practice variability was documented: over half of surgeons were uncertain about phenol concentration, only 7.6% measured doses with calibrated syringes, and 16.9% treated asymptomatic disease despite guideline recommendations against it. The survey supports adoption of a safety-first national framework mandating standardized formulations, dose documentation, occupational exposure controls, and protective equipment to align Turkish practice with European guidelines that increasingly restrict phenol use due to toxicity concerns.

title: Effectiveness of virtual reality technology combined with conventional pelvic floor rehabilitation training in postpartum myofascial pelvic pain syndrome: A randomized controlled trial
summary:
# Summary

This randomized controlled trial examined postpartum myofascial pelvic pain syndrome (MPPS), a condition characterized by trigger points, muscle hypertonicity, and impaired relaxation capacity that develops when pregnancy-induced loading on pelvic floor muscles causes chronic muscle tightening, local hypoxia, and acidosis. Virtual reality combined with conventional pelvic floor rehabilitation training significantly improved the relaxation capacity of fast-twitch muscle fibers and reduced recovery time from maximum contraction to resting state, with 80.77% of participants experiencing complete pain resolution. However, virtual reality did not increase pelvic floor muscle thickness, improve pain scores on the Visual Analog Scale, or alter muscle elasticity (Young's modulus) compared to conventional therapy alone, suggesting its benefits are primarily neuromotor and sensory rather than structural. The mechanism appears to involve VR's ability to engage multiple sensory modalities, enhance muscle perception, reduce stress and anxiety, and stimulate sensory receptors to improve motor function without altering muscle architecture. While virtual reality showed effectiveness for acute pain management through improved relaxation capacity, both experimental and control groups achieved significant pain reduction, indicating that adequate intervention with conventional pelvic floor rehabilitation alone is also therapeutically valuable for this postpartum condition.

title: Noninvasive Detection and Thermal Ablation Therapy of Endometriosis Using Silica-Coated Gold Nanorods
summary: Endometriosis, a prevalent gynecological disorder causing chronic pelvic pain and infertility, currently relies on invasive laparoscopic surgery for diagnosis, which carries risks of bleeding and bowel injuries. This study presents a novel diagnostic and therapeutic approach using silica-coated gold nanorods (AuNR@Si(F)-PEG) conjugated with fluorescein isothiocyanate (FITC) for noninvasive endometriosis detection and treatment. The nanoparticles preferentially accumulate in endometriosis lesions via the enhanced permeability and retention effect through leaky blood vessels, enabling high-sensitivity photoacoustic imaging detection while the FITC fluorophore facilitates tissue identification. Under near-infrared laser irradiation, the AuNRs achieve superior photothermal conversion (52.1% efficiency) and light-to-heat conversion, reaching temperatures of 49.5°C and inducing significant cell death in endometrial tissue while maintaining stability throughout treatment. The silica coating critically enhances nanoparticle colloidal and photostability in biological media, preserving both imaging performance and photothermal properties compared to uncoated alternatives. These findings demonstrate that AuNR@Si(F)-PEG nanoparticles represent a promising theranostic platform for noninvasive endometriosis diagnosis via photoacoustic imaging combined with laser-induced thermal ablation therapy, potentially replacing conventional invasive surgical approaches.

title: Deep learning prediction model based on multi-modal transvaginal ultrasound scan images for endometrial cancer
summary: This study developed and validated a deep learning fusion model based on multi-modal transvaginal ultrasound imaging for distinguishing endometrial cancer from benign endometrial diseases, including endometriosis, polyps, adenomyoma, and myomas. The ResNet-18-based fusion model integrating grayscale, color Doppler, and 3D reconstructed ultrasound sequences achieved superior diagnostic accuracy compared to individual imaging modalities and radiologist assessments, with an AUC of 0.92 in external testing and 86% accuracy versus 78% for radiologists (p<0.001). The model demonstrated notably higher sensitivity for cancer detection (91% vs. 30% for radiologists) while maintaining excellent specificity (98%) for benign disease identification, with GRAD-CAM visualization confirming the model focused appropriately on endometrial and myometrial regions. This AI-assisted approach substantially improved radiologist diagnostic accuracy and sensitivity for endometrial cancer detection, potentially reducing reliance on invasive diagnostic procedures like biopsy and hysteroscopy. The findings establish transvaginal ultrasound-based deep learning as an effective clinical tool for endometrial cancer diagnosis, particularly important given the disease's high prevalence in developed countries and dramatically improved prognosis when detected at early FIGO stage I.

title: Dissolving Microneedles Loaded with Lipid Nanocarriers for Vaginal Delivery of Clotrimazole: In Vitro and Ex Vivo Evaluation
summary:
# Summary

This study developed dissolving microneedle arrays loaded with clotrimazole-containing lipid nanocarriers (LNCs) for vaginal treatment of vulvovaginal candidiasis caused by *Candida albicans* and *Candida glabrata*. The LNC-loaded microneedles achieved effective fungal inhibition at a 10-fold lower drug dose compared to commercial clotrimazole cream, while avoiding the systemic toxicity and drug resistance associated with prolonged oral antifungal therapy. The microneedle delivery system successfully penetrates the cervicovaginal mucus barrier that typically impedes drug penetration, with the dissolvable poly(vinyl alcohol) tips enabling direct tissue contact. In vitro and ex vivo evaluations using disk diffusion assays and vaginal explant biofilm models confirmed superior antifungal efficacy of the LNC formulation. A prototype intravaginal ring incorporating multiple microneedle arrays was developed and demonstrated biocompatibility with vaginal epithelial cells, offering a novel local delivery approach that provides sustained, uniform drug release while minimizing adverse effects compared to conventional topical creams requiring daily dosing.

title: Effect of early administration of total enteral feeding with different regimens of nutritional preparations on the growth status of very low birth weight preterm infants
summary:
# Summary

This study compared three early enteral feeding regimens in very low birth weight preterm infants to optimize growth outcomes. Extensively hydrolyzed protein formula (EHF) significantly improved weight gain, growth rate, and protein intake while reducing feeding intolerance complications including vomiting, abdominal distension, and gastric retention, and notably decreased the incidence of necrotizing enterocolitis. In contrast, standard preterm formula (SPF) promoted greater body length and head circumference but was associated with higher diarrhea incidence. Both EHF and SPF regimens shortened hospital stays compared to breast feeding alone, with EHF achieving full enteral nutrition more rapidly. The findings underscore the clinical importance of selecting appropriate nutritional preparations for preterm infants, as early enteral nutrition prevents complications associated with prolonged parenteral nutrition, such as intestinal mucosal atrophy and impaired lactase development, while supporting critical neurological and metabolic development.

title: Ovarian tissue quality assessment and fertility preservation strategies enabled by multi-omics and artificial intelligence: current applications and clinical perspectives
summary:
# Summary

Gonadotoxic chemotherapy and radiotherapy cause profound damage to ovarian tissue through DNA double-strand breaks, oxidative stress, stromal fibrosis, and immune dysregulation, thereby diminishing ovarian reserve and threatening fertility in cancer survivors. Ovarian tissue cryopreservation (OTC) is essential for fertility preservation in these patients, with slow freezing and vitrification methods minimizing ice crystal damage while maintaining follicular activity. Multi-omics approaches—including transcriptomics, proteomics, and metabolomics—enable high-resolution characterization of ovarian tissue by precisely mapping follicular, stromal, immune, and vascular populations and revealing transcriptional states associated with follicle activation, quiescence, and atresia. Artificial intelligence augments these analyses through deep-learning models that enable automated follicle identification and stromal fibrosis quantification, while extracting quantitative imaging biomarkers such as ovarian texture and perfusion gradients that correlate with functional recovery after transplantation. Together, these multi-omics and AI-driven approaches provide molecular characteristics of follicle survival ability, matrix integrity, and cryopreservation-induced damage, enabling predictive modeling to estimate endocrine recovery duration and pregnancy likelihood in prepubertal girls and adolescents undergoing fertility preservation.

title: Transrectal biplane ultrasound-guided transperineal biopsy for rectal and peri-rectal lesions: an 18-year retrospective analysis
journal: Quantitative Imaging in Medicine and Surgery
summary:
# Summary

This 18-year retrospective study evaluates transrectal ultrasound-guided transperineal biopsy (TRUSTPB) as a diagnostic approach for rectal and perirectal lesions, demonstrating superior performance compared to standard endoscopic techniques. TRUSTPB achieved a 96.0% technical success rate with 91.9% diagnostic accuracy and 95.1% sensitivity for malignancy detection, with zero false-positive cases, outperforming endoscopic fine-needle aspiration (FNA) which showed significantly lower detection rates particularly for submucosal or necrotic lesions. Core needle biopsy yielded higher diagnostic accuracy than fine-needle aspiration (P<0.05), particularly for larger lesions greater than 25 mm, with needle selection guided by real-time ultrasound and lesion characteristics such as size and morphology. The technique's real-time guidance capability enabled dynamic needle trajectory adjustment and vascular mapping via Color Doppler imaging to avoid critical structures, while maintaining complication rates comparable to other biopsy approaches.
